  departure_date date [note: 'Actual date the guest checked out of the property (yyyy-MM-dd). May differ from the originally reserved departure date in cases of early departure or extended stay. Used in conjunction with arrival_date to compute Length of Stay (LOS).']
  do_not_disturb_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the guest activated Do Not Disturb (DND) status during the stay (True = DND activated at any point; False = DND not activated). Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS housekeeping module. Used for housekeeping scheduling and guest preference analytics.']
  fb_revenue decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total Food and Beverage (F&B) charges posted to the guest folio during the stay, including restaurant, bar, room service, and minibar charges. Sourced from Oracle Hospitality MICROS POS. Contributes to TRevPAR calculation.']
  gss_score decimal(4,2) [note: 'Overall Guest Satisfaction Score (GSS) for the stay as captured by Medallia post-stay survey, typically on a scale of 1-10 or 1-100 depending on survey instrument. Distinct from NPS; measures overall satisfaction rather than likelihood to recommend. Used for Satisfaction and Loyalty Tracking (SALT) reporting.']
  guest_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the guest stay by traveler category. \'fit\' (Free Independent Traveler) indicates leisure individual; \'corporate\' indicates business traveler under a negotiated account; \'group\' indicates part of a group block; \'crew\' indicates airline or transport crew; \'complimentary\' indicates no-charge stay. Drives segmentation and LTV modeling.']
  los_nights varchar [note: 'Actual number of nights the guest stayed at the property, computed as departure_date minus arrival_date. A core metric for Average Length of Stay (ALOS) analysis, revenue management, and demand forecasting in IDeaS G3 RMS.']
  loyalty_points_earned varchar [note: 'Number of loyalty program points earned by the guest for this stay, based on the applicable earning rate for the rate plan, room type, and member tier. Sourced from Salesforce CRM loyalty management. Used for loyalty program liability accrual and member engagement analytics.']
  loyalty_tier_at_stay varchar [note: 'The guest\'s loyalty program tier level at the time of the stay (e.g., Silver, Gold, Platinum, Diamond). Captured as a snapshot to preserve historical tier context for LTV analysis and personalization, independent of the guest\'s current tier. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: promote to reference product as tiers vary by brand]']
  nps_score varchar [note: 'Net Promoter Score (NPS) provided by the guest for this stay, on a scale of 0-10. Sourced from Medallia post-stay survey. Used for Guest Satisfaction Score (GSS) tracking, service recovery identification, and experience analytics. Null if guest did not respond to survey.']
  num_adults varchar [note: 'Count of adult guests (18 years and older) registered for the stay as recorded in Oracle OPERA PMS. Used for occupancy reporting, F&B revenue forecasting, and capacity planning.']
  num_children varchar [note: 'Count of child guests (under 18 years) registered for the stay as recorded in Oracle OPERA PMS. Used for family segment analytics, amenity planning, and F&B revenue forecasting.']
  original_arrival_date date [note: 'The arrival date as originally reserved at time of booking, before any modifications. Used to measure early arrival or late arrival deviations and to support wash factor analysis in IDeaS G3 RMS.']
  original_departure_date date [note: 'The departure date as originally reserved at time of booking, before any modifications. Used to identify early departure and extended stay patterns for demand forecasting and wash factor analysis in IDeaS G3 RMS.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Primary payment instrument used to settle the guest folio at checkout. Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS cashiering module. Used for payment mix analysis and PCI DSS compliance reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: credit_card|debit_card|cash|direct_bill|loyalty_points|bank_transfer|voucher|cryptocurrency — promote to reference product]']
  qualifying_nights varchar [note: 'Number of nights from this stay that count toward the guest\'s loyalty program tier qualification. May differ from los_nights if the rate plan is non-qualifying (e.g., wholesale, complimentary). Sourced from Salesforce CRM loyalty management.']
  rate_plan_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the rate plan under which the stay was booked (e.g., BAR, LRA, NRR, corporate negotiated, package). Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS and Sabre SynXis CRS. Critical for revenue management analysis and channel contribution reporting.']
  room_revenue decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total room charges posted to the guest folio for the entire stay, excluding taxes, fees, and ancillary charges. Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS cashiering module. Core component of RevPAR and TRevPAR calculations per USALI.']
  service_recovery_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a service recovery action was initiated during or after this stay (True = service recovery case opened; False = no service recovery). Sourced from Medallia or Salesforce CRM case management. Used for Guest Recovery Rate (GRR) calculation and experience analytics.']
  stay_status varchar [note: 'Outcome status of the guest stay upon checkout processing. \'completed\' indicates normal checkout on scheduled departure date; \'early_departure\' indicates guest checked out before scheduled departure; \'extended\' indicates stay was prolonged beyond original departure date; \'no_show\' indicates reservation was not cancelled but guest never arrived; \'cancelled\' indicates reservation was cancelled post-check-in processing.']
  tax_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total tax charges applied to the guest folio for the stay, including occupancy tax, VAT, city tax, and other applicable levies. Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS cashiering. Required for IFRS/GAAP financial reporting and SOX compliance.']
  total_folio_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total amount charged to the guest folio for the entire stay, inclusive of room revenue, F&B revenue, ancillary revenue, and taxes. Represents the gross billing amount settled at checkout. Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS cashiering module.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this stay history record was last modified in the silver layer lakehouse (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Captures post-checkout adjustments such as folio corrections, survey score updates, or loyalty point recalculations. Used for data lineage and audit compliance.']
  vip_code varchar [note: 'VIP designation code assigned to the guest for this stay in Oracle OPERA PMS (e.g., VIP1, VIP2, VVIP, CELEB). Drives special handling, amenity delivery, and management attention protocols. Null if guest has no VIP designation.']

  Note: 'Consolidated historical record of all completed guest stays across all properties and brands. Captures arrival date, departure date, property, room type, room number, rate plan, ADR, total revenue (rooms + F&B + ancillary), LOS, number of guests, booking channel, and stay outcome (completed, early departure, extended). Sourced from OPERA PMS post-checkout records. Foundational for LTV calculation, segmentation, and personalization. Distinct from active reservations managed in the reservation domain.'
}

Table guest.note {
  note_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each guest note or alert record in the system. Primary key for the guest_note data product.']
  appointment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to spa.appointment. Business justification: Spa pre-arrival traces and VIP setup notes are linked to specific appointments in hotel operations — allergy alerts, therapist briefing notes, amenity placement instructions. Guest notes scoped to a s']
  fnb_outlet_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fnb.fnb_outlet. Business justification: F&B-specific guest notes (allergy alerts, VIP amenity instructions, dietary requirements) are operationally routed to specific outlets for pre-service briefing. Direct FK enables outlet managers to fi']
  member_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.member. Business justification: Loyalty-specific service recovery notes and VIP alerts displayed at check-in must be associated with the member account for loyalty service workflows and member communication audit trails. Front desk ']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the guest profile to which this note is attached. Links the note to the unified guest identity record.']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property at which this note was created. Used to determine property-specific vs. chain-wide scope of the note.']
  reservation_booking_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reservation.reservation_booking. Business justification: Pre-arrival trace management and check-in alert display require notes attached directly to a reservation booking. In Opera PMS, traces are reservation-level constructs. The existing stay_history_id on']
  room_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.room. Business justification: PMS traces/notes are frequently room-specific (e.g., noise complaints, maintenance requests, guest incidents in a specific room). Room-level note reporting supports service recovery, housekeeping brie']
  stay_history_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to guest.stay_history. Business justification: A guest note is frequently written in the context of a specific completed or in-progress stay (e.g., service recovery notes, VIP alerts, housekeeping instructions tied to a particular visit). Adding s']
  action_date date [note: 'The specific hotel business date on which the note action or trace is scheduled to be executed (e.g., the date a pre-arrival amenity should be placed). Distinct from expiry_date which governs visibility.']
  assigned_department varchar [note: 'The department responsible for actioning this note. May differ from the authoring department (e.g., Front Desk authors a note assigned to Housekeeping). Drives departmental task routing in OPERA. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: front_desk|housekeeping|food_and_beverage|concierge|security|guest_relations|reservations|engineering|spa — promote to reference product]']
  authoring_department varchar [note: 'The hotel department that created the note (e.g., Front Desk, Housekeeping, Food and Beverage, Concierge, Revenue Management, Security, Guest Relations). Used for departmental accountability and routing of action items. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: front_desk|housekeeping|food_and_beverage|concierge|revenue_management|security|guest_relations|reservations|engineering — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the note record was first created in the system. Provides precise audit trail for note origination.']
  data_retention_category varchar [note: 'Classification governing how long this note record must be retained per data governance policy. Standard follows default retention schedules; extended applies to VIP or legal-relevant notes; legal_hold prevents deletion during litigation; purge_eligible marks records ready for deletion.']
  display_on_checkin boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this note should be automatically surfaced to front desk staff during the guest check-in process. True = display at check-in; False = do not auto-display. Supports pre-arrival briefing and service personalization.']
  display_on_checkout boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this note should be surfaced to front desk staff during the guest check-out process. True = display at check-out; False = do not auto-display. Useful for billing alerts or follow-up actions at departure.']
  display_on_reservation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this note should be surfaced to reservations agents when a new booking is made for this guest. True = display during reservation creation; False = do not auto-display. Supports proactive service planning.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date after which the note is no longer considered active or actionable. Null indicates the note has no expiry and remains permanently active. Used to auto-expire time-bound traces and alerts.']
  gdpr_consent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the guest has provided consent for this type of personal preference data to be stored and processed under GDPR Article 6. True = consent obtained; False = consent not obtained or withdrawn. Critical for EU/EEA guest data compliance.']
  guest_segment varchar [note: 'The guest segment classification at the time the note was created. Free Independent Traveler (FIT), corporate, group, VIP, loyalty elite, or airline crew. Supports segment-specific service delivery and analytics.']
  is_confidential boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the note content is restricted to management-level staff only and should not be visible to all front-line employees. True = management-only visibility; False = visible to all authorized staff.']
  is_vip_alert boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this note is specifically flagged as a VIP protocol alert requiring elevated service standards. True = VIP alert; False = standard note. Drives VIP pre-arrival briefing workflows.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 language code indicating the language in which the note text was authored (e.g., en, fr, de, zh-CN). Supports multilingual property operations and translation workflows.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the note was most recently updated by any staff member. Supports audit trail and change detection in the Silver layer ETL pipeline.']
  note_category varchar [note: 'Secondary classification providing finer-grained grouping within a note type (e.g., Room Preference, Dietary Restriction, Billing Alert, VIP Protocol, Housekeeping Instruction, Security Alert). Sourced from OPERA trace/alert category codes. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: room_preference|dietary|billing|vip_protocol|housekeeping|security|loyalty|accessibility|transport — promote to reference product]']
  note_date date [note: 'The business date on which the note was created, aligned to the hotel operational date (which may differ from the calendar date for overnight shifts). Used for daily operations reporting (DOR) and pre-arrival briefing.']
  note_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the note. Active notes are visible and actionable; resolved notes have been actioned; expired notes have passed their expiry date; cancelled notes were voided by staff; pending notes are awaiting review or approval.']
  note_text varchar [note: 'Full freeform text body of the note or alert as entered by property staff. Contains the detailed narrative, instruction, or observation. Classified as confidential as it may contain sensitive guest behavioral or preference information.']
  note_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the note indicating its business purpose. Preference captures guest likes/dislikes; complaint records a service failure; compliment records positive feedback; alert flags an operational action required; trace is a time-bound task or follow-up; general is unclassified. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: preference|complaint|compliment|alert|trace|general — promote to reference product if additional types are needed]']
  opera_trace_reference varchar [note: 'Source system identifier for the trace or alert record as assigned by Oracle OPERA PMS. Used for reconciliation and lineage tracing back to the operational system of record.']
  priority_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this note is marked as high priority, requiring immediate staff attention or pre-arrival action. True = high priority; False = standard priority.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Graduated priority ranking of the note for staff triage and pre-arrival briefing. Critical notes require immediate escalation; high notes require same-day action; medium notes are addressed during the stay; low notes are informational.']
  recurrence_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this note should automatically recur on every future stay for this guest (True) or is a one-time note (False). Supports persistent preference management for repeat guests.']
  recurrence_pattern varchar [note: 'Defines the frequency at which a recurring note is re-activated. Every_stay applies on each new reservation; annual applies once per calendar year; seasonal applies during defined seasonal periods; one_time applies only once. Null if recurrence_flag is False.']
  resolution_notes varchar [note: 'Freeform text describing the action taken to resolve or address the note. Captures what was done, by whom, and any follow-up required. Classified as confidential as it may contain sensitive guest interaction details.']
  resolved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the note was marked as resolved or actioned by staff. Null if the note has not yet been resolved. Supports service recovery tracking and staff performance analytics.']
  scope varchar [note: 'Defines whether the note is visible only at the originating property (property) or across all properties in the chain (chain). Chain-wide notes support cross-property service continuity for loyalty and VIP guests.']
  source_channel varchar [note: 'The channel or interface through which the note was originally captured (e.g., PMS front desk terminal, CRM, mobile app, web portal, voice call, email, in-person interaction). Supports omnichannel guest profile analytics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pms|crm|mobile_app|web|voice|email|in_person — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this note was ingested into the Silver layer. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and ETL audit. Opera_pms is the primary source; salesforce_crm for CRM-originated notes; medallia for feedback-triggered notes; manual for direct Silver layer entries.']

  Note: 'Freeform and structured notes and alerts attached to guest profiles by property staff. Captures note type (preference, complaint, compliment, alert, trace), note text, authoring department, property scope (property-specific vs. chain-wide), expiry date, and priority flag. Sourced from OPERA PMS Guest Traces and Alerts. Enables cross-property service continuity and pre-arrival briefing. Distinct from formal service recovery cases managed in the experience domain.'
}

Table guest.communication_consent {
  communication_consent_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the communication consent record. Primary key.']
  booking_source_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.booking_source. Business justification: GDPR and CCPA audit trails require recording which booking channel collected the guest\'s communication consent. Regulators and DPOs need to verify consent provenance by channel. consent_source is a pl']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Communication consent is frequently captured via a specific marketing campaign (e.g., a consent-capture or double-opt-in campaign). Linking to the originating campaign enables GDPR accountability repo']
  guest_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the guest profile who provided or withdrew consent. Links to the guest master record in CRM.']
  member_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.member. Business justification: Loyalty program communications (points statements, tier upgrade notifications, promotion emails) require consent records tied directly to the member account for GDPR/CAN-SPAM compliance audits. Loyalt']
  consent_audit_trail varchar [note: 'Structured or semi-structured audit trail capturing the history of consent changes, including who made changes, when, and why. May be JSON or delimited text format.']
  consent_capture_url varchar [note: 'The URL of the web page or application screen where the consent was captured. Helps trace back to the exact consent form version and context.']
  consent_expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which the consent expires and must be re-confirmed. Null for consents without expiration. Some jurisdictions and internal policies require periodic re-consent.']
  consent_granted_date date [note: 'The date on which the guest provided explicit consent for the specified communication type and purpose. Critical for GDPR and CCPA compliance audit trails.']
  consent_granted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when consent was granted, including time zone information. Provides granular audit trail for regulatory compliance.']
  consent_language_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter ISO 639-1 language code indicating the language in which the consent was presented and captured. Required for demonstrating informed consent in the guest native language.']
  consent_method varchar [note: 'The mechanism by which consent was captured. Distinguishes between explicit opt-in (GDPR-compliant), implicit opt-in, pre-checked box (non-compliant in many jurisdictions), verbal consent, written consent, or electronic signature.']
  consent_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes providing additional context about the consent record. May include details about special circumstances, guest requests, or staff observations during consent capture.']
  consent_purpose varchar [note: 'The business purpose for which the communication consent applies. Examples include marketing campaigns, promotional offers, transactional notifications, service updates, loyalty program communications, and guest satisfaction surveys.']
  consent_source varchar [note: 'The channel or touchpoint through which the guest provided or withdrew consent. Examples include web form, mobile app, front desk during check-in, call center, email preference link, CRM manual entry by staff, loyalty program enrollment, or booking engine. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: web_form|mobile_app|front_desk|call_center|email_link|crm_manual_entry|loyalty_enrollment|booking_engine — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  consent_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the guest consent. Indicates whether the guest has opted in, opted out, has a pending consent request, or if consent has expired or been revoked.']
  consent_text_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the consent text or privacy policy that was presented to the guest at the time of consent. Enables audit trail of what terms the guest agreed to.']
  consent_type varchar [note: 'The communication channel or method for which consent is being tracked. Distinguishes between marketing email, transactional email, SMS, push notifications, postal mail, and phone calls.']
  consent_withdrawn_date date [note: 'The date on which the guest withdrew or revoked their consent. Null if consent has not been withdrawn. Required for right-to-withdraw compliance under GDPR Article 7(3).']
  consent_withdrawn_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when consent was withdrawn, including time zone information. Null if consent remains active. Critical for immediate suppression of communications.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this consent record was first created in the data platform. Part of standard audit trail for data lineage and compliance.']
  crm_consent_reference varchar [note: 'The unique consent record identifier from the source CRM system (Salesforce). Used for traceability and reconciliation with the operational system of record.']
  double_opt_in_confirmed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the guest confirmed their consent via double opt-in mechanism (e.g., clicked email confirmation link). Null if double opt-in was not used or not yet confirmed.']
  double_opt_in_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the consent was confirmed through a double opt-in process (e.g., email confirmation link). True if double opt-in was completed, False otherwise. Best practice for email marketing consent.']
  guest_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the guest primary country of residence at the time consent was captured. Used to determine applicable privacy regulations.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'The IP address from which the consent was submitted. Used as part of the audit trail to demonstrate consent authenticity. May be considered PII in some jurisdictions.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'The primary privacy regulation or jurisdiction governing this consent record. Examples include GDPR (EU), CCPA (California), CASL (Canada), LGPD (Brazil), PIPEDA (Canada federal), APPI (Japan), POPIA (South Africa). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: GDPR|CCPA|CASL|LGPD|PIPEDA|APPI|POPIA — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this consent record was last updated in the data platform. Tracks the most recent change for audit and reconciliation purposes.']
  legal_basis varchar [note: 'The lawful basis under GDPR Article 6 for processing the guest personal data for this communication purpose. Options include consent, legitimate interest, contract fulfillment, legal obligation, vital interest, or public task.']
  marketing_cloud_subscriber_key varchar [note: 'The subscriber key from Salesforce Marketing Cloud or equivalent marketing automation platform. Links consent records to email marketing subscriber profiles for suppression list management.']
  profiling_consent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the guest has consented to automated profiling and decision-making for personalized marketing and offers. Required under GDPR Article 22.']
  record_active_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this consent record is currently active and should be used for consent enforcement. False if the record has been superseded or logically deleted.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system from which this consent record originated. Examples include Salesforce CRM, OPERA PMS, mobile app, web portal, call center system, or front desk system.']
  suppression_list_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this guest and communication type combination is currently on the suppression list (opted out or consent withdrawn). True if suppressed, False if active consent.']
  third_party_sharing_consent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the guest has consented to sharing their personal data with third-party partners (e.g., OTA partners, co-branded loyalty programs). True if consented, False otherwise.']
  user_agent varchar [note: 'The browser or application user agent string captured at the time of consent submission. Provides additional context for consent authenticity verification.']

  Note: 'Guest communication consent and privacy preference records tracking opt-in/opt-out status for each communication channel and purpose. Captures consent type (marketing email, SMS, push notification, postal mail, phone), consent status, consent date, withdrawal date, consent source (web, front desk, CRM), jurisdiction (GDPR, CCPA, CASL), and legal basis for processing. Sourced from Salesforce CRM. Mandatory for regulatory compliance with GDPR and CCPA. Distinct from general contact info.'
}

Table guest.identity_document {
  identity_document_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the guest identity document record. Primary key for the identity document entity.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the guest profile who owns this identity document. Links to the guest master record in the guest domain.']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property where this identity document was captured or verified during check-in. Links to property master data.']
  reservation_booking_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the reservation during which this identity document was captured. May be null for documents captured outside of a specific reservation context.']
  capture_channel varchar [note: 'Channel or touchpoint through which the identity document was captured. Values: front_desk (traditional check-in), mobile_checkin (mobile app), kiosk (self-service kiosk), online_precheckin (web portal), concierge (concierge desk).']
  capture_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the identity document information was first captured into the system. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Represents the business event time of document capture.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this document record meets all regulatory compliance requirements for the jurisdiction. True if compliant, False if additional documentation or verification is required.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this identity document record was first created in the system. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Audit trail field for record creation.']
  data_retention_category varchar [note: 'Data retention classification for this identity document record. Determines how long the record must be retained per regulatory and business requirements. Values: standard (normal retention period), extended (longer retention for compliance), legal_hold (litigation hold), purge_eligible (eligible for deletion).']
  date_of_birth date [note: 'Guest date of birth as recorded on the identity document. Format: yyyy-MM-dd. Used for age verification and compliance.']
  document_number varchar [note: 'The unique identification number printed on the identity document. This is the primary identifier on the document itself (e.g., passport number, national ID number, drivers license number).']
  document_scan_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or file path to the scanned image or digital copy of the identity document stored in the document management system. Used for audit and compliance verification.']
  document_scan_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the identity document was scanned or digitally captured. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  document_type varchar [note: 'Type of identity document presented by the guest. Standard types include passport, national ID card, drivers license, visa, residence permit, and military ID.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the identity document expires and is no longer valid for identification purposes. Format: yyyy-MM-dd. Null for documents with no expiration.']
  full_name_on_document varchar [note: 'Complete legal name as printed on the identity document. Used for verification and compliance purposes to match against guest profile name.']
  gdpr_consent_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the guest has provided explicit consent for the collection and processing of their identity document data under GDPR. True if consent obtained, False otherwise.']
  gender varchar [note: 'Gender marker as recorded on the identity document. Values: M (Male), F (Female), X (Unspecified/Non-binary), U (Unknown/Not stated).']
  government_report_status varchar [note: 'Status of government reporting submission for this identity document. Values: not_required (no reporting obligation), pending (awaiting submission), submitted (sent to authorities), confirmed (acknowledged by authorities), failed (submission error).']
  government_report_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the identity document information was reported to government authorities. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Null if not yet reported or not required.']
  government_reporting_required boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this identity document record must be reported to government authorities per local regulations (e.g., police registration, immigration reporting). True if reporting is required, False otherwise.']
  is_primary_document boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this is the primary identity document on file for the guest. True if this is the main document used for identification, False otherwise.']
  issue_date date [note: 'Date on which the identity document was issued by the issuing authority. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  issuing_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the government agency or authority that issued the identity document (e.g., U.S. Department of State, UK Home Office, Ministry of Interior).']
  issuing_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country or jurisdiction that issued the identity document.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this identity document record was last updated or modified. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Audit trail field for record modification.']
  nationality_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the guests nationality as stated on the identity document.']
  place_of_birth varchar [note: 'City and country of birth as recorded on the identity document. Used for enhanced identity verification in some jurisdictions.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to verify the authenticity of the identity document. Values: manual (staff visual inspection), automated (OCR/document scanner), biometric (facial recognition or fingerprint match), third_party (external verification service).']
  verification_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes recorded by staff during the verification process. May include observations about document condition, discrepancies, or reasons for rejection.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Current verification status of the identity document. Indicates whether the document has been validated by staff or automated systems. Values: pending (awaiting verification), verified (validated and accepted), rejected (invalid or fraudulent), expired (document past expiry date), flagged (requires additional review).']
  verification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the identity document verification was completed. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  visa_entry_type varchar [note: 'Entry type permitted by the visa. Values: single (one entry only), multiple (multiple entries allowed), transit (transit visa). Null for non-visa documents.']
  visa_type varchar [note: 'Type or category of visa if the document is a visa (e.g., tourist, business, student, work). Null for non-visa documents.']

  Note: 'Guest identity document records capturing passport, national ID, driver\'s license, and visa details required for check-in compliance and international travel regulations. Captures document type, document number, issuing country, issue date, expiry date, scan/image reference, and verification status. Sourced from OPERA PMS check-in capture. Required for AML compliance, international property operations, and government reporting in applicable jurisdictions.'
}

Table guest.segment {
  segment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the guest market segment record. Primary key for both segment master definitions and individual guest-to-segment assignment records.']
  cancellation_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reservation.cancellation_policy. Business justification: Segment-based cancellation policy configuration: revenue management assigns specific cancellation policies to guest segments (e.g., corporate flexible, OTA non-refundable). The existing cancellation_p']
  hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.hierarchy. Business justification: Segment scope alignment to organizational hierarchy (brand, region, cluster) is essential for chain-level revenue management rollup reporting. A segment defined at a brand or regional hierarchy node a']
  market_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to revenue.market_segment. Business justification: Rate strategy and displacement analysis: guest segments must map to revenue market segments so revenue managers can apply correct pricing strategies, yield rules, and LRA eligibility. This mapping dri']
  marketing_guest_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.guest_segment. Business justification: Properties cross-reference booking market segments (transient, group, contract) with marketing behavioral segments (high-value leisure, business road warrior) for campaign eligibility rules, personali']
  parent_segment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent segment in the hierarchy for roll-up aggregation. Null for top-level segments.']
  primary_superseded_by_assignment_segment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the segment assignment record that supersedes this one. Used for tracking segment migration history and maintaining assignment lineage.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the individual guest profile for guest-to-segment assignment records. Null for segment master definition records.']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.property. Business justification: Property-level guest segmentation is a core revenue management process: segments defined at a specific property (vs. chain-wide) drive rate strategy, displacement analysis, and RevPAR reporting. Reven']
  tier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.tier. Business justification: Guest segments are mapped to loyalty tier eligibility rules for rate strategy and yield management. Revenue management systems join segment to tier to apply correct points multipliers, determine LRA e']
  adr_index_vs_property decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average Daily Rate index for this segment relative to property-wide ADR. 1.0 = at property average, >1.0 = premium segment, <1.0 = discount segment. Used for segment mix optimization.']
  advance_booking_window_days varchar [note: 'Typical advance booking window in days for this segment. Used for demand forecasting and pickup analysis. Transient segments typically 0-30 days, Group segments 90-365 days.']
  ancillary_revenue_per_stay decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average ancillary revenue (F&B, spa, parking, resort fees, etc.) per stay for this segment. Used for total revenue forecasting and TRevPAR optimization.']
  assignment_confidence_score decimal(4,3) [note: 'Confidence score (0.000 to 1.000) for ML-inferred segment assignments. Higher scores indicate greater model certainty. Null for rule-based and manual assignments.']
  assignment_date date [note: 'Date when the guest was assigned to this segment. Used for tracking segment migration and guest lifecycle analysis.']
  assignment_effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this segment assignment is effective for the guest. Used for time-bound segment membership (e.g., corporate contract validity period).']
  assignment_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when this segment assignment expires for the guest. Null for indefinite assignments. Used for contract-based and time-limited segment memberships.']
  assignment_method varchar [note: 'Method used to assign the guest to this segment. Rule-based uses business rules (e.g., booking channel, rate code), ML-inferred uses machine learning models for predictive segmentation, Manual-override represents analyst or sales manager assignment, Reservation-based inherits from reservation market segment code.']
  assignment_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes explaining the rationale for manual segment assignments or overrides. Used for audit trail and knowledge transfer.']
  assignment_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the business reason for this segment assignment (e.g., CORP_CONTRACT, BOOKING_CHANNEL, STAY_PATTERN, SALES_OVERRIDE, LTV_TIER). Used for assignment audit and quality control.']
  average_los_days decimal(4,1) [note: 'Historical average Length of Stay in days for guests in this segment. Used for demand forecasting and inventory optimization.']
  commission_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether bookings in this segment are eligible for travel agent or OTA commission payments.']
  commission_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Standard commission percentage paid to intermediaries for bookings in this segment. Null if commission_eligible is False.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this segment record was first created in the lakehouse. Used for data lineage and audit trail.']
  displacement_analysis_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether group bookings in this segment should undergo displacement analysis to compare group revenue against potential transient revenue. Used for group evaluation and acceptance decisions.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this segment definition was retired or superseded. Null for currently active segments.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this segment definition became effective. Used for historical segment analysis and time-travel queries.']
  fb_attachment_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of guests in this segment who purchase F&B services during their stay. Used for TRevPAR analysis and ancillary revenue forecasting.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Numeric level in the segment hierarchy for roll-up reporting. Level 1 is top-level (e.g., Transient), Level 2 is sub-segment (e.g., Qualified Corporate), Level 3 is micro-segment (e.g., Tech Industry Corporate).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this segment record was last updated. Used for change tracking and incremental processing.']
  loyalty_points_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether stays booked under this segment earn loyalty program points and elite night credits.']
  loyalty_points_multiplier decimal(4,2) [note: 'Multiplier applied to base points earning for this segment. Standard is 1.0, promotional segments may have higher multipliers (e.g., 1.5x, 2.0x).']
  lra_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this segment has Last Room Availability commitment, meaning inventory is available at the contracted rate even when the property is sold out. Typically True for top-tier corporate contracts and loyalty elite members.']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or system identifier of the user or process that last modified this record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  rate_strategy_type varchar [note: 'Pricing strategy applicable to this segment. Dynamic uses RMS (Revenue Management System) optimization, Fixed uses BAR (Best Available Rate) or published rates, Negotiated uses contracted rates, Promotional uses discount codes, Opaque uses non-branded OTA channels.']
  revpar_contribution_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Historical percentage contribution of this segment to total property RevPAR. Sum across all segments equals 100%. Used for strategic segment mix planning.']
  segment_category varchar [note: 'Broader business category for revenue and demand analysis. Used for RevPAR (Revenue Per Available Room) decomposition and TRevPAR (Total Revenue Per Available Room) attribution.']
  segment_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code identifying the market segment (e.g., TRANS, GRP, CORP, WHSL, COMP, CREW). Sourced from OPERA PMS Market Segment codes and IDeaS G3 RMS segment taxonomy.']
  segment_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the market segment (e.g., Transient, Group, Corporate Negotiated, Wholesale, Complimentary, Airline Crew).']
  segment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the segment definition. Active segments are available for assignment and reporting, Inactive segments are hidden from selection but retained for historical analysis, Deprecated segments are being phased out, Pending segments are awaiting approval.']
  segment_type varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the segment type. Transient represents FIT (Free Independent Traveler) guests, Group represents MICE (Meetings Incentives Conferences Exhibitions) and leisure groups, Contract represents negotiated corporate accounts, Wholesale represents OTA (Online Travel Agency) and tour operator bookings, Complimentary represents comp stays and employee rates, Crew represents airline and travel industry staff.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of record that originated this segment definition or assignment. OPERA for PMS market segments, IDEAS for RMS segments, SALESFORCE for CRM segmentation, MANUAL for analyst-created segments.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of this segment in the source system. Used for data reconciliation and lineage tracking.']
  usali_mapping_code varchar [note: 'Standard USALI account code for financial reporting and GOP (Gross Operating Profit) attribution. Ensures consistent revenue classification across properties.']
  yield_management_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this segment is subject to dynamic yield optimization and inventory controls by the RMS. True for transient and qualified segments, False for contracted and complimentary segments.']

  Note: 'Guest market segmentation master definitions and individual guest-to-segment assignment records. The master component defines business segments (transient, group, contract, wholesale, complimentary, crew) with segment code, name, hierarchy level, USALI mapping, and applicable rate strategy. The assignment component tracks individual guest-to-segment associations including assignment date, assignment method (rule-based, ML-inferred, manual override), confidence score, validity period, and superseding assignment reference. Sourced from OPERA PMS Market Segment codes, IDeaS G3 RMS, and Salesforce CRM segmentation. Used for RevPAR analysis, demand forecasting, targeted marketing, rate fence enforcement, and LTV-based prioritization. Note: this product contains both reference definitions and transactional assignments — the master definitions change infrequently while assignments are high-volume.'
}

Table guest.relationship {
  relationship_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the guest-to-guest relationship record. Primary key.']
  corporate_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the corporate account associated with this relationship. Used for corporate travel program management and booker-to-traveler attribution.']
  relationship_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary guest in the relationship. The guest from whom the relationship originates.']
  relationship_related_guest_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the related guest in the relationship. The guest to whom the primary guest is related.']
  billing_authority_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the related guest has authority to handle billing and payment matters for the primary guest.']
  booking_authority_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the related guest has authority to make bookings on behalf of the primary guest. Used for assistant-mediated booking and corporate travel coordinator scenarios.']
  consent_date date [note: 'Date when consent was obtained from both guests for this relationship record. Required for data privacy compliance.']
  consent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether both guests have provided consent for this relationship to be recorded and used for service personalization. Required for General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) and California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this relationship record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  data_retention_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when this relationship record is scheduled for deletion or archival per data retention policies. Required for GDPR right to erasure compliance.']
  direction varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the relationship is bidirectional (mutual, both guests recognize the relationship) or unidirectional (one-way, only the primary guest recognizes the relationship).']
  emergency_contact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the related guest is designated as an emergency contact for the primary guest.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when the relationship ended or is scheduled to end. Null for ongoing relationships.']
  gdpr_lawful_basis varchar [note: 'The lawful basis under GDPR Article 6 for processing this relationship data. Typically consent or legitimate interest for guest relationship management.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this relationship record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes providing additional context about the relationship. Used for service personalization and special handling instructions.']
  primary_contact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the related guest is the primary contact for the primary guest. Used for assistant-mediated booking attribution and corporate travel coordinator visibility.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority ranking of this relationship relative to other relationships for the same primary guest. Lower numbers indicate higher priority. Used when multiple relationships exist and one must be selected (e.g., primary emergency contact).']
  relationship_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the relationship. Active relationships are used for service personalization and household-level aggregation.']
  relationship_type varchar [note: 'Type of relationship between the two guests. Defines the nature of the association (e.g., spouse/partner, parent/child, executive assistant, corporate booker-to-traveler, travel companion).']
  shared_preference_inheritance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether preferences should be inherited or shared between the two guests. Enables family recognition programs (e.g., greeting children by name based on parent preferences).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of record from which this relationship was sourced. Typically Salesforce CRM relationship objects or front desk manual entry in Oracle OPERA PMS.']
  source_system_record_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of this relationship record in the source system. Used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when the relationship became effective. Used for historical tracking and lifecycle management.']
  verified_date date [note: 'Date when the relationship was verified. Null if not yet verified.']
  verified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the relationship has been verified by staff or through a validation process. Unverified relationships may be self-reported or imported without confirmation.']

  Note: 'Guest-to-guest relationship records capturing known associations between guest profiles for household-level service personalization and corporate travel program management. Captures relationship type (spouse/partner, parent/child, executive assistant, corporate booker-to-traveler, travel companion), relationship direction (bidirectional or unidirectional), start date, end date, primary contact designation, and shared preference inheritance flag. Enables family recognition programs (e.g., greeting children by name), household-level LTV aggregation, assistant-mediated booking attribution, and corporate travel coordinator visibility. Sourced from Salesforce CRM relationship objects and front desk manual entry.'
}

Table reservation.reservation_booking {
  reservation_booking_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the reservation booking record. Primary key for the reservation_booking product. System-generated surrogate key used across all downstream systems and analytics.']
  booking_source_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the booking source channel through which this reservation was created (direct website, OTA, GDS, phone, walk-in, corporate portal, etc.). Used for channel attribution and commission calculation.']
  campaign_offer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign_offer. Business justification: Reservations made under promotional offers (advance purchase, member exclusive) must be directly linked to the offer for offer performance reporting, yield management, and compliance with offer terms.']
  cancellation_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reservation.cancellation_policy. Business justification: reservation_booking currently stores cancellation_policy_code as a denormalized STRING. The cancellation_policy table is the authoritative reference master for cancellation terms in this domain. Addin']
  corporate_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the corporate account if this reservation is booked under a negotiated corporate rate agreement. Used for corporate billing and rate compliance auditing.']
  market_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the market segment classification for this reservation (transient, group, corporate, leisure, government, etc.). Used for revenue management segmentation and forecasting.']
  member_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.member. Business justification: Loyalty points posting, tier-based rate eligibility, and VIP service delivery at booking all require a direct FK to the loyalty member record. loyalty_member_number is a denormalized surrogate; replac']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the guest master record who is the primary guest on this reservation. Links to guest profile containing contact information, preferences, and loyalty status.']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the property where this reservation is booked. Links to property master containing location, brand, segment, and operational details.']
  reservation_group_block_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the group block if this reservation is part of a group booking (conference, wedding, corporate event, etc.). Null for individual FIT reservations.']
  reservation_rate_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reservation.reservation_rate_plan. Business justification: A reservation booking is made under a specific reservation-domain rate plan (the bookable rate plan catalog presented to guests and agents). reservation_booking already has a cross-domain revenue_rate']
  room_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.room_type. Business justification: Booking-to-room-type FK is essential for availability checking, rate loading, upgrade eligibility, and RevPAR reporting by room type. Every PMS links a booking to the requested room type as a proper F']
  strategy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to revenue.strategy. Business justification: Bookings are made under an active revenue strategy (compression, shoulder-night push, etc.). Linking booking to strategy enables strategy effectiveness reporting — did bookings materialize at target A']
  tier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.tier. Business justification: Tier at time of booking drives upgrade eligibility, benefit entitlement, and points multiplier application. Revenue management and front-desk systems require the member\'s tier captured at booking to a']
  travel_agent_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reservation.travel_agent. Business justification: Reservation_booking currently references travel agent via travel_agent_iata_number (string business key). Normalizing to FK using travel_agent_id allows joining to get full agent details (agency_name,']
  accessibility_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the guest has requested an ADA-compliant accessible room. Used for room assignment and compliance with Americans with Disabilities Act requirements.']
  arrival_date date [note: 'Scheduled date the guest is expected to check in to the property. Used for occupancy forecasting, revenue projections, and operational planning (housekeeping, staffing).']
  average_daily_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average nightly room rate for this reservation, calculated as total_room_revenue divided by (length_of_stay * number_of_rooms). Core KPI for revenue management and competitive benchmarking.']
  booking_date date [note: 'Date the reservation was originally created in the system. Used to calculate booking lead time, pickup analysis, and demand forecasting accuracy.']
  booking_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the reservation. Tracks progression from initial booking through completion or cancellation. Updated by PMS based on guest actions and property operations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: confirmed|tentative|waitlisted|cancelled|no_show|checked_in|checked_out — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  booking_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time the reservation was created, including timezone. Principal business event timestamp for this transaction. Used for channel performance analysis and real-time availability updates.']
  cancellation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time the reservation was cancelled, if applicable. Null for active reservations. Used to calculate cancellation rates and revenue displacement analysis.']
  commission_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total commission payable to the booking channel or travel agent for this reservation. Calculated based on commission schedule and total room revenue.']
  confirmation_number varchar [note: 'Externally-facing alphanumeric confirmation code provided to the guest at time of booking. Used by guests to retrieve, modify, or cancel their reservation. Unique across all properties and channels.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this reservation record was first created in the data warehouse. Used for data lineage and audit trail. Distinct from booking_timestamp which represents the business event time.']
  crs_confirmation_number varchar [note: 'Confirmation number generated by Sabre SynXis CRS for reservations originating through the central reservation system. Used for cross-system reconciliation and channel attribution.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts on this reservation (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Used for multi-currency reporting and financial consolidation.']
  departure_date date [note: 'Scheduled date the guest is expected to check out from the property. Used to calculate length of stay and room availability for subsequent bookings.']
  early_checkin_requested_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the guest has requested early check-in (before standard check-in time). Used for operational planning and guest service recovery.']
  guarantee_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the reservation is guaranteed against no-show. Determines cancellation policy enforcement and revenue recognition timing.']
  late_checkout_requested_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the guest has requested late check-out (after standard check-out time). Used for housekeeping scheduling and room availability management.']
  length_of_stay varchar [note: 'Number of nights the guest is booked to stay, calculated as departure_date minus arrival_date. Key metric for revenue management, ALOS calculation, and yield optimization.']
  modification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to this reservation (date change, rate change, guest change, etc.). Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  number_of_adults varchar [note: 'Count of adult guests (typically age 18+) included in this reservation. Used for occupancy reporting, F&B forecasting, and compliance with fire safety regulations.']
  number_of_children varchar [note: 'Count of child guests (typically under age 18) included in this reservation. Used for amenity planning, F&B forecasting, and family-friendly service delivery.']
  number_of_rooms varchar [note: 'Count of rooms reserved under this booking. Typically 1 for individual reservations; may be greater than 1 for multi-room bookings or small groups.']
  package_code varchar [note: 'Code representing a bundled package offering if this reservation includes additional services beyond room only (e.g., breakfast included, spa credit, parking, resort fee). Null for room-only bookings.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Primary payment instrument used to guarantee or settle this reservation. Distinct from payment channel (web, mobile, front desk). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: credit_card|debit_card|cash|bank_transfer|mobile_payment|loyalty_points|corporate_billing — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  pms_reservation_code varchar [note: 'Native reservation identifier from Oracle OPERA PMS. Used for operational lookups and integration with front desk, housekeeping, and cashiering modules.']
  points_earned varchar [note: 'Number of loyalty program points earned by the guest for this reservation. Calculated based on room revenue, member tier, and promotional multipliers.']
  special_requests varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing guest special requests at time of booking (e.g., high floor, near elevator, extra pillows, early check-in). Used by operations to enhance guest experience.']
  total_room_revenue decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total room revenue for this reservation across all nights, before taxes and fees. Calculated as nightly rate multiplied by length of stay and number of rooms. Key component of RevPAR and ADR calculations.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this reservation record was last updated in the data warehouse. Used for incremental ETL processing and change data capture.']
  vip_status_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this reservation is flagged for VIP treatment (high-value guest, loyalty elite member, celebrity, executive, etc.). Triggers enhanced service protocols and amenities.']

  Note: 'Core master record for an individual guest reservation across all booking channels (direct, OTA, GDS, CRS via Sabre SynXis). Captures the full booking lifecycle from inquiry through confirmation, modification, cancellation, and no-show. Stores arrival/departure dates, LOS (Length of Stay), room type requested, rate code, BAR/LRA/NRR rate plan, guarantee method, booking status, source channel, CRS confirmation number, OPERA PMS reservation ID, number of adults/children, and special request flags. SSOT for reservation identity across the enterprise.'
}

Table reservation.booking_status_history {
  booking_status_history_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for booking_status_history']
  reservation_booking_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the parent reservation record. Links this status history event to the specific booking being tracked.']
  agent_name varchar [note: 'The name of the agent or system user who performed the action. Denormalized for audit trail completeness and reporting convenience.']
  booking_source_code varchar [note: 'The specific source within the channel (e.g., EXPEDIA, BOOKING_COM, BRAND_WEBSITE, CALL_CENTER). Provides granular attribution for the event.']
  cancellation_reason_code varchar [note: 'For cancellation events, the specific reason code (e.g., GUEST_CANCEL, WEATHER, EMERGENCY, DUPLICATE_BOOKING, RATE_ISSUE). Null for non-cancellation events.']
  channel_code varchar [note: 'The distribution channel through which this event was initiated (e.g., DIRECT, OTA, GDS, CRS, VOICE, WALK_IN, MOBILE_APP). Denormalized for audit completeness.']
  confirmation_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known unique confirmation number for the reservation. Denormalized from parent reservation for audit trail completeness and query performance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this history record was created in the data warehouse. This is the ETL load time, distinct from event_timestamp.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  dispute_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this event is associated with a guest dispute or service recovery case. True if disputed, False otherwise.']
  event_date date [note: 'The calendar date of the event, extracted from event_timestamp for simplified date-based reporting and partitioning.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when this lifecycle event occurred in the source system. This is the business event time, not the ETL load time.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Discriminator indicating the category of lifecycle event: status_change (status transition only), modification (booking amendment), cancellation (booking cancelled), no_show (guest did not arrive), check_in (guest arrived), check_out (guest departed).']
  guest_notification_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an automated notification (email, SMS, push) was sent to the guest for this event. True if sent, False otherwise.']
  guest_notification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the guest notification was sent. Null if no notification was sent.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'The IP address from which the event was initiated. Captured for security audit, fraud detection, and compliance purposes. May be considered PII in some jurisdictions.']
  modification_field_name varchar [note: 'For modification events, the specific field or attribute that was changed (e.g., arrival_date, room_type_code, rate_code, number_of_adults). Enables granular change tracking.']
  modification_type varchar [note: 'For modification events, specifies the category of amendment: date_change (arrival/departure dates), room_type_change (upgrade/downgrade), rate_plan_change (rate code switch), occupancy_change (number of guests), special_request_update (preferences), guest_name_change (name correction), payment_method_change (guarantee method), package_change (package modification), add_on_change (ancillary services). Null for non-modification events. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: date_change|room_type_change|rate_plan_change|occupancy_change|special_request_update|guest_name_change|payment_method_change|package_change|add_on_change — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  new_status varchar [note: 'The reservation status after this event. Represents the current state of the booking at the time of this history record. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: inquiry|tentative|waitlisted|confirmed|guaranteed|modified|cancelled|no_show|checked_in|checked_out|due_in|due_out — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  new_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'For modification events, the value of the field after the change. Stored as string to accommodate various data types. Null for non-modification events.']
  no_show_reason_code varchar [note: 'For no-show events, the reason code (e.g., NO_CONTACT, FLIGHT_DELAY, GUEST_FORGOT, DUPLICATE_BOOKING). Null for non-no-show events.']
  original_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'For modification events, the value of the field before the change. Stored as string to accommodate various data types. Null for non-modification events.']
  penalty_fee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'For cancellation or modification events, any penalty or change fee assessed per the rate plan rules. Zero if no penalty applies.']
  previous_status varchar [note: 'The reservation status immediately before this event. Null for the initial status record. Values align with Oracle OPERA PMS reservation status codes. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: inquiry|tentative|waitlisted|confirmed|guaranteed|modified|cancelled|no_show|checked_in|checked_out|due_in|due_out — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  property_code varchar [note: 'The unique code identifying the hotel or resort property where this reservation event occurred. Denormalized for partition key and audit completeness.']
  rate_difference_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'For modification events, the monetary difference between the original and new rate (positive for increase, negative for decrease). Null for non-rate-change events.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason for the status change or modification (e.g., GUEST_REQUEST, RATE_CHANGE, OVERBOOKING, NO_SHOW, EARLY_DEPARTURE, SYSTEM_AUTO). Aligns with property-defined reason code tables.']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation or additional context for the status change or modification. Captures agent notes or system-generated descriptions.']
  revenue_impact_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The estimated revenue impact of this event (positive for revenue gain, negative for revenue loss). Calculated based on rate changes, cancellations, and penalties.']
  sla_actual_minutes varchar [note: 'The actual processing time in minutes from event initiation to completion. Used to measure SLA performance.']
  sla_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this event met the defined Service Level Agreement (SLA) for processing time. True if compliant, False if SLA was breached.']
  sla_target_minutes varchar [note: 'The target processing time in minutes defined by the SLA for this event type. Used to calculate SLA compliance.']
  system_source varchar [note: 'The source system or interface that generated this event (e.g., OPERA_PMS for front desk, SYNXIS_CRS for central reservations, MOBILE_APP for guest self-service).']
  transaction_reference varchar [note: 'The unique transaction or session identifier from the source system. Enables correlation with source system logs for troubleshooting and dispute resolution.']
  user_agent varchar [note: 'The browser or application user agent string for web/mobile-initiated events. Supports device analytics and troubleshooting.']

  Note: 'Immutable audit trail of all reservation lifecycle events including status transitions (Inquiry → Tentative → Confirmed → Modified → Cancelled → No-Show → Checked-In → Checked-Out) and all booking modifications. For status changes, captures previous status, new status, transition timestamp, triggering agent/system, and reason code. For modifications (the SSOT for all amendment records), captures amendment type (date change, room type upgrade/downgrade, rate plan switch, occupancy change, special request update), original values, new values, modification channel, modification timestamp, agent ID, and any associated rate difference or penalty fee. Enables lifecycle analytics, SLA compliance tracking, revenue impact analysis, dispute resolution, and guest service recovery workflows. Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS status change and modification events.'
}

Table reservation.cancellation {
  cancellation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the cancellation or no-show event. Primary key.']
  ar_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ar_invoice. Business justification: Cancellations trigger revenue reversals, refund processing, and penalty charges that must link to the original AR invoice for accurate revenue recognition, refund authorization, and financial reportin']
  booking_source_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.booking_source. Business justification: Cancellations require booking source attribution for channel-specific cancellation policy enforcement, commission reversal calculations, and OTA penalty assessment. Currently only links to ota_partner']
  cancellation_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reservation.cancellation_policy. Business justification: Cancellation currently references policy via cancellation_policy_code (string). Normalizing to FK allows joining to get full policy details (policy_name, policy_description, penalty_type, penalty_amou']
  market_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to revenue.market_segment. Business justification: Segment-level cancellation analysis (cancellation propensity, revenue lost by segment) is a standard revenue management report. Cancellation records need direct segment attribution for standalone canc']
  ota_partner_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the OTA partner if the cancellation was initiated by or processed through an OTA channel.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the guest who held the cancelled or no-show reservation.']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property where the cancellation or no-show occurred.']
  reservation_booking_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the reservation that was cancelled or resulted in a no-show.']
  confirmation_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known reservation confirmation number associated with the cancelled or no-show reservation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this cancellation record was first created in the system.']
  dispute_date date [note: 'Date when the guest initiated a dispute of the cancellation penalty or no-show fee.']
  dispute_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the guest has disputed the cancellation penalty or no-show fee charge.']
  dispute_resolution_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the dispute resolution process (open, resolved in guest favor, resolved in property favor, or withdrawn by guest).']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the cancellation or no-show event occurred. For cancellations: when the cancellation was processed. For no-shows: the date/time the guest failed to arrive.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Type of non-fulfillment event: guest-initiated cancellation, property-initiated cancellation, OTA-initiated cancellation, or no-show (non-arrival).']
  guarantee_charge_processed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the guarantee charge (penalty or no-show fee) was successfully processed against the guarantee method.']
  guarantee_method varchar [note: 'Method used to guarantee the reservation (credit card, deposit, corporate guarantee, travel agent guarantee, or none). Critical for no-show charge processing.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this cancellation record was last updated.']
  original_arrival_date date [note: 'The scheduled arrival date of the cancelled or no-show reservation.']
  original_departure_date date [note: 'The scheduled departure date of the cancelled or no-show reservation.']
  ota_chargeback_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary amount charged back to the OTA for commission recovery on this cancellation or no-show.']
  ota_chargeback_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the property is eligible to charge back the commission to the OTA for this cancellation or no-show per the OTA contract terms.']
  penalty_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary penalty or fee charged for the cancellation or no-show, in the property\'s base currency.']
  penalty_applicable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a cancellation penalty or no-show fee is applicable based on the policy and timing of the event.']
  penalty_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the penalty amount.']
  posting_status varchar [note: 'Status of the penalty charge posting to the guest folio or account (posted, pending, reversed, or failed).']
  processing_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the cancellation or no-show was processed (PMS - Property Management System, CRS - Central Reservation System, OTA - Online Travel Agency, GDS - Global Distribution System, call center, mobile app, web). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pms|crs|ota|gds|call_center|mobile_app|web — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason for cancellation (e.g., CHANGE_PLANS, DUPLICATE_BOOKING, PRICE_ISSUE, EMERGENCY, OTHER). Null for no-show events.']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description or additional details about the cancellation reason provided by the guest or agent.']
  refund_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary amount refunded to the guest after applicable penalties, in the property\'s base currency.']
  refund_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the guest is eligible for a refund of any prepaid amounts after penalty deduction.']
  revenue_lost_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total room revenue lost due to the cancellation or no-show, calculated from the original reservation rate and length of stay.']
  reversal_date date [note: 'Date when the penalty charge was reversed.']
  reversal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cancellation penalty or no-show fee charge was subsequently reversed.']
  reversal_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation or reason for reversing the cancellation penalty or no-show fee charge.']
  room_nights_lost varchar [note: 'Number of room nights lost due to the cancellation or no-show, calculated as the difference between arrival and departure dates.']
  waiver_authorized_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the manager or agent who authorized the penalty waiver.']
  waiver_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cancellation penalty or no-show fee was waived by the property.']
  waiver_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason for waiving the penalty (e.g., LOYALTY_STATUS, EMERGENCY, SERVICE_RECOVERY, MANAGER_DISCRETION).']
  window_hours varchar [note: 'Number of hours before arrival that the cancellation occurred, used to evaluate penalty applicability per the cancellation policy.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing all reservation non-fulfillment events including guest cancellations, property-initiated cancellations, OTA-initiated cancellations, and no-show (non-arrival) events. For cancellations: records event date/time, reason code, applicable cancellation policy, penalty/fee amount, cancellation window evaluation, refund eligibility, and processing channel. For no-shows: records no-show date, no-show fee charged, guarantee method used for charge, automatic guarantee charge processing, and OTA chargeback eligibility. Common fields: waiver flag, waiver reason, posting status to guest folio, processing agent/channel, and any subsequent dispute or reversal. Critical for revenue leakage analysis, NRR policy enforcement, revenue recovery operations, OTA chargeback management, and CPOR (Cost Per Occupied Room) reporting.'
}

  Note: 'Master record for a group room block associated with a corporate account, travel agency, or event. Links to the event domain for MICE group blocks and to the channel domain for wholesale/tour operator blocks. Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS Group Block module and Delphi by Amadeus for event-linked groups.'
}

  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property where the group block pickup occurred. Enables property-level group performance analysis.']

  reservation_group_block_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent group block from which this pickup was made. Links to the master group block allocation.']
  reservation_rate_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reservation.reservation_rate_plan. Business justification: group_block_pickup currently stores rate_code as a denormalized STRING representing the rate at which an individual pickup was booked against the group block. The reservation_rate_plan table is the au']
  room_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.room_type.   arrival_date date [note: 'Scheduled check-in date for the pickup reservation. Used for occupancy forecasting and group block utilization analysis.']
  block_utilization_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the total group block consumed at the time of this pickup. Calculated as (total_block_rooms - remaining_block_rooms) / total_block_rooms * 100.']

  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the rate amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Essential for multi-currency group block management.']
  cutoff_date date [note: 'Date by which the group block inventory must be picked up or released back to general inventory. Critical for attrition management.']
  departure_date date [note: 'Scheduled check-out date for the pickup reservation. Used to calculate Length of Stay (LOS) and room night consumption.']

  guarantee_type varchar [note: 'Method used to guarantee the pickup reservation. Critical for no-show risk assessment and revenue assurance.']
