  booking_source_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.booking_source. Business justification: Room blocks originate through a specific booking source (direct sales, GDS, OTA). The plain-text booking_source column is a denormalization of booking_source. A proper FK enables channel-level group b']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Group blocks are often created for marketing campaigns (wedding packages, corporate incentive programs, event promotions). Required for attributing group revenue to campaigns, measuring campaign ROI i']
  campaign_offer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign_offer. Business justification: Group room blocks are frequently created in conjunction with specific promotional offers (group rate packages, meeting planner offers). room_block has campaign_id but not campaign_offer_id. Offer-leve']
  event_booking_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the associated event or group booking that this room block supports.']
  event_group_block_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.event_group_block. Business justification: Block reconciliation process: revenue managers must reconcile the inventory-side room_block against the event-side event_group_block to validate contracted vs. available room nights. A direct FK enabl']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Group room block revenue recognition and budget vs. actual group revenue reporting require blocks to be tied to fiscal periods. Finance teams reconcile contracted vs. picked-up group room nights by fi']
  group_evaluation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to revenue.group_evaluation. Business justification: Group evaluations assess displacement and revenue impact before accepting blocks. Linking blocks to originating evaluations enables post-booking performance review, wash factor validation, and revenue']
  ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Group blocks generate revenue recognition entries, attrition penalty postings, and deposit liability tracking that must post to specific GL accounts per USALI chart of accounts. Revenue managers need ']
  market_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to revenue.market_segment. Business justification: Room blocks are classified by market segment (group, SMERF, corporate) for displacement analysis, revenue mix reporting, and pace tracking. Revenue managers require segment attribution on every block ']
  member_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.member. Business justification: Group blocks for loyalty events, tier-specific promotions, and member appreciation weekends require tracking the owning member. Revenue management needs this for commission tracking, tier credit alloc']
  negotiated_rate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to revenue.revenue_negotiated_rate. Business justification: Corporate and consortia group blocks are priced against a specific negotiated rate agreement. This FK enables contract compliance tracking, rate loading verification, and committed room night reconcil']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the hotel or resort property where the room block is allocated.']
  revenue_rate_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to revenue.revenue_rate_plan. Business justification: Group block contracting requires linking the block to the specific rate plan used for pricing. Revenue managers load group rates against a rate plan; this FK enables rate compliance auditing and group']
  seasonal_calendar_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.seasonal_calendar. Business justification: Group room blocks are evaluated for displacement against seasonal demand forecasts. Revenue managers run group displacement analysis by season to decide whether to accept group business. Linking room_']
  attrition_penalty_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary penalty amount charged to the group if pickup falls below the attrition threshold. Expressed in property base currency.']
  attrition_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Contractual percentage threshold below which the group incurs attrition penalties for unmet room night commitments. Expressed as percentage (e.g., 20.00 for 20%).']
  available_room_nights varchar [note: 'Remaining number of room nights still available for pickup within the block.']
  block_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code identifying the room block, used for reservations and reporting. Externally-known identifier for group bookings.']
  block_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the room block, typically including the group or event name for easy identification.']
  block_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the room block: tentative (pending confirmation), definite (confirmed and contracted), waitlist (pending availability), cancelled (terminated), released (inventory returned), completed (event concluded).']
  block_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the room block by purpose: group (general group travel), event (MICE events), corporate (business contracts), tour (tour operators), wedding (social events), conference (meeting-focused).']
  cancellation_policy varchar [note: 'Text description of the cancellation terms and penalties applicable to the room block.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Text description explaining the reason for room block cancellation, used for lost business analysis.']
  cancelled_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the room block was cancelled, if applicable.']
  commission_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Commission rate percentage payable to intermediaries or group organizers for the block. Expressed as percentage (e.g., 10.00 for 10%).']
  confirmed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the room block status changed from tentative to definite (confirmed).']
  contracted_room_nights varchar [note: 'Total number of room nights contracted across all room types and dates in the block agreement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the room block record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in the block (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  cutoff_date date [note: 'Date by which the group must pick up reserved rooms or release them back to general inventory. Also known as release date.']
  deposit_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary deposit amount required to secure the block, expressed in property base currency.']
  deposit_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the deposit payment must be received to maintain the block reservation.']
  deposit_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a deposit is required to secure the room block.']
  end_date date [note: 'Last date of the room block period when rooms are reserved for the group or event.']
  internal_notes varchar [note: 'Confidential internal notes and comments for hotel staff regarding the room block management and service delivery.']
  lra_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the block has Last Room Availability commitment, meaning the group rate applies even if only one room remains available.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the room block record was last modified.']
  overbooking_allowed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the block allows overbooking beyond contracted room nights.']
  owner_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address of the block owner for communication and coordination.']
  owner_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary contact or decision-maker responsible for the room block on the client side.']
  owner_phone varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number of the block owner.']
  picked_up_room_nights varchar [note: 'Cumulative number of room nights actually reserved and consumed from the block by guests.']
  pickup_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated percentage of contracted room nights that have been picked up (reserved). Expressed as percentage (e.g., 85.50 for 85.5%).']
  special_requests varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing any special requirements, preferences, or requests associated with the room block (e.g., room location preferences, amenities, accessibility needs).']
  start_date date [note: 'First date of the room block period when rooms are reserved for the group or event.']

  Note: 'Group or event room block record reserving a contracted quantity of rooms by room type for a defined date range and pickup period. Captures block code, block name, group/event association, contracted room-nights by type and date, cumulative pickup, release/cutoff dates, attrition percentage and penalty terms, wash factor, and block status lifecycle (tentative, definite, cancelled, released). Links to group reservations and event contracts. Supports group inventory management, attrition tracking, and block release decisions in Oracle OPERA PMS and Delphi by Amadeus.'
}

Table inventory.block_pickup {
  block_pickup_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the daily block pickup tracking record.']
  booking_source_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.booking_source. Business justification: Group block pickup must be tracked by booking source for channel-level pickup pace reporting and commission calculations. booking_channel_code is a plain-text denormalization of booking_source; replac']
  corporate_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the corporate or event account that owns this group block contract.']
  demand_forecast_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to revenue.demand_forecast. Business justification: Block pickup pace reporting compares actual pickup against demand forecast projections for the same stay date and room type. Revenue managers use this link for automated pickup variance calculation an']
  event_group_block_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.event_group_block. Business justification: Group pickup pace reporting: event sales managers track daily pickup against the contracted event group block. Linking block_pickup directly to event_group_block enables event-level pickup pace dashbo']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Block pickup pace reporting is performed by fiscal period to compare actual vs. budgeted group room revenue. Finance and revenue management use pickup data aligned to fiscal periods for period-close g']
  group_evaluation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to revenue.group_evaluation. Business justification: Block pickup tracking measures actual performance against group evaluation forecasts. Linking enables wash factor accuracy measurement, displacement cost validation, and RMS model tuning—essential for']
  market_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to revenue.market_segment. Business justification: Block pickup is segmented by market for revenue mix and pace reporting. Revenue managers track group pickup velocity by segment to identify underperforming segments and adjust strategy. `market_segmen']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the hotel or resort property where the block pickup occurred.']
  revenue_rate_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to revenue.revenue_rate_plan. Business justification: Block pickup pace reporting tracks rooms picked up against a specific rate plan to measure group revenue realization. Revenue management systems compare pickup ADR to the contracted rate plan; this FK']
  room_block_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent group room block contract for which pickup is being tracked.']
  room_type_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific room type (e.g., King Deluxe, Double Queen) for which pickup is tracked.']
  block_status varchar [note: 'The current lifecycle status of the group block for this stay date and room type.']
  contracted_rooms varchar [note: 'The total number of rooms committed in the original group block contract for this room type and stay date.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this block pickup record was first created in the system.']
  cumulative_pickup varchar [note: 'The total cumulative number of rooms picked up from the block from contract inception through the current pickup date.']
  cutoff_date date [note: 'The contractual date by which the group must pick up rooms or release unused inventory back to general availability.']
  days_to_arrival varchar [note: 'The number of days remaining from the pickup date until the stay date, used to assess booking window and release timing.']
  displacement_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'The estimated revenue opportunity cost of accepting this group block instead of selling rooms to higher-rated transient guests.']
  group_code varchar [note: 'The unique alphanumeric code assigned to the group block in the PMS for identification and reservation linking.']
  is_lra_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this block is eligible for Last Room Availability, meaning the group can book rooms even when the hotel is sold out.']
  net_pickup varchar [note: 'The adjusted pickup count after applying wash factor, representing the expected actual occupied rooms from the block.']
  pickup_date date [note: 'The date on which this pickup measurement was captured or calculated, representing the snapshot date for reporting.']
  pickup_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments regarding pickup performance, release decisions, or special circumstances for this block and stay date.']
  pickup_pace_variance varchar [note: 'The difference between current year pickup and prior year pickup at the same point in time, indicating booking pace trend.']
  pickup_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of contracted rooms that have been picked up, calculated as (rooms_picked_up / contracted_rooms) * 100.']
  pickup_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'The negotiated room rate (ADR) for rooms picked up from this block, used for revenue calculation and forecasting.']
  pickup_revenue decimal(12,2) [note: 'The total room revenue generated from picked-up rooms, calculated as rooms_picked_up multiplied by pickup_rate.']
  pickup_variance varchar [note: 'The difference between actual rooms picked up and contracted rooms (rooms_picked_up minus contracted_rooms), indicating over or under performance.']
  prior_year_pickup varchar [note: 'The number of rooms picked up for the same group or comparable group at the same point in time last year, used for pace analysis.']
  released_rooms varchar [note: 'The number of rooms from the block that have been released back to general inventory due to non-pickup or cutoff date passage.']
  remaining_block_rooms varchar [note: 'The number of rooms still available in the block that have not yet been picked up (contracted minus picked up).']
  rooms_picked_up varchar [note: 'The number of rooms from the block that have been reserved or booked by group attendees as of the pickup date.']
  stay_date date [note: 'The specific calendar date for which block pickup is being measured. This is the guest arrival or occupancy date.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this block pickup record was last modified or refreshed.']
  wash_factor decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of block rooms expected to cancel or no-show, used to adjust net pickup forecasts and overbooking strategies.']

  Note: 'Daily pickup tracking record for group room blocks capturing rooms picked up per room type per stay date against the contracted block. Tracks cumulative pickup, remaining block availability, pickup pace versus prior year, and variance to contracted rooms. Supports group revenue management decisions and block release timing in Oracle OPERA PMS and IDeaS G3.'
}

Table inventory.out_of_order {
  out_of_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for out_of_order']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: OOO room financial impact (lost revenue estimates, actual repair costs, insurance claims) must be reported by fiscal period for period-close financial statements. Finance teams require OOO records tie']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fixed_asset. Business justification: OOO room records are frequently caused by a specific fixed asset failure (HVAC, plumbing, elevator). Linking OOO to fixed_asset enables capex/maintenance cost tracking, asset impairment analysis, and ']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the hotel property where the room is located.']
  room_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific physical room that is out-of-order or out-of-service.']
  service_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to experience.service_case. Business justification: OOO records triggered by guest complaints (guest_impacted_flag, relocation_required_flag) reference the originating service case. Facilities and guest services teams use this link for SLA compliance r']
  actual_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Actual cost incurred for repairs, maintenance, or renovation work. Captured upon work order completion for financial reconciliation and FF&E tracking.']
  actual_return_date date [note: 'Actual date when the room was returned to sellable inventory and made available for guest occupancy. Null if still out-of-order.']
  actual_return_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the room was returned to sellable inventory, including time of day for accurate inventory restoration tracking.']
  ada_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the out-of-order condition affects an ADA-compliant accessible room, requiring special handling for guest accommodation and compliance tracking.']
  approved_by_name varchar [note: 'Name of the manager or supervisor who approved the out-of-order designation for audit trail and accountability.']
  closed_by_name varchar [note: 'Name of the staff member who closed the out-of-order record and verified the room is ready for guest occupancy.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this out-of-order record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and operational reporting.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for cost amounts (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  duration_days varchar [note: 'Total number of days the room was out-of-order or out-of-service, calculated from start date to actual return date. Used for RevPAR and occupancy impact analysis.']
  estimated_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated cost in local currency for repairs, maintenance, or renovation work required to return the room to service. Used for CapEx and OpEx planning.']
  expected_return_date date [note: 'Planned or estimated date when the room is expected to be returned to sellable inventory after repairs or maintenance are completed.']
  guest_impacted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a guest reservation was impacted by this out-of-order designation (True if guest had to be relocated or reservation cancelled).']
  impact_on_occ decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage point impact on property occupancy rate due to this room being out-of-order. Used for STR reporting and competitive benchmarking.']
  impact_on_revpar decimal(10,2) [note: 'Calculated impact on property RevPAR due to this room being out-of-order. Critical metric for revenue management and performance analysis.']
  lost_revenue_estimate decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated revenue loss due to the room being unavailable for sale during the out-of-order period. Calculated using ADR and occupancy forecasts for RevPAR impact analysis.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, observations, or special instructions related to the out-of-order condition and repair work.']
  ooo_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the reason category for the room being out-of-order or out-of-service (e.g., MAINT, REPAIR, RENO, DAMAGE, INSPECT).']
  ooo_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the specific reason the room is out-of-order or out-of-service (e.g., plumbing leak, HVAC failure, carpet replacement, pest control treatment).']
  ooo_status varchar [note: 'Designation type: OOO (Out-of-Order - not counted in available inventory) or OOS (Out-of-Service - counted in inventory but not sold). Follows USALI standards for inventory accounting.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Urgency level assigned to the repair or maintenance work (Critical, High, Medium, Low). Critical priority indicates immediate revenue impact or safety concern.']
  record_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the out-of-order record (Open - newly reported, In Progress - work underway, Completed - room returned to service, Cancelled - designation reversed).']
  relocation_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether guest relocation to another room or property was required due to this out-of-order condition. Used for service recovery tracking.']
  reported_by_name varchar [note: 'Name of the staff member who reported the out-of-order condition for operational tracking and accountability.']
  responsible_department varchar [note: 'Department responsible for addressing the issue and returning the room to service (e.g., Housekeeping, Maintenance, Engineering, Facilities, Renovation). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Housekeeping|Maintenance|Engineering|Facilities|Renovation|Pest Control|Safety|Quality Assurance — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  safety_concern_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the out-of-order condition involves a safety or health hazard requiring immediate attention per OSHA or local safety regulations.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when the room was first designated as out-of-order or out-of-service and removed from sellable inventory.']
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the room was designated as out-of-order or out-of-service, including time of day for accurate inventory impact tracking.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this out-of-order record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  work_order_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the maintenance or repair work order associated with this out-of-order designation. Links to work order management system.']

  Note: 'Out-of-Order (OOO) and Out-of-Service (OOS) designation record for individual rooms removed from sellable inventory. Captures OOO/OOS reason code, start date, expected return-to-service date, actual return date, responsible department, work order reference, and impact on RevPAR. Distinguishes between OOO (not counted in available inventory) and OOS (counted but not sold) per USALI standards. Managed in Oracle OPERA PMS.'
}

  campaign_offer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign_offer. Business justification: Promotional allotments are created for specific campaign offers (e.g., early-bird packages, seasonal promotions). allotment already has campaign_id but not campaign_offer_id. Offer-level allotment tra']
  channel_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.channel_contract. Business justification: Allotments are contractually governed by channel agreements (e.g., wholesale allotments defined in OTA or travel agent contracts). Linking allotment to channel_contract enables contract compliance tra']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the distribution channel, Online Travel Agency (OTA), Global Distribution System (GDS), tour operator, wholesale partner, or corporate account to which this allotment is allocated.']

Revenue management needs this link for contract compliance monitoring, allotment utilization reporting, commission calcula']

Business justification: Channel allotments are managed by sales/distribution departments with dedicated cost center budgets for channel management labor, technology costs, and commission expenses. Essential for departmental ']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Allotment performance (sold vs. allocated rooms, commission accruals) is reviewed by fiscal period for channel revenue recognition and period-close reporting. Finance teams reconcile allotment-driven ']
  guest_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.guest_segment. Business justification: Allotments are created for specific guest segments (leisure, corporate, wholesale). Linking allotment to guest_segment supports segment-level allotment performance reporting and enables marketing team']
  market_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to revenue.market_segment. Business justification: Allotments are assigned to market segments (wholesale, consortia, corporate) for channel revenue reporting and segment mix analysis. Revenue managers require segment attribution on allotments to produ']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to experience.program. Business justification: Room allotments are created to protect inventory capacity for specific experience programs (e.g., honeymoon suite packages, wellness retreat allotments). Revenue management and experience operations t']

Business justification: Loyalty promotions (e.g., \'Double Points Weekend\', \'Stay 3 Pay 2\') require dedicated room allotments to guarantee inventory availability for qualifying members. Revenue management creates allotments d']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the hotel property where this allotment applies.']
  revenue_rate_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the rate plan associated with this allotment, defining the pricing structure and rate rules for bookings made through this channel or partner. Nullable if rate is managed separately.']
  room_type_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the room type (e.g., Standard King, Deluxe Suite) for which this allotment is defined.']
  seasonal_calendar_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.seasonal_calendar. Business justification: Allotments are negotiated and structured around seasonal demand periods. Revenue managers tie allotment contracts to seasonal calendar periods to evaluate performance against seasonal demand classific']
  tier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.tier. Business justification: Tier-restricted allotments are a core loyalty benefit fulfillment mechanism — certain room allotments (e.g., suite blocks) are reserved exclusively for Platinum/Gold tier members as part of guaranteed']
  activated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this allotment was activated and made available for bookings. Nullable if not yet activated.']
  adjustment_trigger_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether performance-based automatic adjustment of the allotment quantity is enabled (True) or disabled (False). When enabled, the allotment quantity may be increased or decreased based on sell-through performance.']
  allocated_quantity varchar [note: 'The total number of rooms pre-allocated to the channel or partner for the specified room type and date range.']
  allotment_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier or code for the allotment, used for tracking and reporting purposes in the Central Reservation System (CRS) and Property Management System (PMS).']
  allotment_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name for the allotment (e.g., \'Expedia Summer Block\', \'Corporate XYZ Annual Contract\').']
  allotment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the allotment: active (currently in use), inactive (not currently active), suspended (temporarily halted), expired (past end date), or pending (awaiting activation).']
  allotment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the allotment by partner type: channel (generic distribution channel), OTA (Online Travel Agency), GDS (Global Distribution System), wholesale (wholesale partner), corporate (corporate account), group (group booking block), or tour_operator (tour operator allocation). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: channel|ota|gds|wholesale|corporate|group|tour_operator — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  auto_release_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether unsold allotment inventory is automatically released back to general inventory when the release period is reached (True) or requires manual release (False).']
  available_quantity varchar [note: 'The number of rooms remaining available in this allotment (calculated as allocated_quantity minus sold_quantity).']
  booking_window_end_days varchar [note: 'The maximum number of days before arrival that bookings can be made from this allotment. For example, 365 means bookings can be made up to 365 days in advance. Nullable if no maximum booking window applies.']
  booking_window_start_days varchar [note: 'The minimum number of days before arrival that bookings can be made from this allotment. For example, 1 means bookings can be made starting 1 day before arrival. Nullable if no minimum booking window applies.']
  commission_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'The commission percentage paid to the channel or partner for bookings made through this allotment. For example, 15.00 represents a 15% commission.']
  contract_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier of the legal contract or agreement governing this allotment arrangement with the channel or partner.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this allotment record was first created in the Central Reservation System (CRS).']
  end_date date [note: 'The last date on which this allotment is effective. Nullable for open-ended allotments.']
  expired_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this allotment expired and was no longer available for bookings. Nullable if not yet expired.']
  freesale_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the channel or partner can sell beyond the allocated quantity by accessing general inventory (True) or is restricted to the allocated quantity only (False). Freesale allows partners to sell from the hotel\'s available inventory without a fixed allotment limit.']
  freesale_threshold_quantity varchar [note: 'The minimum number of rooms that must remain available in general inventory before freesale access is restricted or disabled for this allotment. Nullable if freesale is not enabled.']
  lra_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Last Room Availability (LRA) is enabled for this allotment, allowing the channel or partner to sell the last available room at the property (True) or restricting access when inventory is low (False).']
  max_los varchar [note: 'The maximum number of nights a guest can book when reserving from this allotment. Nullable if no maximum Length of Stay (LOS) restriction applies.']
  min_los varchar [note: 'The minimum number of nights a guest must book when reserving from this allotment. Nullable if no minimum Length of Stay (LOS) restriction applies.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this allotment record was last modified or updated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about this allotment, including special terms, conditions, or operational instructions.']
  overbooking_limit varchar [note: 'The maximum number of rooms that can be overbooked for this allotment beyond the allocated quantity, used for yield optimization and revenue management. Nullable if overbooking is not permitted.']
  performance_threshold_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'The minimum sell-through percentage required to maintain or increase the allotment quantity. For example, 80.00 means the partner must sell at least 80% of the allocated rooms to avoid reduction. Nullable if no performance threshold applies.']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'The priority ranking of this allotment relative to other allotments for the same room type and date range. Lower numbers indicate higher priority for inventory allocation. Used by Revenue Management System (RMS) for dynamic allocation.']
  release_period_days varchar [note: 'The number of days before the arrival date by which unsold allotment inventory must be released back to general inventory. For example, a 7-day release period means unsold rooms are returned to the hotel 7 days before arrival.']
  sold_quantity varchar [note: 'The number of rooms from this allotment that have been sold or reserved by the channel or partner.']
  start_date date [note: 'The first date on which this allotment becomes effective and inventory is allocated to the channel or partner.']
  suspended_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this allotment was suspended or temporarily halted. Nullable if never suspended.']

  Note: 'Channel or partner allotment record defining a pre-allocated inventory quota for a specific distribution channel, OTA, tour operator, wholesale partner, or corporate account. Captures allotment quantity by room type and date range, release period (days before arrival), auto-release rules, sell-through/freesale thresholds, performance-based adjustment triggers, and channel-specific rate linkage. Managed in Sabre SynXis CRS for real-time channel distribution and inventory allocation across OTA, GDS, and wholesale partners.'
}

  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fixed_asset. Business justification: Premium room amenities (TVs, furniture, artwork) meeting capitalization thresholds are tracked as fixed assets requiring depreciation schedules, useful life tracking, and CapEx budget management. Esse']
  inventory_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fnb.inventory_item. Business justification: Minibar replenishment and in-room F&B cost tracking: room amenities that are consumable F&B items (minibar stock) must reference the fnb inventory_item catalog to trigger stock depletion transactions,']
  parent_room_amenity_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on room_amenity (parent_room_amenity_id)']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the property where this amenity is available. Links to the property master data.']
  room_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific physical room where this amenity is installed. Null if amenity is defined at room type level only.']
  room_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.room_type. Business justification: room_amenity product description states amenities are \'available by room type and individual room\'. Currently only has FK to room (room_id). Many amenities are defined at room_type level (e.g., all De']
  tier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.tier. Business justification: Premium amenities (welcome gifts, pillow menus, turndown service) are tier-gated benefits in loyalty programs. Housekeeping and front desk operations require knowing which amenities are restricted to ']
  amenity_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the amenity type for inventory management and guest preference tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: technology|bathroom|bedding|minibar|furniture|climate_control|entertainment|connectivity — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  amenity_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the amenity type. Used for system integration and reporting consistency across properties.']
  amenity_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the amenity including brand, model, features, and guest-facing benefits. Used for marketing and guest communication.']
  amenity_name varchar [note: 'Business-friendly name of the amenity as displayed to guests and staff. Examples: Smart TV, Minibar, Espresso Machine, Premium Bedding.']
  charge_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Price charged to guest if amenity is consumed or used. Null for complimentary amenities. Used for minibar items and premium services.']
  condition_status varchar [note: 'Current physical condition of the amenity as assessed during last inspection. Drives maintenance and replacement decisions.']
  cost_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the unit cost. Required when unit_cost is populated.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this amenity record was first created in the system. Used for audit trails and data lineage.']

  display_sequence varchar [note: 'Sort order for displaying amenities in guest-facing interfaces and reports. Lower numbers appear first.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date after which this amenity configuration is no longer active. Null indicates no planned end date.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which this amenity configuration becomes active and available for guest use or booking display.']
  guest_visible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this amenity is displayed in guest-facing channels such as booking engines, mobile apps, and property websites. Used for marketing and distribution.']
  installation_date date [note: 'Date when the amenity was installed or placed in the room. Used for tracking asset age and planning replacement cycles.']
  is_ada_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the amenity meets ADA accessibility requirements. Critical for compliance and guest accommodation matching.']
  is_complimentary boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the amenity is provided at no additional charge to the guest. False indicates a chargeable amenity such as minibar items.']
  is_eco_friendly boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the amenity meets environmental sustainability standards. Used for green certification programs and guest preference matching.']
  is_premium_amenity boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this amenity is considered a premium or luxury feature that differentiates the room type and justifies higher ADR (Average Daily Rate).']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent inspection or condition assessment of the amenity. Critical for quality assurance and guest satisfaction.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this amenity record was most recently updated. Used for change tracking and synchronization.']
  maintenance_frequency_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between scheduled maintenance or inspection cycles for this amenity. Used for preventive maintenance scheduling.']
  model_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer model number or SKU for the amenity. Used for procurement, warranty tracking, and replacement ordering.']
  next_scheduled_replacement_date date [note: 'Planned date for replacing or refreshing the amenity based on lifecycle policy. Supports proactive FF&E capital planning.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational availability of the amenity. Inactive or out_of_service amenities are not available to guests.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'Purchase order reference number for the amenity acquisition. Used for financial reconciliation and audit trails.']
  quantity varchar [note: 'Number of units of this amenity present in the room or room type. Example: 2 for pillows, 1 for minibar.']
  special_instructions varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, handling instructions, or guest communication guidelines related to the amenity.']
  unit_cost decimal(10,2) [note: 'Acquisition cost per unit of the amenity. Used for CapEx (Capital Expenditure) tracking and ROI (Return on Investment) analysis.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit in which the amenity quantity is measured. Used for inventory tracking and replenishment.']
  warranty_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when manufacturer warranty coverage expires. Important for cost planning and vendor relationship management.']

  Note: 'Master catalog of in-room amenities and features available by room type and individual room, including minibar contents, technology amenities (smart TV, Bluetooth speaker), bathroom fixtures, and premium bedding configurations. Tracks amenity installation date, condition, and replacement schedule.'
}

Table revenue.revenue_rate_plan {
  revenue_rate_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each rate plan record in the revenue management system. Primary key for the revenue_rate_plan data product. Role: MASTER_RESOURCE.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Rate plans are frequently created to support specific marketing campaigns (flash sales, seasonal promotions). Campaign managers track which rate plans were deployed per campaign to measure ROI and rev']
  catering_menu_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.catering_menu. Business justification: MICE and group rate plans bundle specific catering menus (e.g., all-inclusive day delegate packages). Linking revenue_rate_plan to catering_menu enables package pricing validation, F&B revenue attribu']
  distribution_channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.channel. Business justification: Rate plans are distributed through specific channels (OTA, GDS, direct booking). Revenue managers configure which channels can sell which rate plans - core distribution strategy. Replaces denormalized']
  franchise_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.franchise_agreement. Business justification: Franchise agreements define royalty_fee_pct, marketing_fee_pct, and loyalty_fee_pct that directly affect rate plan economics and minimum rate floors. Linking revenue_rate_plan to franchise_agreement e']
  included_fnb_outlet_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fnb.fnb_outlet. Business justification: Rate plans include F&B benefits (breakfast at specific outlet, dining credits). Required for package fulfillment, benefit tracking, revenue allocation between rooms and F&B departments per USALI stand']
  ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Rate plans must post room revenue to specific GL accounts for revenue recognition and financial statement preparation. Essential for USALI compliance and audit trail.']
  loyalty_tier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.tier. Business justification: Rate plans enforce tier-specific eligibility (loyalty_tier_required field exists). Business process: validating member tier qualification for rate plan access at booking time. Essential for tier-based']
  market_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to revenue.market_segment. Business justification: revenue_rate_plan.market_segment_code (STRING) is a denormalized reference to the market segment the rate plan targets. Adding market_segment_id normalizes this to the authoritative market_segment rec']
  program_config_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.program_config. Business justification: Rate plans must validate against loyalty program rules (points eligibility, conversion rates, tier requirements). Business process: ensuring rate plan configuration aligns with active loyalty program ']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to experience.program. Business justification: Rate plans bundle experience programs (e.g., \'Romance Package\' rate plan tied to a curated experience program). This FK enables package revenue attribution and program eligibility enforcement at booki']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property or hotel at which this rate plan is applicable. Enables property-level rate plan management across a multi-property portfolio. A null value indicates a chain-wide or global rate plan applicable to all properties.']
  room_type_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific room type or room category to which this rate plan applies. A null value indicates the rate plan applies to all room types at the associated property.']
  touchpoint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to experience.touchpoint. Business justification: Rate plans are designed around guest journey touchpoints (pre-arrival, check-in, in-stay offers) for targeted pricing strategies. Revenue managers create touchpoint-specific rates to optimize conversi']
  advance_purchase_days varchar [note: 'Minimum number of days prior to arrival that a booking must be made to qualify for this rate plan. Common values are 7, 14, 21, or 30 days for advance purchase promotional rates. A value of 0 indicates no advance purchase requirement.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the revenue manager or director who approved this rate plan for distribution. Supports SOX financial controls and internal audit requirements for rate authorization governance.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this rate plan was formally approved for activation and distribution. Distinct from created_timestamp; captures the authorization event in the rate plan lifecycle. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  base_rate_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'The principal nightly room rate amount in the operating currency before any adjustments, taxes, or fees. Represents the rack rate or floor price for this rate plan. Used as the reference value for ADR (Average Daily Rate) and RevPAR (Revenue Per Available Room) calculations. Satisfies MASTER_RESOURCE MEASUREMENT_OR_VALUE category.']
  booking_window_close_date date [note: 'The last date on which new reservations can be made under this rate plan. After this date the rate plan is closed to new bookings even if the stay dates fall within the effective period.']
  booking_window_open_date date [note: 'The earliest date on which reservations can be made under this rate plan. Used to control promotional rate availability windows and prevent premature booking of seasonal or event-driven rates.']
  cancellation_policy_code varchar [note: 'Code referencing the cancellation and penalty policy associated with this rate plan (e.g., FLEX24H, NRR_NOREF, 48H_PENALTY). Determines guest refund eligibility and penalty charges upon cancellation. Critical for NRR (Non-Refundable Rate) and advance purchase rate plans.']
  commission_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of the room rate payable as commission to the booking agent or OTA when is_commissionable is true. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 10.00 for 10%). Null when is_commissionable is false.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this rate plan record was first created in the source system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and compliance reporting. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the base_rate_amount and all monetary fields on this rate plan are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency property portfolios.']
  discount_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount applied to the BAR (Best Available Rate) or rack rate to derive this rate plan\'s selling price. Used for corporate negotiated rates, loyalty member rates, and promotional rates. A value of 0 indicates no discount from the reference rate.']
  drr_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this rate plan is subject to DRR (Dynamic Rate Rule) adjustments by the revenue management system. When true, IDeaS G3 RMS or Infor EzRMS may automatically adjust the rate based on demand forecasts, occupancy levels, and competitive benchmarking.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The calendar date from which this rate plan becomes active and bookable. Used by IDeaS G3 RMS and Sabre SynXis CRS to control rate availability windows. Satisfies MASTER_AGREEMENT EFFECTIVE_FROM category.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The calendar date after which this rate plan is no longer available for new bookings. Null indicates an open-ended rate plan with no defined end date. Satisfies MASTER_AGREEMENT EFFECTIVE_UNTIL category.']
  gds_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this rate plan is distributed through GDS (Global Distribution System) channels such as Amadeus, Sabre, Travelport. Controls rate visibility to travel agents using GDS booking tools.']
  is_commissionable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a travel agent or OTA (Online Travel Agency) commission is payable on bookings made under this rate plan. Drives commission accrual and payment processing in SAP S/4HANA AP.']
  is_lra_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this rate plan carries LRA (Last Room Availability) obligation, meaning the contracted rate must be honored even when only the last room in a category is available. Applicable to corporate negotiated rate contracts.']
  is_refundable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether bookings under this rate plan are eligible for a full refund upon cancellation within the policy window. False for NRR (Non-Refundable Rate) plans; true for flexible BAR (Best Available Rate) plans.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this rate plan record was most recently updated in the source system. Used to detect changes for incremental ETL processing and audit compliance. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  loyalty_points_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether stays booked under this rate plan qualify for loyalty program points accrual. Wholesale, opaque, and certain promotional rates are typically ineligible for points earning.']
  loyalty_tier_required varchar [note: 'Minimum loyalty program membership tier required to access this rate plan. A value of \'none\' indicates the rate is open to all guests regardless of loyalty status. Used to gate exclusive member rates.']
  max_los varchar [note: 'Maximum number of nights permitted under this rate plan. Used to prevent extended stays at promotional or deeply discounted rates. A null value indicates no maximum stay restriction.']
  meal_plan_type varchar [note: 'Indicates the F&B (Food and Beverage) inclusions bundled with this rate plan. Room only includes no meals; bed and breakfast includes morning meal; half board includes breakfast and dinner; full board includes all three meals; all-inclusive covers meals, beverages, and selected activities.']
  min_los varchar [note: 'Minimum number of nights a guest must book to qualify for this rate plan. A LOS (Length of Stay) restriction used in yield optimization to drive longer stays during high-demand periods. A value of 1 indicates no minimum stay restriction.']
  ota_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this rate plan is distributed through OTA (Online Travel Agency) channels such as Booking.com, Expedia, or Agoda. Controls rate visibility on third-party booking platforms.']
  rate_category varchar [note: 'Secondary classification grouping rate plans by the market segment or distribution channel they serve. Public rates are available to all guests; negotiated rates are contracted with specific accounts; wholesale rates are sold through tour operators; group rates apply to block bookings; loyalty rates are exclusive to program members; employee rates are internal staff benefits.']
  rate_ceiling_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'The maximum rate above which this rate plan cannot be sold, used to cap pricing during peak demand periods for contracted or promotional rates. Null indicates no ceiling constraint.']
  rate_floor_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'The minimum rate below which this rate plan cannot be sold, regardless of dynamic pricing adjustments. Protects rate integrity and brand positioning. Used by IDeaS G3 RMS as a hard constraint in yield optimization algorithms.']
  rate_level varchar [note: 'Indicates the organizational scope at which this rate plan is defined and managed. Property-level plans apply to a single hotel; brand-level plans apply across a brand portfolio; chain-level plans apply across the entire company; global plans may span international markets.']
  rate_plan_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the rate plan across systems (e.g., BAR, CORP001, PKG-SPA). Used as the business key in IDeaS G3 RMS, Sabre SynXis CRS, and Oracle OPERA PMS for rate plan lookup and distribution. Satisfies MASTER_RESOURCE BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category.']
  rate_plan_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the rate plan\'s terms, inclusions, and conditions as displayed to guests and booking agents. Includes details on what is included in the rate, any special conditions, and marketing messaging.']
  rate_plan_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the rate plan displayed to guests and revenue managers (e.g., \'Best Available Rate\', \'AAA Member Rate\', \'Advance Purchase 21-Day\'). Satisfies MASTER_RESOURCE IDENTITY_LABEL category.']
  rate_plan_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the rate plan by its commercial structure. BAR (Best Available Rate) is the publicly available flexible rate; LRA (Last Room Availability) guarantees availability to contracted accounts; NRR (Non-Refundable Rate) carries no cancellation flexibility; corporate rates are negotiated with companies; package rates bundle room with ancillary services; promotional rates are time-limited offers. Satisfies MASTER_RESOURCE CLASSIFICATION_OR_TYPE category.']
  rate_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the rate plan. Active plans are available for booking; inactive plans are temporarily disabled; draft plans are under configuration; suspended plans are paused due to yield or compliance review; archived plans are retired and no longer bookable. Satisfies MASTER_RESOURCE LIFECYCLE_STATUS category.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this rate plan record was sourced. Enables data lineage tracking and conflict resolution when the same rate plan exists in multiple systems.']
  source_system_rate_reference varchar [note: 'The native identifier of this rate plan in the originating operational system (IDeaS G3 RMS, Sabre SynXis CRS, or Oracle OPERA PMS). Enables bidirectional traceability between the lakehouse Silver layer and the system of record.']
  stay_date_restriction varchar [note: 'Defines any day-of-week or date-specific restrictions on when stays under this rate plan are permitted. Weekday-only and weekend-only restrictions are common for corporate and leisure rates respectively; blackout dates exclude specific high-demand periods.']
  tax_inclusive boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the base_rate_amount includes applicable taxes (VAT, occupancy tax, city tax). True for tax-inclusive markets; false for markets where taxes are added at checkout. Critical for accurate revenue recognition under USALI and IFRS/GAAP.']

  Note: 'Master catalog of all rate plans available across properties, including BAR (Best Available Rate), LRA (Last Room Availability), NRR (Non-Refundable Rate), corporate negotiated rates, package rates, and promotional rates. Defines rate structure, cancellation policy, meal plan inclusions, booking conditions, and distribution eligibility. Sourced from IDeaS G3 RMS and Sabre SynXis CRS. This is the SSOT for rate plan definitions.'
}

Table revenue.rate_restriction {
  rate_restriction_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each rate restriction record in the revenue management system. Primary key for the rate_restriction data product in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  cancellation_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reservation.cancellation_policy. Business justification: Revenue managers apply cancellation policy restrictions as part of yield management strategy. Rate restrictions must enforce specific cancellation terms (non-refundable, free cancellation windows) to ']
  distribution_channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.channel. Business justification: Restrictions (stop-sell, min LOS, CTA/CTD) are applied per distribution channel. Revenue managers close channels selectively based on demand - essential inventory control operation. Enables channel-sp']
  dynamic_rate_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to revenue.dynamic_rate_rule. Business justification: rate_restriction.rms_recommendation_flag (BOOLEAN) indicates that a restriction was recommended by the RMS (IDeaS G3 or Infor EzRMS). Dynamic rate rules in the RMS trigger rate restrictions (e.g., min']
  event_booking_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.event_booking. Business justification: Revenue managers apply specific rate restrictions (min LOS, CTA/CTD, hurdle rates) when large event bookings create displacement risk. Essential for RMS restriction management around group blocks and ']
  event_group_block_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.event_group_block. Business justification: Rate restrictions (minimum LOS, closed-to-arrival, stop-sell) are frequently applied in direct response to active group blocks to protect transient revenue. Linking rate_restriction to event_group_blo']
  exempt_promotion_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.promotion. Business justification: Rate restrictions include an is_loyalty_exempt flag; revenue managers must reference the specific loyalty promotion that triggers the exemption. Named process: restriction exemption management for loy']
  exempt_tier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.tier. Business justification: Rate restrictions can be tier-exempt (e.g., Platinum members bypass minimum LOS restrictions). The is_loyalty_exempt flag exists but no tier FK identifies which tier is exempt. Named process: tier-bas']
  market_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to revenue.market_segment. Business justification: Rate restrictions can be scoped to market segments (e.g., restrict Leisure segment on peak dates). rate_restriction has market_segment_code (STRING) which should be normalized to FK. N rate_restrictio']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property (hotel/resort) to which this rate restriction applies. Enables property-level yield optimization and restriction management across the portfolio.']
  revenue_rate_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the rate plan to which this restriction applies. Links to the rate plan master in the CRS/RMS (e.g., BAR, LRA, NRR, DRR). A restriction may apply to one or multiple rate plans; this field captures the primary rate plan association.']
  room_type_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific room type to which this restriction applies. When null, the restriction applies to all room types under the associated rate plan. Enables granular inventory control by room category.']
  seasonal_calendar_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.seasonal_calendar. Business justification: Rate restrictions (CTA, CTD, min LOS) are applied based on seasonal demand periods. rate_restriction.season_code is a denormalized reference to seasonal_calendar. A direct FK enables restriction audit']
  strategy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to revenue.strategy. Business justification: Rate restrictions are operational implementations of the revenue strategy (e.g., minimum LOS restrictions during high-demand periods as defined in the strategy). Adding strategy_id links each restrict']
  advance_purchase_days varchar [note: 'Minimum number of days prior to arrival that a booking must be made to qualify for this rate plan. Supports NRR (Non-Refundable Rate) and advance purchase yield strategies. Null when restriction type is not ADVANCE_PURCHASE.']
  applied_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this rate restriction was activated and distributed to connected channels (CRS, GDS, OTA). Represents the principal business event time — when the restriction became operationally effective in the distribution ecosystem. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or employee identifier of the revenue manager or system process that created this rate restriction record. Supports SOX financial controls audit trail and accountability for yield management decisions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this rate restriction record was first created in the source system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and SLA compliance tracking. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the hurdle_rate_amount and any monetary thresholds defined in this restriction (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Ensures consistent financial reporting per USALI standards.']
  days_of_week_mask varchar [note: '7-character binary string indicating which days of the week the restriction applies, ordered Monday through Sunday (e.g., \'1111100\' = Mon–Fri only, \'0000011\' = Sat–Sun only). Enables day-of-week yield strategies such as weekend minimum stay requirements.']
  demand_forecast_level varchar [note: 'Demand forecast classification at the time this restriction was created or last updated, as determined by the RMS (IDeaS G3 or Infor EzRMS). Provides context for why the restriction was applied and supports post-hoc yield strategy analysis.']
  distribution_status varchar [note: 'Indicates the current distribution state of this restriction across connected channels via Sabre SynXis CRS. \'pending\' = queued for distribution; \'distributed\' = successfully pushed to all channels; \'failed\' = distribution error; \'partial\' = distributed to some but not all channels.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The calendar date from which this rate restriction becomes active and enforceable. Corresponds to the stay date (not booking date) unless the restriction type is ADVANCE_PURCHASE. Formatted as yyyy-MM-dd per model conventions.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The last calendar date on which this rate restriction is enforceable (inclusive). Null indicates an open-ended restriction with no defined end. Formatted as yyyy-MM-dd per model conventions.']
  hurdle_rate_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Minimum acceptable rate (in the property\'s base currency) below which a booking will be rejected for this rate plan on the restricted date(s). A key RMS yield control used to protect ADR during high-demand periods. Null when restriction type is not HURDLE_RATE.']
  is_blackout boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the effective date range is designated as a blackout period, during which the rate plan is completely closed to new bookings. Commonly applied during peak demand events, holidays, or sold-out periods.']
  is_cta boolean [note: 'Indicates whether arrivals are blocked on the effective date(s) for this rate plan and room type. When True, guests cannot check in on the restricted date, preventing short-stay bookings that fragment inventory during high-demand periods.']
  is_ctd boolean [note: 'Indicates whether departures are blocked on the effective date(s) for this rate plan and room type. When True, guests cannot check out on the restricted date, used to extend stays through high-demand nights and maximize occupancy continuity.']
  is_loyalty_exempt boolean [note: 'Indicates whether members of the hotel\'s loyalty program are exempt from this restriction (e.g., LRA — Last Room Availability guarantee for elite members). Supports loyalty program administration and member benefit fulfillment.']
  last_distributed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this restriction was last successfully distributed to external channels (OTA, GDS) via Sabre SynXis CRS. Used to monitor distribution latency and SLA compliance for rate parity management.']
  max_advance_purchase_days varchar [note: 'Maximum number of days prior to arrival within which a booking must be made to qualify for this rate plan. Defines the booking window ceiling for advance purchase restrictions, preventing bookings made too far in advance at promotional rates.']
  max_los varchar [note: 'Maximum number of nights a guest is permitted to stay under this rate plan on the restricted date(s). Used to prevent long-stay bookings at discounted rates during high-demand periods. Null when restriction type is not MAX_LOS.']
  min_los varchar [note: 'Minimum number of nights a guest must stay to be eligible to book under this rate plan on the restricted date(s). A core yield optimization lever in revenue management. Null when restriction type is not MIN_LOS.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional operational notes or context provided by the revenue manager regarding this restriction. May include references to specific events, competitive intelligence, or strategy rationale not captured in structured fields.']
  override_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a revenue manager or front desk supervisor is permitted to manually override this restriction for individual bookings. When False, the restriction is hard-enforced and cannot be bypassed in the PMS or CRS.']
  override_authorization_level varchar [note: 'Minimum staff authorization level required to override this restriction when override_allowed is True. Ensures appropriate governance over yield control exceptions. Null when override_allowed is False.']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'Numeric priority rank used to resolve conflicts when multiple restrictions apply to the same rate plan, room type, and date. Lower values indicate higher priority. Ensures deterministic restriction enforcement when overlapping rules exist in the RMS.']
  restriction_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this rate restriction rule as defined in IDeaS G3 RMS or Sabre SynXis CRS. Used for cross-system reconciliation and distribution to OTA and GDS channels.']
  restriction_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name describing the rate restriction rule (e.g., \'Weekend Min 2-Night Stay\', \'Holiday CTA Block\'). Used by revenue managers and front desk staff to identify restrictions in PMS and RMS interfaces.']
  restriction_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text or coded explanation for why this restriction was applied (e.g., \'Peak demand event\', \'Holiday blackout\', \'Group block protection\', \'RMS auto-recommendation\'). Supports revenue strategy documentation and post-stay performance analysis.']
  restriction_scope varchar [note: 'Defines the level at which this restriction is applied: \'property\' = entire property; \'room_type\' = specific room category; \'rate_plan\' = specific rate plan only; \'channel\' = specific distribution channel (OTA, GDS, direct). Determines how broadly the restriction is enforced across inventory.']
  restriction_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the rate restriction record. \'active\' = currently enforced; \'inactive\' = disabled but retained for history; \'pending\' = scheduled but not yet effective; \'expired\' = past end date; \'overridden\' = manually superseded by a revenue manager.']
  restriction_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the restriction mechanism applied. MIN_LOS = Minimum Length of Stay; MAX_LOS = Maximum Length of Stay; CTA = Closed to Arrival; CTD = Closed to Departure; ADVANCE_PURCHASE = minimum days before arrival required to book; BLACKOUT = date fully closed to booking; HURDLE_RATE = minimum acceptable rate threshold. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MIN_LOS|MAX_LOS|CTA|CTD|ADVANCE_PURCHASE|BLACKOUT|HURDLE_RATE — promote to reference product]']
  rms_recommendation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this restriction was system-recommended by the RMS (True) or manually created by a revenue manager (False). Enables analysis of RMS automation adoption rates and comparison of system vs. human yield decisions.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record that originated this rate restriction record. Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation across IDeaS G3 RMS, Infor EzRMS, Oracle OPERA PMS, and Sabre SynXis CRS.']
  source_system_restriction_reference varchar [note: 'The native identifier of this restriction record in the originating operational system (IDeaS G3 RMS, Sabre SynXis CRS, etc.). Enables traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse Silver Layer and the system of record.']
  updated_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or employee identifier of the revenue manager or system process that last modified this rate restriction record. Supports audit trail and governance of yield management changes.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this rate restriction record was last modified in the source system. Tracks the most recent change for audit and reconciliation purposes. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']

  Note: 'Operational records of rate restrictions applied to rate plans and room types, including minimum LOS (Length of Stay), maximum LOS, closed-to-arrival (CTA), closed-to-departure (CTD), advance purchase requirements, and blackout dates. Managed in IDeaS G3 RMS and distributed via Sabre SynXis CRS. Drives yield optimization by controlling which rates are bookable under specific conditions.'
}

Table revenue.dynamic_rate_rule {
  dynamic_rate_rule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for a Dynamic Rate Rule (DRR) record in the revenue management system. Primary key for this entity.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Dynamic pricing rules are often triggered by marketing campaigns (e.g., increase rates during high-awareness periods). Revenue managers need campaign context to evaluate rule performance and campaign-']
  competitive_set_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the competitive set (comp set) used as the benchmark for competitive-based Dynamic Rate Rules. References the STR STAR Report comp set configuration in Infor EzRMS for RGI, MPI, and ARI-driven pricing decisions.']
  demand_forecast_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the demand forecast record from IDeaS G3 RMS or Infor EzRMS that this forecast-based Dynamic Rate Rule references for its trigger evaluation.']
  distribution_channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.channel. Business justification: Dynamic pricing rules target specific channels differently (e.g., OTA rates adjust based on commission costs, direct channel gets best rate). RMS systems apply channel-specific rate optimization strat']
  guest_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to guest.segment. Business justification: Dynamic pricing rules target specific guest segments for personalized rate adjustments. Revenue management systems apply segment-specific pricing logic (e.g., loyalty tier discounts, corporate negotia']
  ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Dynamic pricing adjustments must post to specific GL accounts for audit trail, revenue variance tracking, and SOX compliance in automated pricing systems.']
  loyalty_tier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.tier. Business justification: Dynamic pricing rules adjust rates based on member tier for personalized revenue optimization. Business process: applying tier-specific rate adjustments in revenue management systems to reward high-va']
  market_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to revenue.market_segment. Business justification: Dynamic rate rules can be scoped to specific market segments. dynamic_rate_rule has market_segment_code (STRING) which should be normalized to FK. N DRR records → 1 market_segment. Removes redundant m']
  monitored_fnb_outlet_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fnb.fnb_outlet. Business justification: Dynamic pricing rules monitor outlet occupancy for total revenue optimization (restaurant full = adjust room+dining packages). Required for integrated revenue management systems that optimize property']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the hotel or resort property to which this Dynamic Rate Rule applies. Links to the property master record in Oracle OPERA PMS.']
  revenue_rate_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the rate plan (e.g., BAR, LRA, NRR) to which this Dynamic Rate Rule applies. A null value indicates the rule applies across all eligible rate plans for the property.']
  room_type_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific room type (e.g., standard king, deluxe suite) to which this Dynamic Rate Rule applies. A null value indicates the rule applies across all room types at the property.']
  strategy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to revenue.strategy. Business justification: Dynamic rate rules implement the automated pricing adjustments defined within the revenue strategy. Adding strategy_id links each DRR to the governing strategy, enabling revenue managers to audit whic']
  triggering_promotion_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.promotion. Business justification: Dynamic rate rules are triggered by or aligned with loyalty promotions (e.g., rate floor adjustments during a tier-challenge promotion). Named process: promotion-triggered dynamic pricing rule configu']
  adjustment_direction varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the Dynamic Rate Rule increases or decreases the rate when triggered. Provides explicit directional intent separate from the sign of adjustment_value to prevent ambiguity in RMS configuration.']
  adjustment_type varchar [note: 'Method by which the Dynamic Rate Rule adjusts the published rate when triggered. Percentage applies a relative increase or decrease to the current rate; absolute_amount adds or subtracts a fixed currency value; bar_offset moves the rate a fixed amount above or below the Best Available Rate (BAR); bar_multiplier applies a multiplier to the BAR.']
  adjustment_value decimal(10,4) [note: 'The magnitude of the rate adjustment applied when the Dynamic Rate Rule is triggered. Interpreted according to adjustment_type: a value of 0.10 with type \'percentage\' means a 10% rate increase; a value of 25.00 with type \'absolute_amount\' means a $25 rate increase; a value of -15.00 with type \'bar_offset\' means $15 below BAR.']
  advance_booking_max_days varchar [note: 'Maximum number of days in advance of the arrival date within which a booking must be made for this Dynamic Rate Rule to be eligible to apply. Null indicates no upper advance booking restriction.']
  advance_booking_min_days varchar [note: 'Minimum number of days in advance of the arrival date that a booking must be made for this Dynamic Rate Rule to be eligible to apply. Supports advance purchase pricing strategies.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Workflow approval state of the Dynamic Rate Rule indicating whether it has been reviewed and authorized by a revenue manager before activation. Supports SOX-compliant pricing governance controls.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the revenue manager or director who approved this Dynamic Rate Rule for activation. Required for SOX financial controls audit trail on pricing decisions.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this Dynamic Rate Rule was approved for activation by the authorized revenue manager. Part of the pricing governance audit trail required under SOX financial controls.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this Dynamic Rate Rule record was first created in the system. Part of the standard audit trail for pricing configuration governance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the property in which rate floor, ceiling, and absolute adjustment values are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  days_of_week_mask varchar [note: 'Seven-character binary string (positions 1–7 = Mon–Sun) indicating which days of the week this Dynamic Rate Rule is eligible to fire. A value of \'1111100\' means Monday through Friday only. Enables day-of-week yield optimization strategies.']
  effective_from date [note: 'The calendar date from which this Dynamic Rate Rule becomes active and eligible to trigger automated pricing adjustments in the RMS. Aligns with the stay date range for yield optimization.']
  effective_until date [note: 'The calendar date on which this Dynamic Rate Rule expires and ceases to trigger automated pricing adjustments. Null indicates an open-ended rule with no defined expiry.']
  is_stackable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this Dynamic Rate Rule can be combined (stacked) with other active rules for the same stay date, room type, and rate plan. When False, this rule takes exclusive precedence based on rule_priority and no other rules are applied concurrently.']
  last_triggered_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this Dynamic Rate Rule was most recently evaluated as true and triggered an automated pricing adjustment in the RMS. Used by revenue managers to monitor rule activity and effectiveness.']
  los_max_nights varchar [note: 'Maximum Length of Stay (LOS) restriction in nights beyond which this Dynamic Rate Rule does not apply. Null indicates no upper LOS restriction.']
  los_min_nights varchar [note: 'Minimum Length of Stay (LOS) restriction in nights that must be satisfied for this Dynamic Rate Rule to apply. Used in LOS-based yield optimization to drive longer stays during high-demand periods.']
  max_rate_ceiling decimal(10,2) [note: 'The highest permissible rate (in property currency) that this Dynamic Rate Rule may produce after adjustment. Acts as a pricing guardrail to prevent rates from exceeding the property\'s maximum acceptable rate cap. Sensitive pricing strategy data.']
  min_rate_floor decimal(10,2) [note: 'The lowest permissible rate (in property currency) that this Dynamic Rate Rule may produce after adjustment. Acts as a pricing guardrail to prevent rates from falling below the property\'s minimum acceptable ADR threshold. Sensitive pricing strategy data.']
  pickup_window_days varchar [note: 'Number of days in the booking pickup window used to evaluate pickup velocity for pickup-based Dynamic Rate Rules. For example, a value of 7 means the rule evaluates rooms booked in the last 7 days.']
  rms_rule_reference_code varchar [note: 'Native rule identifier assigned by the source Revenue Management System (IDeaS G3 RMS or Infor EzRMS) for this Dynamic Rate Rule. Used for system-of-record traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse Silver layer and the operational RMS.']
  rule_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the Dynamic Rate Rule within the Revenue Management System (RMS). Used for cross-system reference between IDeaS G3 RMS, Infor EzRMS, and Sabre SynXis CRS.']
  rule_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the Dynamic Rate Rule\'s business purpose, pricing logic, and intended yield optimization outcome.']
  rule_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name describing the Dynamic Rate Rule, used by revenue managers to identify and manage pricing strategies within the RMS dashboard.']
  rule_priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority ranking that determines the order of evaluation when multiple Dynamic Rate Rules are eligible to fire simultaneously for the same property, room type, and rate plan. Lower numeric values indicate higher priority (e.g., 1 = highest priority).']
  rule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the Dynamic Rate Rule governing whether it is eligible to trigger automated pricing adjustments. Active rules are evaluated by the RMS engine; draft rules are under configuration; suspended rules are temporarily disabled; archived rules are retained for historical audit.']
  rule_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the Dynamic Rate Rule by the trigger mechanism driving automated pricing adjustments. Occupancy-based rules fire on OCC thresholds; pickup-based on booking velocity; competitive-based on RGI/ARI/MPI benchmarks from STR STAR Report; event-based on MICE or local demand events; LOS-based on Length of Stay (LOS) restrictions; forecast-based on RMS demand forecasts. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: occupancy_based|pickup_based|competitive_based|event_based|los_based|forecast_based — promote to reference product]']
