  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which this market segment definition is no longer active. Null indicates an open-ended segment with no planned expiry. Supports segment lifecycle management and historical reporting continuity when segment taxonomies are revised.']
  gdpr_data_subject_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether guest data associated with bookings in this market segment is subject to GDPR data subject rights (right to erasure, portability, access). Relevant for segments involving EU/EEA guests. Drives data retention and anonymization policies in the data lakehouse.']
  gds_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether rates for this market segment are distributed through GDS (Global Distribution System) channels such as Amadeus, Sabre, and Travelport. Controls rate loading and availability display in Sabre SynXis CRS for GDS-connected travel agencies.']
  group_block_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this market segment supports group room block reservations. Group-eligible segments (e.g., MICE, sports groups, tour groups) require group block management in Oracle OPERA PMS and Delphi by Amadeus for BEO (Banquet Event Order) coordination.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Numeric level of this segment within the market segment hierarchy. Level 1 represents top-level categories (e.g., Transient, Group); Level 2 represents sub-categories (e.g., Corporate, Leisure); Level 3 represents granular segments (e.g., Corporate Negotiated, Leisure OTA). Used for hierarchical roll-up in revenue reporting.']
  is_internal_use_only boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this market segment is for internal reporting and analytics purposes only and should not be exposed to guests, OTA partners, or external distribution channels. Internal segments (e.g., complimentary, house use, employee rate) are excluded from external rate distribution in Sabre SynXis CRS.']
  loyalty_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether bookings under this market segment are eligible to earn loyalty program points and benefits. Wholesale, crew, and certain contracted segments may be excluded from loyalty accrual per program rules. Drives loyalty point calculation logic in Salesforce CRM loyalty management.']
  market_segment_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the market segment, including its defining characteristics, typical guest profile, booking behavior, and applicable rate strategies. Used for training, documentation, and business glossary alignment across revenue management, reservations, and sales teams.']
  market_segment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the market segment record. Active segments are available for rate plan assignment, reservation coding, and revenue reporting. Inactive or deprecated segments are retained for historical analysis but excluded from new bookings. Aligns with MASTER_RESOURCE LIFECYCLE_STATUS requirement.']
  min_rate_override_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether revenue managers are permitted to override the minimum rate floor for this segment. Restricting overrides for high-value segments (e.g., LRA corporate) protects rate integrity. Configurable in IDeaS G3 RMS inventory controls and Oracle OPERA PMS rate management.']
  negotiated_rate_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether bookings in this segment require a pre-negotiated rate agreement (e.g., corporate account rate, government per diem, airline crew contract). When true, a valid rate agreement must exist before reservations can be accepted at segment rates in Oracle OPERA PMS.']
  no_show_propensity varchar [note: 'Qualitative classification of the historical no-show rate tendency for this market segment. Distinct from cancellation propensity — no-shows occur without prior cancellation and have different revenue recovery implications. Used in overbooking model calibration within IDeaS G3 RMS.']
  ota_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether rates for this market segment are distributed through OTA (Online Travel Agency) channels such as Expedia, Booking.com, and Hotels.com. Controls channel availability in Sabre SynXis CRS and impacts OTA commission cost tracking.']
  pci_scope boolean [note: 'Indicates whether payment card data associated with bookings in this segment falls within PCI DSS scope. Segments with direct card-present or card-not-present transactions require PCI-compliant data handling. Drives data masking and tokenization requirements in the lakehouse.']
  pms_segment_code varchar [note: 'The segment code as configured in Oracle OPERA PMS. Used for reservation coding, folio routing, and rate plan assignment at the property level. Enables reconciliation between the enterprise data lakehouse and the operational PMS source system.']
  price_sensitivity varchar [note: 'Classification of the segment\'s sensitivity to rate changes. Inelastic segments (e.g., airline crew, government contracted) book regardless of rate fluctuations. Highly elastic segments (e.g., leisure OTA) respond strongly to price changes. Drives DRR (Dynamic Rate Rule) configuration in IDeaS G3 RMS.']
  rate_plan_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the rate plan type typically associated with this segment. BAR (Best Available Rate) for transient leisure, negotiated for corporate accounts, LRA (Last Room Availability) for top-tier corporate, NRR (Non-Refundable Rate) for advance purchase segments. Drives rate loading in Oracle OPERA PMS and Sabre SynXis CRS. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: bar|negotiated|lra|nrr|wholesale|package|complimentary — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  revenue_bucket varchar [note: 'Primary revenue category associated with this market segment for TRevPAR (Total Revenue Per Available Room) and USALI departmental reporting. Rooms-focused segments contribute primarily to rooms revenue; MICE segments contribute across rooms, F&B (Food and Beverage), and events revenue buckets.']
  rms_segment_code varchar [note: 'The segment code as configured in IDeaS G3 RMS or Infor EzRMS. Used for demand forecasting, pricing optimization, and inventory control at the segment level. May differ from PMS segment code due to system-specific taxonomy requirements.']
  segment_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification grouping the segment into a primary demand category. Drives pricing strategy, channel allocation, and revenue reporting roll-ups. Aligns with STR segment category definitions used in STAR Report benchmarking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: transient|group|contract|wholesale|ota|corporate|government|leisure|crew|airline — promote to reference product]']
  segment_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the market segment, used as the externally-known business identifier across Oracle OPERA PMS, IDeaS G3 RMS, Sabre SynXis CRS, and Infor EzRMS. Examples: CORP, TRANS, LEIS, OTA, GRP, GOVT, CREW. Aligns with STR segment code taxonomy for benchmarking and STAR Report mapping.']
  segment_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the market segment as displayed in reporting, dashboards, and revenue management systems. Examples: Corporate Negotiated, Leisure Transient, Online Travel Agency, Government/Military, Group Meetings. Used as the IDENTITY_LABEL for this resource entity.']
  segment_type varchar [note: 'Secondary classification distinguishing whether the segment represents individual (FIT — Free Independent Traveler) bookings, group blocks, negotiated corporate accounts, wholesale/tour operator contracts, or consortia arrangements. Used in IDeaS G3 RMS for demand segmentation and pricing strategy.']
  sort_order varchar [note: 'Numeric value controlling the display sequence of market segments in reports, dashboards, and system configuration screens. Ensures consistent ordering in Oracle OPERA PMS segment lists, IDeaS G3 RMS segment configuration, and BI reporting tools.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this market segment definition originated. Enables data lineage tracking and reconciliation between Oracle OPERA PMS, IDeaS G3 RMS, Sabre SynXis CRS, and Infor EzRMS segment taxonomies in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  str_segment_code varchar [note: 'The official STR (Smith Travel Research) segment code used for competitive benchmarking and STAR Report alignment. Enables mapping of internal segment definitions to the industry-standard STR taxonomy for RGI (Revenue Generation Index), MPI (Market Penetration Index), and ARI (Average Rate Index) calculations.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this market segment record was last modified. Tracks configuration changes to segment definitions, status updates, and taxonomy revisions. Essential for change data capture (CDC) in ETL pipelines feeding IDeaS G3 RMS and Infor EzRMS.']
  usali_segment_code varchar [note: 'The USALI (Uniform System of Accounts for the Lodging Industry) standardized segment code used for financial reporting and departmental P&L allocation. Ensures consistency in GOP (Gross Operating Profit), NOI (Net Operating Income), and EBITDA reporting across properties and for external financial audits under GAAP/IFRS.']

  Note: 'Master classification of revenue market segments used for demand analysis, pricing strategy, and performance reporting. Defines segment code, segment name, segment category (transient, group, contract, wholesale, OTA, corporate, government, leisure, crew, airline), contribution margin profile, booking behavior characteristics, average lead time, and cancellation propensity. SSOT for market segment taxonomy aligned to STR segment definitions and used across revenue, reservation, and channel domains.'
}

Table revenue.competitive_set {
  competitive_set_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for competitive_set']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.brand. Business justification: Competitive sets are defined by brand tier comparability — revenue managers scope STR comp sets to matching brand tiers. Brand alignment is a required input for comp set approval and STR submission. A']
  hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.hierarchy. Business justification: Competitive sets are defined at market and geographic levels that align with hierarchy nodes (geographic_region, str_market_name). Linking competitive_set to hierarchy enables portfolio-level STR benc']
  market_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to revenue.market_segment. Business justification: competitive_set.market_segment (STRING) is a denormalized reference to the market segment focus of the competitive set. Adding market_segment_id normalizes this to the authoritative market_segment rec']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the subject property (the \'home\' property) that owns this competitive set definition. Links to the property master record in the Property domain.']
  advance_purchase_window_days varchar [note: 'The booking lead time window in days used for rate shopping comparisons within this competitive set (e.g., 0 = same-day, 7 = 7-day advance, 30 = 30-day advance). Ensures rate intelligence is captured at consistent booking horizons for fair ADR comparison.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which the competitive set definition was formally approved by the revenue management authority. Supports audit trail requirements for comp set governance and STR STAR Report submission integrity.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the revenue management leader or Director of Revenue who approved this competitive set definition. Required for governance and audit trail of comp set changes that affect STR STAR Report submissions.']
  ari_target decimal(5,2) [note: 'The Average Rate Index (ARI) target threshold for this competitive set. ARI = Property ADR / Comp Set ADR. A value above 1.00 indicates the property targets a higher ADR than the competitive set average. Drives BAR pricing strategy in IDeaS G3 RMS.']
  benchmarking_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code used for normalizing ADR, RevPAR, and TRevPAR benchmarking metrics across all properties in the competitive set (e.g., \'USD\', \'EUR\', \'GBP\'). Ensures consistent monetary comparison in STR STAR Report analysis.']
  competitive_set_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name assigned to the competitive set (e.g., \'Downtown Luxury Primary Set\', \'Airport Corridor Secondary Set\'). Used in Infor EzRMS and STR STAR Report submissions to identify the grouping.']
  competitive_set_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the competitive set. \'active\' sets are used in live RMS benchmarking and STR reporting; \'inactive\' sets are suspended; \'pending_approval\' awaits revenue management sign-off; \'archived\' are historical sets retained for trend analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this competitive set record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and change history tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  demand_segment_focus varchar [note: 'The primary guest demand segment this competitive set is designed to benchmark. \'transient\' focuses on FIT (Free Independent Traveler) and leisure; \'group\' focuses on MICE (Meetings, Incentives, Conferences, Exhibitions) and group blocks; \'contract\' focuses on negotiated corporate rates; \'all_segments\' covers the full mix.']
  effective_from date [note: 'The date from which this competitive set definition becomes active and is used for STR STAR Report benchmarking, RGI, MPI, and ARI calculations. Aligns with STR reporting period start.']
  effective_until date [note: 'The date on which this competitive set definition expires and is no longer used for active benchmarking. Null indicates an open-ended, currently active set. Supports historical trend analysis when comp set membership changes.']
  geographic_market varchar [note: 'The geographic market or submarket in which the competitive set operates (e.g., \'Manhattan Midtown\', \'Chicago O\'Hare Corridor\', \'Miami Beach\'). Aligns with STR market definitions used for Market Penetration Index (MPI) calculations.']
  ideass_comp_set_code varchar [note: 'The competitive set reference code as configured in IDeaS G3 RMS for pricing strategy alignment. Enables synchronization of comp set definitions between Infor EzRMS benchmarking and IDeaS G3 RMS dynamic pricing rules (DRR) and BAR optimization.']
  is_str_submitted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this competitive set has been formally submitted to STR (Smith Travel Research) for STAR Report benchmarking. True means the set is registered with STR and receives STAR Report data; False means it is an internal-only comp set.']
  last_reviewed_date date [note: 'The most recent date on which the competitive set membership and parameters were reviewed by the revenue management team. Best practice requires annual or semi-annual review to ensure comp set relevance as market conditions evolve.']
  los_benchmark_nights varchar [note: 'The standard Length of Stay (LOS) in nights used as the basis for rate comparison within this competitive set. Typically set to 1 night for transient benchmarking. Used to normalize ADR comparisons across properties with different LOS mix.']
  market_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the primary geographic market for this competitive set (e.g., \'USA\', \'GBR\', \'DEU\'). Used for multi-national portfolio benchmarking and currency normalization in revenue performance reporting.']
  max_room_count varchar [note: 'Maximum number of guestrooms a competitor property may have to qualify for inclusion in this competitive set. Used alongside min_room_count to define the acceptable size range for comp set membership.']
  member_count varchar [note: 'Total number of competitor properties currently included in this competitive set. STR STAR Report typically requires a minimum of 3 and maximum of 10 competitor properties for statistically valid benchmarking. Derived from the comp set membership roster but stored here for quick reference.']
  min_room_count varchar [note: 'Minimum number of guestrooms a competitor property must have to qualify for inclusion in this competitive set. Ensures size comparability for RevPAR and occupancy benchmarking. Typically aligned with the subject property\'s room count range.']
  mpi_target decimal(5,2) [note: 'The Market Penetration Index (MPI) target threshold for this competitive set. MPI = Property Occupancy Rate / Comp Set Occupancy Rate. A value above 1.00 indicates the property targets higher occupancy than the competitive set average. Used in revenue strategy planning.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next periodic review of this competitive set definition. Ensures comp set membership remains current and aligned with market dynamics. Triggers a review workflow in the Revenue Management System.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for revenue management team annotations regarding this competitive set, such as rationale for competitor inclusion/exclusion, market context, or pending changes. Not used in automated benchmarking calculations.']
  primary_benchmarking_metric varchar [note: 'The primary Key Performance Indicator (KPI) used to evaluate competitive performance for this comp set. Options include RevPAR (Revenue Per Available Room), ADR (Average Daily Rate), Occupancy Rate (OCC), TRevPAR (Total Revenue Per Available Room), or GOPPAR (Gross Operating Profit Per Available Room).']
  property_type_filter varchar [note: 'Filter defining the property service type composition of this competitive set. Ensures benchmarking is conducted against properties of comparable service level and amenity offering. Aligns with AHLA property classification standards.']
  rate_shop_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which competitor rates are retrieved from OTA and GDS channels for properties in this competitive set. Drives the cadence of dynamic pricing adjustments in IDeaS G3 RMS. Only applicable when rate_shopping_enabled is True.']
  rate_shopping_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether automated rate shopping (competitive rate intelligence) is active for this competitive set. When True, the RMS retrieves competitor BAR rates from OTA channels and GDS for dynamic pricing decisions. When False, only STR STAR Report data is used.']
  rgi_target decimal(5,2) [note: 'The Revenue Generation Index (RGI) target threshold set by the revenue management team for this competitive set. RGI = Property RevPAR / Comp Set RevPAR. A target above 1.00 indicates the property aims to outperform the comp set. Used in IDeaS G3 RMS and Infor EzRMS for performance goal-setting.']
  rms_comp_set_reference varchar [note: 'The native competitive set identifier as stored in the Revenue Management System (Infor EzRMS or IDeaS G3 RMS). Used for cross-system reconciliation and data lineage tracing between the lakehouse Silver Layer and the operational RMS.']
  set_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the competitive set indicating its strategic purpose. \'primary\' is the main STR STAR Report comp set; \'secondary\' is an alternate benchmarking group; \'custom\' is an ad-hoc internal set; \'str_defined\' is a set defined by STR directly; \'internal\' is used for internal rate intelligence only.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this competitive set definition was originated or last updated. Supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer. \'infor_ezrms\' = Infor EzRMS; \'ideass_g3\' = IDeaS G3 RMS; \'manual\' = manually entered; \'str_direct\' = received directly from STR.']
  str_comp_set_code varchar [note: 'The unique alphanumeric code assigned by STR (Smith Travel Research) to identify this competitive set in the STAR Report system. Required for submitting and retrieving benchmarking data from STR. Maps directly to the STR comp set identifier in Infor EzRMS.']
  str_market_code varchar [note: 'The STR-assigned market code identifying the geographic market in which this competitive set is benchmarked. Used to retrieve market-level aggregates for MPI calculation and to align with STR STAR Report market definitions.']
  str_report_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which STR STAR Report data is received and processed for this competitive set. Determines the cadence of RGI, MPI, and ARI metric refreshes in the Revenue Management System.']
  str_submarket_code varchar [note: 'The STR-assigned submarket code for more granular geographic benchmarking within a broader STR market. Used when the competitive set operates in a distinct submarket (e.g., airport vs. downtown within the same city market).']
  str_submission_date date [note: 'The date on which this competitive set was submitted to STR for STAR Report registration. Relevant only when is_str_submitted is True. Used for audit and compliance tracking of STR data agreements.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this competitive set record was most recently modified. Used to detect changes in comp set definitions, track membership updates, and support incremental ETL processing in the Databricks Silver Layer. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']

  Note: 'Master definition of competitive hotel sets used for STR STAR Report benchmarking and competitive rate intelligence. Captures comp set name, property membership, comp set type (primary, secondary, custom), effective date range, and STR property codes. Managed in Infor EzRMS and used to calculate RGI (Revenue Generation Index), MPI (Market Penetration Index), and ARI (Average Rate Index).'
}

Table revenue.group_evaluation {
  group_evaluation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each group business evaluation record generated by IDeaS G3 RMS. Primary key for the group_evaluation data product in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the corporate account, travel agency, or group organizer account associated with this group inquiry, as maintained in Salesforce CRM and Oracle OPERA PMS AR module.']
  applicable_promotion_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.promotion. Business justification: Group displacement analysis must account for active loyalty promotions during the group\'s stay dates, as promotions affect transient demand and displacement cost. Named process: promotion-adjusted gro']
  booking_source_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.booking_source. Business justification: Group evaluations originate from specific booking sources (group desk, travel agent, OTA group portal). FK enables channel-level group business analysis, commission calculation for group bookings, and']
  budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.finance_budget. Business justification: Group evaluations compare displacement cost and net revenue impact against the property\'s financial budget. Revenue managers explicitly reference budgeted transient ADR and room revenue when making gr']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Group RFPs often originate from marketing campaigns targeting corporate/SMIC segments. Linking the campaign helps measure campaign effectiveness in driving qualified group leads and conversion rates.']
  campaign_offer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign_offer. Business justification: Group sales evaluations frequently reference specific campaign offers (e.g., a corporate group requesting a promotional rate tied to an active offer). Revenue managers must validate offer eligibility ']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.channel. Business justification: Group evaluations are channel-specific — the proposed group rate, displacement analysis, and commission terms depend on which distribution channel the group inquiry came through. FK enables channel-le']
  competitive_set_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to revenue.competitive_set. Business justification: group_evaluation.rgi_benchmark (DECIMAL) is a competitive index metric used in group displacement analysis. This metric is computed relative to a specific competitive set. Adding competitive_set_id li']
  decision_maker_member_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.member. Business justification: Group bookings often involve loyalty members as decision-makers, influencing pricing decisions. Business process: tracking member-led group inquiries for loyalty credit attribution and personalized gr']
  demand_forecast_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to revenue.demand_forecast. Business justification: group_evaluation.demand_forecast_occupancy_pct (DECIMAL) is a denormalized copy of the projected occupancy from the demand forecast used in displacement cost calculations. Adding demand_forecast_id li']
  event_booking_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.event_booking. Business justification: Group evaluation in revenue management directly produces an accepted event booking. Revenue managers track which evaluation led to which confirmed booking for displacement analysis, conversion reporti']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Group evaluations assess displacement cost and net revenue impact within a fiscal period context. Revenue managers and finance teams review group business decisions against period budgets; this FK ena']
  function_space_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.function_space. Business justification: Group displacement analysis requires knowing which function spaces are requested to calculate total revenue displacement (rooms + space rental). Revenue managers evaluate function space availability a']
  inquiry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.inquiry. Business justification: Group evaluations are initiated from event inquiries in the standard MICE sales-to-revenue workflow. Linking evaluation to inquiry enables lead-to-evaluation conversion tracking, inquiry qualification']
  ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Group booking decisions impact revenue recognition and require GL account mapping for group revenue tracking, displacement cost accounting, and profitability analysis.']
  market_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to revenue.market_segment. Business justification: Group business evaluations are classified by market segment (e.g., Group Corporate, Group Association, Group SMERF). Adding market_segment_id links each group evaluation to its market segment classifi']
  meeting_space_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.meeting_space. Business justification: Hotel group evaluations assess both room block revenue and meeting space requirements together. Linking group_evaluation to meeting_space enables total group revenue assessment including space rental ']
  negotiated_rate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to revenue.revenue_negotiated_rate. Business justification: Group evaluations for corporate or wholesale accounts may reference an existing negotiated rate agreement as the basis for the proposed group rate. Adding revenue_negotiated_rate_id links the group ev']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the hotel or resort property for which the group evaluation is being conducted. Links to the property master record in the Property domain.']
  proposal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.proposal. Business justification: Revenue managers evaluate group business by reviewing the sales proposal to assess displacement cost and yield impact. The group evaluation record must reference the originating proposal to support MI']
  proposed_banquet_menu_package_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fnb.banquet_menu_package. Business justification: During group evaluation, revenue managers assess the proposed banquet menu package to calculate total group F&B revenue before a BEO is created. The existing primary_banquet_event_order_id covers post']
  rate_availability_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to revenue.rate_availability. Business justification: Group evaluations are conducted against the current rate availability snapshot for the requested stay dates. Adding rate_availability_id links the group evaluation to the specific rate availability re']
  revenue_rate_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to revenue.revenue_rate_plan. Business justification: Group evaluations are performed for specific rate plans. group_evaluation has rate_plan_code (STRING) which should be normalized to FK. N group_evaluation records → 1 revenue_rate_plan. Removes redund']
  seasonal_calendar_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.seasonal_calendar. Business justification: Group displacement calculations require the seasonal transient demand baseline (estimated_occupancy_pct, estimated_adr, demand_classification) from seasonal_calendar. Linking group_evaluation to the s']
  strategy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to revenue.strategy. Business justification: Group business evaluations are made within the context of the active revenue strategy. The strategy defines minimum acceptable rates, target occupancy, and displacement thresholds that directly inform']
  ancillary_revenue_estimate decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated ancillary revenue from the group including spa, parking, audio-visual, business center, and other non-room, non-F&B spend. Used in total spend calculation for net revenue impact assessment.']
  arrival_date date [note: 'Proposed first night of the group room block. Used as the start of the displacement analysis window in IDeaS G3 RMS.']
  attrition_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Expected attrition percentage for the group block by group type, representing the proportion of contracted rooms the group is expected not to use. Distinct from wash factor in that attrition is the contractual exposure, while wash is the statistical pickup adjustment.']
  counter_offer_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'Alternative per-room per-night rate proposed by the revenue manager as a counter-offer to the group organizer when the proposed_group_rate is below the minimum_acceptable_rate. Populated only when revenue_manager_decision = \'counter_offer\'.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp recording when this group evaluation record was first captured in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Used for data lineage and ETL audit trail.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts in this evaluation record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Ensures consistent financial reporting per USALI and IFRS/GAAP standards.']
  cutoff_date date [note: 'The date by which unbooked rooms in the group block are released back to general inventory. After this date, the property may sell unreserved block rooms to transient guests. Critical for inventory management and wash factor actualization.']
  decision_override_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text justification provided by the revenue manager when their decision overrides the RMS recommendation (e.g., strategic account relationship, future business potential, competitive displacement risk). Required when revenue_manager_decision differs from rms_recommendation.']
  decision_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the revenue manager recorded their final accept, decline, or counter-offer decision. Used for SLA tracking and decision audit trail.']
  departure_date date [note: 'Proposed last night of the group room block (checkout date). Defines the end of the displacement analysis window.']
  displacement_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total estimated revenue lost from displaced transient business if the group block is accepted, calculated as transient_rooms_displaced multiplied by transient_adr_displaced across the stay period. Key component of the net revenue impact analysis.']
  evaluation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the group evaluation workflow. Tracks progression from initial inquiry receipt through revenue manager decision. \'pending\' = awaiting RMS analysis; \'under_review\' = revenue manager reviewing; \'recommended_accept\' = RMS recommends acceptance; \'recommended_decline\' = RMS recommends decline; \'accepted\' = revenue manager approved; \'declined\' = revenue manager rejected.']
  evaluation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The principal real-world event timestamp when the group evaluation was initiated and processed by IDeaS G3 RMS. Represents the business event time, distinct from record audit timestamps.']
  fb_revenue_estimate decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated Food and Beverage (F&B) revenue contribution from the group, including banquet, catering, and outlet spend. Sourced from Delphi by Amadeus event catering estimates and historical group F&B spend patterns.']
  group_name varchar [note: 'Name of the group or event associated with the booking inquiry (e.g., \'Acme Corp Annual Sales Conference 2025\'). Used for identification and reporting across Delphi by Amadeus and Oracle OPERA PMS.']
  group_room_revenue decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total projected room revenue from the group block at the proposed group rate, calculated as adjusted room block (post-wash) multiplied by proposed_group_rate across all nights. Gross revenue contribution from rooms only.']
  group_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the group by business segment. Drives wash factor parameters and attrition modeling in IDeaS G3 RMS. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: corporate|association|government|leisure|sports|wedding|social|tour_operator|incentive — promote to reference product]']
  historical_wash_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Historical average wash percentage for this group type and property, derived from past group performance data in IDeaS G3 RMS. Serves as the baseline input before adjustments for lead time and current demand conditions.']
  inquiry_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric reference number assigned to the group booking inquiry, as generated by the Central Reservation System (CRS) or Property Management System (PMS). Used to cross-reference the evaluation with the originating inquiry across Oracle OPERA PMS and Sabre SynXis CRS.']
  is_definite_booking boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the group inquiry has converted to a definite (contracted) booking following a positive revenue manager decision. True = definite booking confirmed; False = still in tentative or evaluation stage.']
  lead_time_bucket varchar [note: 'Categorical bucket representing the number of days between the group inquiry date and the proposed arrival date. Used as a parameter in wash factor modeling, as groups booked further in advance typically exhibit higher wash rates.']
  los_nights varchar [note: 'Total number of nights in the proposed group stay, calculated as departure_date minus arrival_date. Represents the Length of Stay (LOS) for the group block used in displacement and revenue impact modeling.']
  minimum_acceptable_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'The minimum per-room per-night rate recommended by IDeaS G3 RMS below which accepting the group would result in negative net revenue impact after accounting for displacement costs. This is the RMS-generated floor rate for negotiation.']
  net_revenue_impact decimal(12,2) [note: 'Net revenue impact of accepting the group, calculated as total group spend (room + F&B + ancillary) minus displacement cost. Positive value indicates the group is accretive; negative value indicates the group destroys revenue versus transient alternative. Primary decision metric in the evaluation.']
  peak_night_rooms varchar [note: 'Maximum number of rooms occupied on any single night within the group block. Used for capacity planning and displacement analysis at the peak demand point.']
  proposed_group_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'The per-room per-night rate proposed by the group organizer or sales manager for the room block, expressed in the property\'s operating currency. This is the rate under evaluation against the displacement cost and minimum acceptable rate.']
  revenue_manager_decision varchar [note: 'Final decision made by the revenue manager after reviewing the RMS recommendation and displacement analysis. May align with or override the RMS recommendation. \'pending\' indicates no decision has been recorded yet.']
  revpar_impact decimal(10,2) [note: 'Estimated change in Revenue Per Available Room (RevPAR) for the property during the group stay dates if the group is accepted, compared to the transient-only forecast. Negative RevPAR impact is a key indicator for decline recommendation.']
  rgi_benchmark decimal(5,2) [note: 'The property\'s Revenue Generation Index (RGI) from the STR STAR Report for the competitive set during the evaluation period. Used as a competitive context benchmark to assess whether accepting the group at the proposed rate is competitively rational.']
  rms_recommendation varchar [note: 'Automated accept, decline, or counter-offer recommendation generated by IDeaS G3 RMS based on displacement analysis, wash factor modeling, and net revenue impact. This is the system-generated recommendation prior to revenue manager override.']
  room_block_size varchar [note: 'Total number of guest rooms requested in the group block across all nights of the stay. This is the peak or contracted room block size as submitted in the group inquiry.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'The native record identifier from the originating operational system (IDeaS G3 RMS group evaluation ID or Delphi by Amadeus opportunity ID). Enables traceability back to the system of record for audit and reconciliation purposes.']
  transient_adr_displaced decimal(10,2) [note: 'The forecasted Average Daily Rate (ADR) of the transient business that would be displaced by the group block, as estimated by IDeaS G3 RMS. Used to quantify the revenue opportunity cost of accepting the group.']
  transient_rooms_displaced varchar [note: 'Estimated number of transient (individual) room nights that would be displaced if the group block is accepted, as calculated by IDeaS G3 RMS displacement analysis. Core input to the net revenue impact calculation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp recording when this group evaluation record was last modified in the Silver Layer lakehouse, including revenue manager decision updates or status changes.']
  wash_factor_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The adjusted wash factor percentage applied to the contracted room block to estimate actual pickup. Derived from historical wash percentage, group type attrition patterns, and booking lead time bucket. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 15.00 = 15% wash). Used to calculate the adjusted room block for revenue modeling.']

  Note: 'Group business evaluation records generated by IDeaS G3 RMS for group booking inquiries, encompassing the full displacement analysis and wash factor modeling. Captures group inquiry reference, proposed dates, room block size, proposed group rate, displacement cost analysis (transient rooms displaced, transient ADR displaced, net revenue impact, total spend including F&B and ancillary), wash factor parameters (expected attrition percentage by group type, booking lead time bucket, historical wash percentage, adjusted wash factor), recommended minimum rate, accept/decline recommendation, evaluation status, and revenue manager decision. This is the SSOT for all group revenue decision-making including displacement modeling and block attrition forecasting.'
}

Table revenue.pickup_report {
  pickup_report_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each pickup report record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this entity. Entity role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — represents a discrete revenue management snapshot event tracking booking pace against forecast for a given stay date and property.']
  active_promotion_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.promotion. Business justification: Pickup velocity spikes are frequently driven by loyalty promotions; revenue managers correlate booking pace with active promotions in daily pickup reports. Named process: promotion-driven pickup veloc']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Pickup velocity reports are used to measure whether a marketing campaign is driving booking pace — a named revenue management process. Revenue managers monitor pickup reports during campaign flights t']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.channel. Business justification: Pickup reports track booking pace by channel — a core revenue management daily process. FK from pickup_report to channel enables channel-level pickup velocity analysis and channel mix optimization dec']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Pickup velocity and booking pace are tracked by cost center for departmental revenue forecasting and operational planning at the department level.']
  demand_forecast_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to revenue.demand_forecast. Business justification: pickup_report tracks reservation pace against forecast for a given stay date. Adding demand_forecast_id links each pickup report to the specific demand forecast it measures pace against. The forecaste']
  event_booking_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.event_booking. Business justification: Pickup velocity reports track room block pickup rates for specific event bookings against cutoff dates. Essential for group pace reporting, attrition risk assessment, and event booking performance mon']
  event_group_block_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.event_group_block. Business justification: Pickup reports monitor room pickup velocity against group blocks — the event_group_block is the primary source of group room pickup data. Revenue managers use this link to track block-to-pickup ratios']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Pickup reports are used in period-close revenue reconciliation processes. Finance teams require pickup data tagged to fiscal periods for month-end accruals and revenue recognition reporting under USAL']
  fnb_revenue_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fnb.revenue_center. Business justification: Revenue managers run F&B pickup reports segmented by revenue center (restaurant vs. bar vs. banquet) to track F&B pace alongside room pickup for total revenue management. The existing monitored_fnb_ou']
  market_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to revenue.market_segment. Business justification: Pickup reports track booking pace by market segment. pickup_report has segment_code (STRING) which should be normalized to FK. N pickup_report records → 1 market_segment. Removes redundant segment_cod']
  monitored_fnb_outlet_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fnb.fnb_outlet. Business justification: Pickup reports track outlet reservation pace for outlets accepting reservations (fine dining, specialty restaurants). Required for outlet capacity planning, labor scheduling, inventory management alig']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property (hotel, resort, or vacation property) for which this pickup report is generated. Links to the property master record in the Property domain.']
  revenue_rate_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to revenue.revenue_rate_plan. Business justification: pickup_report.rate_plan_code (STRING) is a denormalized reference to the rate plan being tracked. Adding revenue_rate_plan_id normalizes this to the authoritative revenue_rate_plan record. rate_plan_c']
  seasonal_calendar_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.seasonal_calendar. Business justification: Pickup velocity reports are analyzed in the context of seasonal demand periods to assess booking pace relative to seasonal norms. Linking pickup_report to seasonal_calendar enables pace-vs-season benc']
  strategy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to revenue.strategy. Business justification: Pickup reports measure booking pace against the revenue strategy targets for a given stay date. Linking pickup_report to strategy enables revenue managers to assess whether pace is on track to meet st']
  adr_on_books decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average Daily Rate (ADR) of all rooms currently on books for the stay_date as of the report_date. Calculated as total room revenue on books divided by rooms_on_books. Core revenue performance indicator alongside occupancy pace. Expressed in the property\'s operating currency.']
  adr_variance_to_forecast decimal(10,2) [note: 'Difference between adr_on_books and forecasted_adr (adr_on_books minus forecasted_adr). Positive value indicates rate is tracking above forecast; negative value indicates rate is below forecast. Used alongside rooms_variance_to_forecast to assess overall revenue pace.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this pickup report record was first created in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED requirement.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary values in this pickup report record (adr_on_books, forecasted_adr, revpar_on_books, forecasted_revpar, prior_year_adr, adr_variance_to_forecast). Typically the property\'s local operating currency (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  day_of_week varchar [note: 'Day of the week corresponding to the stay_date (e.g., Monday, Friday, Saturday). Critical demand pattern dimension — weekday vs weekend demand profiles differ significantly in hospitality, driving different pricing strategies and pace expectations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Monday|Tuesday|Wednesday|Thursday|Friday|Saturday|Sunday — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  demand_level varchar [note: 'Revenue management system classification of expected demand intensity for the stay_date as of the report_date. Drives pricing strategy and inventory control decisions: \'distressed\' triggers promotional actions; \'peak\' triggers rate maximization and LOS (Length of Stay) restrictions.']
  forecasted_adr decimal(10,2) [note: 'Revenue management system forecast for the expected ADR (Average Daily Rate) on the stay_date, as generated by IDeaS G3 RMS or Infor EzRMS. Baseline against which adr_on_books is compared to assess rate performance vs plan.']
  forecasted_revpar decimal(10,2) [note: 'Revenue management system forecast for RevPAR (Revenue Per Available Room) on the stay_date. Baseline against which revpar_on_books is compared to assess overall revenue pace vs plan. Expressed in the property\'s operating currency.']
  forecasted_rooms varchar [note: 'Revenue management system forecast for total rooms expected to be occupied on the stay_date, as generated by IDeaS G3 RMS or Infor EzRMS at the time of this report snapshot. Baseline against which rooms_on_books pace is measured to compute variance.']
  is_special_event boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the stay_date coincides with a designated special event (conference, concert, sporting event, holiday, local festival) that materially impacts demand. When true, pace expectations and pricing strategies are adjusted relative to normal demand patterns.']
  is_weekend boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the stay_date falls on a weekend (Saturday or Sunday). Boolean flag used to segment pickup reports into weekday vs weekend cohorts for pace analysis, as weekend demand patterns and pricing strategies differ materially from weekday.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between the report_date and the stay_date (stay_date minus report_date). Represents the booking window or days-out at the time of this snapshot. Critical for pace analysis — revenue managers compare pickup velocity across standard lead-time intervals (90, 60, 30, 14, 7, 1 days out).']
  los_restriction_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a LOS (Length of Stay) restriction (minimum stay requirement) is currently active for the stay_date. LOS restrictions are a key revenue management lever applied during high-demand periods to maximize total revenue across multiple nights.']
  min_los varchar [note: 'Minimum number of nights a guest must stay to book the stay_date, when a LOS restriction is active. Null when los_restriction_active is false. Used to enforce stay-through requirements during high-demand periods to prevent short-stay displacement of longer, higher-value bookings.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text annotations entered by the revenue manager to document observations, strategy decisions, or contextual factors affecting the pickup pace for this stay_date (e.g., \'Rate opened on OTA due to slow pace\', \'Group block released — expect transient pickup\'). Supports audit trail and strategy documentation.']
  pickup_velocity decimal(10,4) [note: 'Rate of new reservations being added per day for the stay_date, calculated as the incremental rooms picked up divided by the number of days elapsed in the measurement window. Expressed as rooms per day. Enables revenue managers to assess acceleration or deceleration of booking pace.']
  prior_year_adr decimal(10,2) [note: 'ADR (Average Daily Rate) on books for the equivalent stay_date in the prior year, at the same lead time. Enables year-over-year rate comparison to assess whether current pricing strategy is yielding rate improvement or erosion versus the prior period.']
  prior_year_rooms_on_books varchar [note: 'Number of rooms that were on books for the equivalent stay_date in the prior year, at the same lead time (days out). Enables year-over-year (YOY) pace comparison — a standard revenue management benchmarking technique to contextualize current booking pace against historical performance.']
  report_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which this pickup snapshot was captured. Represents the \'as-of\' date for all rooms-on-books and ADR figures in this record. The difference between stay_date and report_date defines the lead time (days out). Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP requirement (date-precision is standard for daily pickup reports).']
  report_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for this pickup report record, typically formatted as PKP-{PropertyCode}-{StayDate}-{SequenceNumber}. Used by revenue managers to reference specific pickup snapshots in IDeaS G3 RMS and Infor EzRMS reporting. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER requirement.']
  report_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of this pickup report record. \'draft\' indicates the snapshot is being assembled; \'published\' indicates it is available for revenue manager consumption; \'superseded\' indicates a later snapshot for the same stay_date/report_date exists; \'cancelled\' indicates the report was voided. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER LIFECYCLE_STATUS requirement.']
  report_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the pickup report generation cadence: \'daily\' for standard automated daily snapshots, \'weekly\' for weekly pace summaries, \'monthly\' for month-end pace reviews, or \'adhoc\' for analyst-triggered on-demand reports. Determines the reporting frequency context for pace trend analysis.']
  revpar_on_books decimal(10,2) [note: 'Revenue Per Available Room (RevPAR) derived from current on-books position, calculated as (adr_on_books multiplied by rooms_on_books) divided by total_available_rooms. Composite pace metric combining rate and occupancy performance. Expressed in the property\'s operating currency.']
  room_type_code varchar [note: 'Room type classification for which this pickup report is scoped (e.g., STD, DLX, STE, KNG, DBL). When null, the report covers all room types for the property. Enables room-type-level pace analysis to support inventory controls and upsell strategy.']
  rooms_on_books varchar [note: 'Total number of rooms confirmed on books for the stay_date as of the report_date. This is the primary pace indicator — the current committed inventory position. Revenue managers compare this against forecast and prior-year actuals to assess demand trajectory.']
  rooms_on_books_14d varchar [note: 'Number of rooms that were on books for the stay_date when the lead time was 14 days (i.e., the snapshot captured 14 days prior to stay_date). Critical short-window pace indicator used to trigger last-minute pricing tactics and inventory controls.']
  rooms_on_books_1d varchar [note: 'Number of rooms that were on books for the stay_date when the lead time was 1 day (i.e., the snapshot captured 1 day prior to stay_date). Final pre-arrival pace indicator representing near-final committed occupancy before day-of walk-ins.']
  rooms_on_books_30d varchar [note: 'Number of rooms that were on books for the stay_date when the lead time was 30 days (i.e., the snapshot captured 30 days prior to stay_date). Key pace benchmark for near-term demand assessment and rate strategy adjustments.']
  rooms_on_books_60d varchar [note: 'Number of rooms that were on books for the stay_date when the lead time was 60 days (i.e., the snapshot captured 60 days prior to stay_date). Standard pace benchmark used by revenue managers to assess mid-range booking momentum.']
  rooms_on_books_7d varchar [note: 'Number of rooms that were on books for the stay_date when the lead time was 7 days (i.e., the snapshot captured 7 days prior to stay_date). Near-arrival pace indicator used to assess last-week demand and trigger promotional or restriction actions.']
  rooms_on_books_90d varchar [note: 'Number of rooms that were on books for the stay_date when the lead time was 90 days (i.e., the snapshot captured 90 days prior to stay_date). Used as a historical pace benchmark for comparison against current rooms_on_books to measure pickup velocity over the 90-day window.']
  rooms_variance_to_forecast varchar [note: 'Difference between rooms_on_books and forecasted_rooms (rooms_on_books minus forecasted_rooms). Positive value indicates ahead-of-pace (over-performing vs forecast); negative value indicates behind-pace (under-performing). Primary KPI for revenue manager intervention decisions.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this pickup report data was sourced (IDeaS_G3_RMS, Infor_EzRMS, OPERA_PMS, or Manual for analyst-entered overrides). Enables data lineage tracking and source-system reconciliation in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  special_event_name varchar [note: 'Name or description of the special event associated with the stay_date when is_special_event is true (e.g., \'Super Bowl Weekend\', \'Annual Tech Conference\', \'New Year\'s Eve\'). Provides context for anomalous pace patterns in revenue management reporting.']
  stay_date date [note: 'The specific calendar date for which booking pace and pickup velocity are being measured. This is the future arrival/occupancy date that revenue managers are monitoring. Central dimension of the pickup report — all rooms-on-books and ADR snapshots relate to this date.']
  total_available_rooms varchar [note: 'Total number of rooms available for sale for the stay_date at the property (net of out-of-order and out-of-service rooms). Denominator for occupancy rate (OCC) calculation and RevPAR computation. Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS inventory controls.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this pickup report record was last modified in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED requirement.']

  Note: 'Reservation pickup tracking records measuring the pace of bookings against forecast for a given stay date and property. Captures stay date, report date, rooms on books at various lead-time snapshots (90-day, 60-day, 30-day, 14-day, 7-day, 1-day), ADR on books, RevPAR on books, pickup velocity, and variance to forecast. Critical for revenue managers to assess booking pace and adjust pricing strategy.'
}

Table revenue.negotiated_rate {
  negotiated_rate_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the negotiated rate agreement record in the lakehouse Silver layer. Primary key for this entity.']

  campaign_offer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign_offer. Business justification: Negotiated rates are sometimes tied to specific campaign offers (e.g., consortia rates bundled with a promotional offer). Rate loading process requires referencing the campaign offer for offer code va']

  distribution_channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.channel. Business justification: Corporate negotiated rates are loaded to specific channels (GDS with specific chain codes, OTA corporate programs, direct booking engines). Rate loading and distribution tracking requires knowing whic']
  event_booking_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.event_booking. Business justification: Bespoke negotiated rates are frequently created for a single large event booking (e.g., a corporate conference). Linking the negotiated rate to the specific event booking enables rate loading validati']
  event_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.event_contract. Business justification: Negotiated rates are formalized and referenced in event contracts. Linking the negotiated rate record to the event contract enables contract compliance auditing, rate loading verification against cont']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Corporate and consortia negotiated rates are loaded and audited by fiscal year for rate parity compliance, commission reconciliation, and financial reporting. Finance teams require fiscal period taggi']
  franchise_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.franchise_agreement. Business justification: Negotiated rates at franchised properties must comply with franchise agreement brand standards, minimum rate requirements, and loyalty fee obligations. Linking revenue_negotiated_rate to franchise_agr']
  included_fnb_outlet_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fnb.fnb_outlet. Business justification: Corporate negotiated rates include outlet benefits (breakfast at specific outlet, lounge access). Required for contract fulfillment, benefit tracking, outlet revenue attribution, guest entitlement ver']
  included_menu_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fnb.menu. Business justification: Corporate and consortia negotiated rate contracts that include F&B (e.g., breakfast-inclusive corporate rates) specify the exact menu included. This is a contractual element — rate loading in the CRS/']
  ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Negotiated corporate rates post to specific GL accounts for contract revenue tracking, corporate account profitability analysis, and revenue recognition compliance.']
  market_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to revenue.market_segment. Business justification: Negotiated rates are classified by market segment (e.g., Corporate, Wholesale). revenue_negotiated_rate has market_segment_code (STRING) which should be normalized to FK. N negotiated_rate records → 1']
  minimum_loyalty_tier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.tier. Business justification: Corporate negotiated rates often include tier-based eligibility or enhanced benefits. Business process: enforcing tier requirements for corporate rate access, common in negotiated contracts that offer']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to experience.program. Business justification: Corporate negotiated rates frequently bundle specific experience programs (e.g., executive lounge access, welcome amenity). Linking negotiated rates to experience programs enables contract compliance ']
  promotion_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.promotion. Business justification: Negotiated corporate rates are sometimes bundled with loyalty promotions (e.g., bonus points for corporate travelers on negotiated rates). Named process: promotion-enhanced negotiated rate configurati']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property for which this negotiated rate agreement applies. A single negotiated rate may be scoped to one property or replicated per property for multi-property contracts.']
  revenue_rate_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent rate plan definition under which this negotiated rate is structured. Links to the rate plan master in Sabre SynXis CRS or Oracle OPERA PMS.']
  room_type_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific room type to which this negotiated rate applies. Null if the rate applies to all room types under the agreement.']
  strategy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to revenue.strategy. Business justification: Negotiated rate agreements are established within the context of the revenue strategy (e.g., LRA rates, corporate rates aligned to strategy targets). Adding strategy_id links each negotiated rate to t']
  travel_agent_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reservation.travel_agent. Business justification: Negotiated rates are contracted with specific travel agents. Linking revenue_negotiated_rate to travel_agent enables rate loading verification, commission reconciliation, and contract management. reve']
  advance_booking_days varchar [note: 'Minimum number of days in advance a reservation must be made to qualify for this negotiated rate. Zero indicates same-day booking is permitted.']
  amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'The contracted nightly room rate (Average Daily Rate) agreed upon for this account. Represents the fixed or base rate per room per night in the specified currency. Core pricing field for revenue management and contract compliance.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval workflow status for this negotiated rate agreement. Rates must be approved by revenue management leadership before loading into CRS/GDS channels.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the revenue management leader or director who approved this negotiated rate agreement for distribution.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this negotiated rate agreement received final approval for loading and distribution.']
  blackout_dates varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or date range expressions representing dates on which this negotiated rate is not available (e.g., peak demand periods, holidays, special events). Stored as a structured string; detailed blackout records are managed in the rate_restriction product.']
  breakfast_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether breakfast is included in the negotiated rate. Affects F&B (Food and Beverage) revenue allocation and rate value proposition reporting.']
  commission_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Commission percentage payable to the TMC, travel agency, or consortia for reservations booked under this negotiated rate. Expressed as a percentage of the negotiated rate amount.']
  committed_room_nights varchar [note: 'Total number of room nights the account has committed to book under this negotiated rate agreement for the contract period. Basis for volume discount qualification and contract performance tracking.']
  consortia_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the travel consortia (e.g., Virtuoso, Signature, Ensemble, Internova) under which this negotiated rate is offered. Null for direct corporate accounts not affiliated with a consortia.']
  contract_end_date date [note: 'The date on which the negotiated rate agreement expires and the rate is no longer available for booking. Null for open-ended agreements. Aligns with the MASTER_AGREEMENT EFFECTIVE_UNTIL category.']
  contract_reference_number varchar [note: 'External contract or agreement reference number assigned by the account, TMC, or legal department. Used for contract management, audit, and dispute resolution.']
  contract_start_date date [note: 'The date from which the negotiated rate agreement becomes contractually binding and the rate is available for booking. Aligns with the MASTER_AGREEMENT EFFECTIVE_FROM category.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this negotiated rate record was first created in the source system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and GDPR/CCPA data lifecycle management.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the negotiated rate amount is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency property portfolios.']
  gds_chain_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter GDS chain code used to identify the hotel brand/chain in Sabre, Amadeus, Galileo, and Worldspan for rate loading and distribution of this negotiated rate.']
  iata_number varchar [note: 'IATA-assigned numeric identifier for the Travel Management Company (TMC) or travel agency associated with this negotiated rate. Used for commission tracking and GDS rate access validation.']
  is_lra boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this negotiated rate carries a Last Room Availability (LRA) obligation, requiring the property to honor the contracted rate as long as any room of the applicable type remains available, regardless of demand or yield management controls.']
  is_non_refundable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether reservations booked under this negotiated rate are non-refundable (NRR). Affects cancellation policy enforcement and revenue recognition timing.']
  max_los varchar [note: 'Maximum number of consecutive nights allowed per reservation under this negotiated rate. Null if no upper limit is imposed.']
  min_los varchar [note: 'Minimum number of consecutive nights required per reservation to qualify for this negotiated rate. Enforced at booking in Sabre SynXis CRS and Oracle OPERA PMS.']
  min_night_commitment varchar [note: 'Minimum number of room nights the account is contractually committed to consume during the contract period. Used for volume tracking and contract compliance reporting.']
  negotiation_owner varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the revenue manager or sales manager responsible for negotiating and managing this rate agreement. Used for accountability and performance tracking.']
  rate_bar_variance_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage discount or premium of this negotiated rate relative to the Best Available Rate (BAR) at time of contract negotiation. Used by revenue management to assess rate competitiveness and yield impact.']
  rate_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric rate code used to identify and load this negotiated rate in Sabre SynXis CRS and Oracle OPERA PMS. This is the externally-known business identifier referenced in GDS/CRS rate loading and reservation transactions.']
  rate_includes_tax boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the negotiated rate amount is inclusive of applicable taxes. Critical for revenue recognition, financial reporting, and guest billing accuracy.']
  rate_loaded_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the negotiated rate was successfully loaded into Sabre SynXis CRS and/or Oracle OPERA PMS for distribution. Null if loading has not yet occurred.']
  rate_loading_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the rate loading process in Sabre SynXis CRS and Oracle OPERA PMS. Indicates whether the negotiated rate has been successfully distributed to all applicable booking channels.']
  rate_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name describing the negotiated rate agreement, typically referencing the account or program name (e.g., \'Acme Corp 2025 Corporate Rate\'). Used in reporting and rate management dashboards.']
  rate_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the negotiated rate agreement. Controls whether the rate is bookable and distributed through CRS/GDS channels.']
  rate_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the pricing structure for this negotiated rate. Fixed rates are static for the contract period; dynamic rates may fluctuate within agreed bounds; tiered rates vary by volume; per diem rates are government-mandated ceiling rates.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this negotiated rate record originated. Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation across Oracle OPERA PMS, Sabre SynXis CRS, and Salesforce CRM.']
  source_system_rate_reference varchar [note: 'Native identifier of this negotiated rate record in the originating operational system (Oracle OPERA PMS, Sabre SynXis CRS, or Salesforce CRM). Enables traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse and source systems.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this negotiated rate record was last modified in the source system. Supports change tracking, audit compliance, and incremental ETL processing.']

  Note: 'Corporate and wholesale negotiated rate agreements defining contracted pricing for specific accounts, travel management companies (TMCs), and consortia. Captures account identifier, rate code, negotiated rate amount, LRA (Last Room Availability) obligation, blackout dates, minimum night commitment, contract period, and rate loading status in Sabre SynXis CRS and Oracle OPERA PMS. Distinct from public rate plans — these are account-specific contracted rates.'
}

Table revenue.strategy {
  strategy_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for strategy']
  aligned_promotion_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.promotion. Business justification: Revenue strategies are built around loyalty promotion periods (e.g., tier challenge windows, bonus-points weekends). Named process: loyalty-aligned revenue strategy planning. Strategy documents must r']
  budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.finance_budget. Business justification: Revenue strategies are aligned with financial budgets for strategic planning, ensuring revenue tactics support approved financial targets and ownership expectations.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Revenue strategies often incorporate specific marketing campaigns as tactical levers (e.g., deploy summer family campaign to drive weekend occupancy). Strategy owners need this link for execution trac']
  competitive_set_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the competitive set definition used for benchmarking this strategy. The competitor set defines the peer properties against which RGI, MPI, and ARI targets are measured via STR STAR Report.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Revenue strategies are implemented at cost center level for tactical execution, departmental accountability, and operational performance management.']
  demand_forecast_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the demand forecast record that informed and underpins this revenue strategy. Links to the revenue.demand_forecast product to provide the analytical basis for strategy targets and tactical decisions.']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Revenue strategies are anchored to fiscal planning periods for budget cycle alignment, target-setting (RevPAR, GOPPAR), and period-close variance reporting. A travel-hospitality revenue manager always']
  gss_score_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to experience.gss_score. Business justification: Revenue strategies explicitly target GSS improvement as a demand driver (experience-driven revenue strategy). Strategic plans link rate positioning to satisfaction goals—properties pursuing rate growt']
  hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.hierarchy. Business justification: Revenue strategies are defined at multiple organizational levels (property, region, brand portfolio) aligned with hierarchy nodes. A hierarchy_id on strategy enables regional and portfolio-level prici']
  hma_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.hma_contract. Business justification: Revenue strategies must be calibrated against HMA performance test thresholds and fee structures. Revenue managers reference HMA terms (base fee basis, incentive fee triggers) when setting target RevP']
  market_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to revenue.market_segment. Business justification: strategy.primary_market_segment_focus (STRING) is a denormalized reference to the primary market segment the strategy targets. Adding market_segment_id normalizes this to the authoritative market_segm']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Revenue strategies in full-service hotels are scoped to profit centers (rooms, F&B, spa, events) for GOPPAR and TRevPAR targeting. Finance and revenue management jointly set profit-center-level strate']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to experience.program. Business justification: Revenue strategies are frequently built around specific guest experience programs (e.g., a luxury package strategy tied to a curated experience program). Linking strategy to program enables revenue im']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the hotel or resort property to which this revenue strategy applies. Links to the property master record in Oracle OPERA PMS.']
  seasonal_calendar_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.seasonal_calendar. Business justification: Revenue managers build pricing strategies around seasonal demand periods defined in seasonal_calendar (demand_classification, estimated_revpar, blackout dates). Linking strategy to the seasonal period']
  superseded_by_strategy_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the newer revenue strategy record that replaced this one when status is \'superseded\'. Enables lineage tracking of strategy evolution and supports audit trail for revenue governance.']
  target_tier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.tier. Business justification: Revenue strategies are often tier-targeted (e.g., drive Platinum member bookings in shoulder season, protect Gold tier inventory). Named process: tier-targeted revenue strategy planning and execution.']
  activated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the strategy status transitioned to \'active\' and began governing live pricing and inventory decisions. Distinct from approval timestamp — a strategy may be approved in advance and activated at a later point.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the Revenue Director or authorized revenue management leader who formally approved this strategy. Provides governance and audit trail for strategic revenue decisions per SOX internal controls requirements.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the revenue strategy was formally approved by the authorizing Revenue Director. Establishes the official approval event in the strategy lifecycle for governance and audit trail purposes.']
  channel_focus varchar [note: 'The primary distribution channel(s) this strategy targets for demand generation (e.g., \'Direct Web\', \'GDS\', \'OTA\', \'Voice/CRS\', \'Corporate Direct\'). Informs channel mix optimization and OTA partnership management decisions. Aligns with Sabre SynXis CRS channel codes.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this revenue strategy record was first created in the system. Provides the audit trail creation anchor for the strategy lifecycle.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary target values in this strategy record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Ensures consistent financial reporting across multi-currency property portfolios.']
  horizon_type varchar [note: 'Categorizes the temporal scope of the strategy planning horizon. \'weekly\' for short-term tactical adjustments, \'monthly\' for rolling monthly plans, \'quarterly\' for seasonal strategies, \'annual\' for full-year budget-aligned strategies, \'event_based\' for strategies tied to specific demand events.']
  inventory_control_approach varchar [note: 'The approved inventory management methodology for this strategy period. \'open\' allows all bookings, \'hurdle_rate\' applies minimum rate thresholds, \'length_of_stay\' (LOS) enforces minimum/maximum stay restrictions, \'nested\' uses tiered availability controls, \'closed\' restricts availability for specific segments or channels.']
  key_tactical_actions varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative documenting the specific tactical actions approved as part of this strategy (e.g., \'Open BAR for all channels on shoulder nights\', \'Apply 5-night minimum LOS restriction over peak weekend\', \'Close OTA channels when occupancy exceeds 85%\'). Provides governance documentation for revenue decisions.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'The scheduled date for the next formal performance review of this revenue strategy. Drives workflow triggers in the revenue management calendar and ensures timely strategy adjustments based on actual vs. target performance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text commentary, rationale, or contextual notes provided by the revenue management team regarding this strategy. May include market intelligence, competitive observations, or risk considerations not captured in structured fields.']
  planning_horizon_end_date date [note: 'The last date of the planning period covered by this revenue strategy. Together with planning_horizon_start_date, defines the full strategy window for yield optimization and performance tracking.']
  planning_horizon_start_date date [note: 'The first date of the planning period covered by this revenue strategy. Defines the beginning of the stay-date window over which the strategy governs pricing and inventory decisions.']
  pricing_approach varchar [note: 'The approved pricing methodology governing rate-setting for this strategy period. \'bar_based\' uses Best Available Rate (BAR) as the anchor, \'dynamic\' applies algorithmic Dynamic Rate Rules (DRR), \'fixed\' uses static rate plans, \'hybrid\' combines approaches, \'competitive_parity\' aligns rates to competitive set benchmarks.']
  review_frequency varchar [note: 'The approved cadence at which this strategy is scheduled for performance review and potential revision by the revenue management team. Governs the operational rhythm of the revenue management process.']
  rms_strategy_reference_code varchar [note: 'The native strategy or scenario identifier from the source Revenue Management System (IDeaS G3 RMS or Infor EzRMS). Enables traceability back to the originating RMS record for reconciliation and system-of-record alignment.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this strategy record originated. \'IDeaS_G3_RMS\' for strategies generated by IDeaS G3, \'Infor_EzRMS\' for Infor-sourced strategies, \'Manual\' for strategies entered directly by revenue management staff.']
  special_event_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this strategy was created in response to a specific demand-driving special event (e.g., major conference, sporting event, concert, holiday period). When True, the strategy may include event-specific pricing premiums and inventory controls.']
  special_event_name varchar [note: 'Name of the demand-driving special event associated with this strategy when special_event_flag is True (e.g., \'Super Bowl LX\', \'Annual Medical Conference\', \'New Year Holiday Period\'). Provides context for strategy rationale and post-event performance analysis.']
