  strategy_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this revenue strategy record within the Revenue Management System (RMS). Used for cross-system reference and audit trail. Sourced from IDeaS G3 RMS or Infor EzRMS strategy identifiers.']
  strategy_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name describing the revenue strategy (e.g., \'Q3 2025 Transient Yield Push\', \'Holiday Season Total Revenue Strategy\'). Used for identification in reporting and governance workflows.']
  strategy_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the revenue strategy record. \'draft\' indicates work-in-progress, \'pending_approval\' awaits revenue director sign-off, \'approved\' is authorized but not yet in effect, \'active\' is currently governing pricing decisions, \'superseded\' has been replaced by a newer strategy, \'archived\' is retained for historical audit.']
  strategy_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the revenue strategy by business segment. \'transient\' covers individual guest pricing, \'group\' covers group block strategies, \'total_revenue\' encompasses all revenue streams (TRevPAR focus), \'food_and_beverage\' targets F&B revenue optimization, \'events\' covers MICE and event revenue. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: transient|group|total_revenue|food_and_beverage|events|loyalty — promote to reference product if additional types emerge]']
  target_adr decimal(10,2) [note: 'The approved target Average Daily Rate (ADR) in the property\'s operating currency for the strategy period. ADR is the primary pricing KPI representing average revenue earned per occupied room per night. Used to benchmark actual ADR performance against strategic intent.']
  target_ari decimal(6,3) [note: 'The approved target Average Rate Index (ARI) for the strategy period. ARI = Property ADR / Competitive Set ADR. Measures the property\'s rate positioning relative to its competitive set. Used alongside MPI and RGI for comprehensive competitive benchmarking.']
  target_goppar decimal(10,2) [note: 'The approved target Gross Operating Profit Per Available Room (GOPPAR) for the strategy period. GOPPAR incorporates operating costs and provides a profitability-adjusted view of revenue performance, aligned with USALI GOP reporting standards.']
  target_mpi decimal(6,3) [note: 'The approved target Market Penetration Index (MPI) for the strategy period. MPI = Property Occupancy / Competitive Set Occupancy. Measures the property\'s occupancy share relative to its competitive set as defined in STR STAR Report.']
  target_occupancy_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The approved target Occupancy Rate (OCC) expressed as a percentage (0.00–100.00) for the strategy period. Represents the proportion of available rooms expected to be sold. Core input to RevPAR calculation and yield optimization decisions.']
  target_revpar decimal(10,2) [note: 'The approved target Revenue Per Available Room (RevPAR) in the property\'s operating currency for the strategy period. RevPAR = ADR × Occupancy Rate. The primary composite KPI for hotel revenue performance and competitive benchmarking via STR STAR Report.']
  target_rgi decimal(6,3) [note: 'The approved target Revenue Generation Index (RGI) for the strategy period. RGI = Property RevPAR / Competitive Set RevPAR. An RGI above 1.0 indicates the property is outperforming its competitive set. Sourced from STR STAR Report benchmarking data.']
  target_trevpar decimal(10,2) [note: 'The approved target Total Revenue Per Available Room (TRevPAR) encompassing all revenue streams (rooms, F&B, spa, events, ancillary) per available room for the strategy period. Relevant for total revenue strategies beyond rooms-only yield optimization.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the last person to modify this strategy record. Supports change management and audit trail for revenue governance.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this revenue strategy record was last modified. Tracks the most recent change event for audit trail and data lineage purposes in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Monotonically incrementing version number for this strategy record. Increments each time the strategy is revised and re-approved. Supports audit trail and change management for revenue governance workflows.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the revenue management professional who created this strategy record. Supports governance, accountability, and audit trail requirements.']

  Note: 'Revenue strategy records documenting the approved pricing and inventory strategy for a property over a defined planning horizon. Captures strategy period, property, strategy type (transient, group, total revenue), target ADR, target occupancy, target RevPAR, key tactical actions, strategy status (draft, approved, active), and approving revenue director. Provides governance and audit trail for strategic revenue decisions.'
}

Table loyalty.member {
  member_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the loyalty program member. Primary key.']
  enrollment_cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Member acquisition costs are allocated to the enrolling property\'s cost center for departmental P&L. Marketing ROI analysis and CAC (customer acquisition cost) reporting by department require linking ']
  marketing_guest_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.guest_segment. Business justification: Members are assigned to marketing segments for targeted campaign delivery, personalization, and offer optimization. Essential for marketing operations to identify which loyalty members belong to which']
  member_enrollment_hotel_property_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the property where the member enrolled in the loyalty program, if enrollment occurred at a physical location.']
  member_guest_profile_id varchar [note: 'FK to guest.profile.profile_id — MUST-HAVE: Loyalty member must resolve to guest identity for personalization, stay history correlation, and profile merge operations. Without this FK, loyalty benefits cannot be applied at check-in.']
  member_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the guest domain. Links the loyalty member to their guest profile which owns PII and stay history.']
  member_property_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the property that the member visits most frequently or has designated as their preferred location.']
  tier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the loyalty tier product. Represents the current tier assignment (e.g., Silver, Gold, Platinum, Elite).']
  account_closure_date date [note: 'Date when the membership account was closed or terminated. Null for active accounts.']
  account_closure_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for membership account closure. Used for churn analysis and program improvement.']
  communication_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member has opted in to receive marketing communications and promotional offers from the loyalty program.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the member record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  currency_preference varchar [note: 'Member\'s preferred currency for displaying points value and pricing. Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code.']
  current_points_balance varchar [note: 'Current available points balance that can be redeemed by the member. Reflects earned points minus redeemed and expired points.']
  email_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member has consented to receive email communications. Separate from general communication opt-in for channel-specific consent.']
  enrollment_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the member enrolled in the loyalty program. Used for channel effectiveness analysis.']
  enrollment_date date [note: 'Date when the guest enrolled in the loyalty program. Marks the beginning of the member lifecycle.']
  language_preference varchar [note: 'Member\'s preferred language for communications and service delivery. Three-letter ISO 639-2 language code.']
  last_stay_date date [note: 'Date of the member\'s most recent completed stay. Used for recency analysis and churn prediction.']
  lifetime_nights varchar [note: 'Total number of room nights stayed by the member since enrollment. Used for tier qualification and LTV (Lifetime Value) calculation.']
  lifetime_points_earned varchar [note: 'Total cumulative points earned by the member since enrollment. Used for tier qualification and lifetime value tracking.']
  lifetime_revenue decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total revenue generated by the member across all stays, F&B (Food and Beverage), and ancillary services since enrollment. Key LTV (Lifetime Value) metric.']
  lifetime_stays varchar [note: 'Total number of completed stays by the member since enrollment. Key metric for member engagement and tier qualification.']
  membership_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique membership identifier assigned to the loyalty program member. Used for member recognition and program transactions.']
  membership_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the loyalty membership account. Active members can accrue and redeem points; suspended or closed accounts have restricted access.']
  next_stay_date date [note: 'Date of the member\'s next confirmed reservation. Used for engagement tracking and personalized offers.']
  nps_score varchar [note: 'Most recent NPS (Net Promoter Score) rating provided by the member. Scale of -100 to 100, measuring likelihood to recommend the brand.']
  partner_sharing_consent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member has consented to share their data with loyalty program partners (airlines, car rentals, etc.) for cross-program benefits.']
  points_expired varchar [note: 'Total cumulative points that have expired due to inactivity or program rules.']
  points_redeemed varchar [note: 'Total cumulative points redeemed by the member for rewards, upgrades, or benefits since enrollment.']
  preferred_room_type varchar [note: 'Member\'s preferred room type for reservations. Used for personalized booking experiences and upgrade offers.']
  salt_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'SALT (Satisfaction and Loyalty Tracking) composite score measuring member satisfaction and loyalty. Proprietary metric combining multiple engagement and satisfaction indicators.']
  sms_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member has consented to receive SMS/text message communications.']
  tier_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the member\'s current tier status expires. Used for tier renewal campaigns and retention efforts.']
  tier_qualification_date date [note: 'Date when the member qualified for their current tier. Used for tier anniversary tracking and benefit eligibility.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the member record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  vip_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member is designated as a VIP for special recognition and service. May be based on tier, LTV (Lifetime Value), or manual assignment.']
  ytd_nights varchar [note: 'Number of room nights stayed by the member in the current calendar year. Used for annual tier qualification.']
  ytd_revenue decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total revenue generated by the member in the current calendar year. Used for tier qualification and annual performance tracking.']
  ytd_stays varchar [note: 'Number of completed stays by the member in the current calendar year. Used for annual tier qualification.']

  Note: 'Core master entity representing a guest enrolled in the loyalty program. Captures membership identity, enrollment date, current tier assignment (FK to tier), lifetime points balance, LTV metrics (total revenue, stays, nights), SALT scores, program opt-in preferences, communication consent, membership number, and account status (active, suspended, closed). This is the SSOT for loyalty membership identity and current member state — distinct from the guest profile in the guest domain, which owns PII and stay history. References guest_id as a foreign key to the guest domain. Also serves as the anchor for member segment assignments (segment memberships tracked temporally within member_segment).'
}

Table loyalty.tier {
  tier_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the loyalty program tier. Primary key.']
  program_config_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.program_config. Business justification: Tier definitions are part of program configuration - the tier structure is defined at the program level. Adding program_config_id links tier definitions to their governing program, enabling program-le']
  bonus_points_on_enrollment varchar [note: 'Number of bonus loyalty points awarded to a member upon first achieving this tier status.']
  breakfast_benefit_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether members at this tier receive complimentary breakfast during their stays.']
  color_code varchar [note: 'Hexadecimal color code used for visual representation of the tier in digital interfaces and marketing materials (e.g., #C0C0C0 for Silver, #FFD700 for Gold).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this tier record was first created in the system.']
  dedicated_support_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether members at this tier have access to a dedicated customer service line or concierge team.']
  display_order varchar [note: 'Numeric sort order for displaying tiers in user interfaces and reports. Lower numbers appear first.']
  downgrade_grace_period_months varchar [note: 'Number of months of grace period provided before downgrading a member who no longer meets qualification thresholds.']
  early_checkin_hours varchar [note: 'Number of hours before standard check-in time that members at this tier are entitled to for early check-in (e.g., 2 for 1pm check-in when standard is 3pm).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this tier definition expires or was discontinued. Null for currently active tiers with no planned end date.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this tier definition became or will become active and available for member assignment.']
  guaranteed_availability_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether members at this tier have guaranteed room availability when booking within specified advance notice periods.']
  icon_url varchar [note: 'URL or path to the icon or badge image representing this tier in digital channels.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this tier record was last updated.']
  late_checkout_hours varchar [note: 'Number of hours past standard checkout time that members at this tier are entitled to for late checkout (e.g., 2 for 2pm checkout when standard is 12pm).']
  lounge_access_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether members at this tier have complimentary access to executive lounges or club floors at participating properties.']
  points_earning_multiplier decimal(5,2) [note: 'Multiplier applied to base points earning for members at this tier (e.g., 1.5 means 50% bonus points, 2.0 means double points).']
  priority_reservation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether members at this tier receive priority handling for reservations and waitlist requests.']
  qualification_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the qualification spend threshold (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  qualification_nights_threshold varchar [note: 'Minimum number of qualifying nights required to achieve or maintain this tier status within the qualification period.']
  qualification_period_months varchar [note: 'Number of months over which qualification activity is measured (e.g., 12 for annual qualification, 24 for rolling two-year window).']
  qualification_points_threshold varchar [note: 'Minimum number of loyalty points required to achieve or maintain this tier status within the qualification period.']
  qualification_spend_threshold decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum monetary spend amount required to achieve or maintain this tier status within the qualification period. Typically measured in the program\'s base currency.']
  qualification_stays_threshold varchar [note: 'Minimum number of qualifying stays required to achieve or maintain this tier status within the qualification period.']
  room_upgrade_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether members at this tier are eligible for complimentary room upgrades at check-in (subject to availability).']
  tier_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the tier (e.g., SILVER, GOLD, PLATINUM, DIAMOND). Used for system integration and display.']
  tier_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the tier, including benefits summary and value proposition, used for member communications and marketing.']
  tier_level varchar [note: 'Numeric ranking of the tier within the program hierarchy. Lower numbers indicate entry-level tiers, higher numbers indicate elite status. Used for tier comparison and upgrade logic.']
  tier_name varchar [note: 'Full display name of the loyalty tier (e.g., Silver Elite, Gold Premier, Platinum Honors, Diamond Ambassador).']
  tier_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the tier. Active tiers are available for member assignment; inactive tiers are temporarily suspended; discontinued tiers are retired but may have legacy members.']
  validity_months varchar [note: 'Number of months that tier status remains valid once achieved, before re-qualification is required (e.g., 12 for annual renewal, 24 for two-year validity).']
  welcome_amenity_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether members at this tier receive a welcome amenity (e.g., fruit basket, beverage credit) upon arrival.']

  Note: 'Reference master defining the loyalty program tier hierarchy (e.g., Silver, Gold, Platinum, Diamond). Captures tier name, tier code, qualification thresholds (nights, stays, points, spend), benefit entitlements, upgrade/downgrade rules, tier validity period, and display order. Governs tier assignment logic and benefit eligibility across the program.'
}

Table loyalty.tier_history {
  tier_history_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this tier status change record. Primary key for the tier history entity.']
  member_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the loyalty member who experienced this tier status change.']
  new_tier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.tier. Business justification: tier_history has new_tier_code (STRING) but no FK to the tier entity. Adding new_tier_id FK formalizes the link to the tier entity for the new tier, enabling tier-level history analysis and ensuring r']
  promotion_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.promotion. Business justification: tier_history has promotional_campaign_code (STRING) for tier changes resulting from promotional campaigns. Adding promotion_id FK formalizes this relationship, enabling promotion-driven tier change tr']
  bonus_points_awarded decimal(18,2) [note: 'Number of bonus loyalty points awarded to the member as a welcome benefit or incentive for achieving this new tier status.']
  change_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for this tier change event, used for external reference and audit tracking.']
  change_reason_code varchar [note: 'Categorical code indicating the business reason for this tier status change.']
  change_reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative explanation of why this tier change occurred, including any special circumstances or manual intervention rationale.']
  change_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when this tier status change was processed and recorded in the system.']
  change_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the directional nature of this tier change relative to the tier hierarchy.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which the new tier status became active and benefits became available to the member.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which this tier status is scheduled to expire, requiring requalification or resulting in downgrade.']
  is_current_tier_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this tier history record represents the members current active tier status (true) or a historical tier status (false).']
  notification_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a notification (email, SMS, app push) was successfully sent to the member informing them of this tier change.']
  notification_sent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the tier change notification was sent to the member.']
  override_approval_code varchar [note: 'Authorization or approval code for manual tier overrides, used for audit and compliance tracking of non-standard tier grants.']
  override_justification varchar [note: 'Detailed business justification provided by the approving authority for manual tier status overrides.']
  previous_tier_code varchar [note: 'The tier status code the member held immediately before this change (e.g., SILVER, GOLD, PLATINUM).']
  qualification_basis varchar [note: 'The primary metric or criteria used to determine eligibility for this tier change (nights, stays, points, spend, or combination thereof). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: qualifying_nights|qualifying_stays|qualifying_points|qualifying_spend|combination|status_match|manual — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  qualifying_nights_achieved varchar [note: 'Total number of qualifying nights the member accumulated during the qualifying period that contributed to this tier change.']
  qualifying_period_end_date date [note: 'The end date of the measurement period during which qualifying activity was accumulated for this tier change.']
  qualifying_period_start_date date [note: 'The start date of the measurement period during which qualifying activity was accumulated for this tier change.']
  qualifying_points_achieved decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total number of qualifying loyalty points the member earned during the qualifying period that contributed to this tier change.']
  qualifying_spend_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary spend amount in the program currency that the member accumulated during the qualifying period that contributed to this tier change.']
  qualifying_spend_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the qualifying spend amount.']
  qualifying_stays_achieved varchar [note: 'Total number of qualifying stays the member completed during the qualifying period that contributed to this tier change.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this tier history record was first created in the lakehouse silver layer.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this tier history record was last modified in the lakehouse silver layer.']
  rollover_nights_granted varchar [note: 'Number of qualifying nights carried forward or granted as a tier benefit to assist with future requalification.']
  source_record_reference varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of this tier change record in the source operational system, used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record from which this tier history record originated (e.g., SFDC_CRM, OPERA_PMS).']
  status_match_source_program varchar [note: 'Name of the external loyalty program from which status was matched, if this tier change resulted from a competitive status match offer.']
  status_match_source_tier varchar [note: 'The tier level held by the member in the external program that was matched to grant this tier status.']
  threshold_nights_required varchar [note: 'The minimum number of qualifying nights required to achieve or maintain the new tier status at the time of this change.']
  threshold_points_required decimal(18,2) [note: 'The minimum number of qualifying points required to achieve or maintain the new tier status at the time of this change.']
  threshold_spend_required decimal(18,2) [note: 'The minimum monetary spend amount required to achieve or maintain the new tier status at the time of this change.']
  threshold_stays_required varchar [note: 'The minimum number of qualifying stays required to achieve or maintain the new tier status at the time of this change.']
  tier_extension_granted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an extension of the tier expiry date was granted as part of this change (e.g., due to service recovery, pandemic policy, or loyalty gesture).']
  tier_extension_months varchar [note: 'Number of months by which the tier expiry date was extended beyond the standard qualification period.']
  triggered_by_system varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the system that initiated or triggered this tier change (e.g., Salesforce CRM, batch requalification job, manual override interface).']

  Note: 'Transactional record of every tier status change for a loyalty member, including the qualification evidence that triggered the change. Captures previous tier, new tier, effective date, expiry date, qualification basis (nights/stays/points/spend achieved), qualifying period, qualifying activity summary (total qualifying nights, stays, spend in period), change reason (earned upgrade, annual requalification, downgrade, manual override, status match), and the agent or system that triggered the change. This is the SSOT for tier progression audit trail and requalification decisions — replacing the need for a separate qualifying_activity entity.'
}

Table loyalty.points_ledger {
  points_ledger_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each points ledger transaction entry. Primary key for the points ledger.']
  accrual_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.accrual_rule. Business justification: Points accrual transactions should reference the rule that governed the earning calculation. Currently points_ledger has earn_multiplier (DECIMAL) but no FK to the rule. Adding accrual_rule_id enables']
  event_booking_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.event_booking. Business justification: Points earned from MICE/event spend (space rental, F&B, AV) must be directly attributable to the event booking for loyalty liability reporting and event-segment points analytics. points_ledger has res']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: IFRS 15/ASC 606 loyalty liability reporting requires points ledger transactions to be mapped to fiscal periods for period-close deferred revenue calculations. Finance teams produce period-level points']
  ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Every points accrual/redemption/expiry affects loyalty liability GL accounts. Daily batch posting process maps points transactions to specific liability, expense, and contra-revenue accounts. Critical']
  member_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the loyalty program member who owns this points transaction.']
  points_channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.channel. Business justification: Points ledger transactions must link to distribution channel for analyzing points accrual patterns by booking channel, understanding channel-specific earning behavior, and calculating true channel pro']
  pos_check_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fnb.pos_check. Business justification: F&B points accrual ledger entries must reference the originating POS check for dispute resolution, points reversal processing, and audit trails. Mirrors existing reservation_booking_id on points_ledge']
  promotion_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.promotion. Business justification: points_ledger has promotion_code (STRING) for bonus points earned via promotions. Adding promotion_id FK formalizes this relationship, enabling promotion-level points tracking and ROI analysis. Nullab']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.property. Business justification: Loyalty finance reconciliation requires linking points accrual/redemption transactions to specific properties for revenue allocation and inter-company settlements. Currently property_code is text; FK ']
  redemption_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.redemption. Business justification: Points ledger entries of transaction_type \'redemption\' should link to the redemption transaction that caused the points burn. Creates bidirectional navigation between ledger and redemption. Nullable (']
  redemption_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.redemption_rule. Business justification: Points redemption/burn transactions should reference the rule that governed the redemption calculation. Enables rule-level reporting and ensures redemptions can be traced to their governing rules. Nul']
  reversal_reference_points_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Points ledger ID of the original transaction that this entry reverses. Null for original transactions. Creates audit trail for cancelled transactions.']
  spa_fitness_class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to spa.fitness_class. Business justification: Points ledger entries generated by fitness class participation must reference the specific class for audit, dispute resolution, and points-earning traceability. points_ledger already links to reservat']
  spa_membership_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to spa.membership. Business justification: Loyalty points earned on spa membership fee payments or membership-based activities must reference the spa membership record for audit and reconciliation. This mirrors how points_ledger references res']
  activity_date date [note: 'Date when the underlying business activity occurred (e.g., stay checkout date, F&B transaction date, partner purchase date). This is the business event date, distinct from posting date.']
  adjustment_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation for manual adjustments or reversals, providing context for audit and member service. Null for standard transactions.']
  adjustment_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code explaining why a manual adjustment or reversal was made (e.g., service recovery, system error correction, duplicate posting). Null for standard transactions.']
  balance_after_transaction decimal(18,2) [note: 'Member\'s total points balance immediately after this transaction was posted. Provides running balance for audit and reconciliation.']
  base_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount in base currency that generated the points accrual (e.g., room revenue, F&B spend). Used to calculate points earned. Null for non-monetary transactions.']
  base_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code of the underlying monetary transaction that generated points (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Null for non-monetary point awards.']
  conversion_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Exchange rate applied when converting points between hotel program and partner program (e.g., 1.5 means 1 hotel point = 1.5 partner points). Null for non-transfer transactions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this ledger entry was first created in the database. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date when these points will expire if not redeemed. Null for non-expiring points or debit transactions. Used to enforce points expiration policies.']
  is_qualifying boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this transaction counts toward tier qualification or elite status progression. True for qualifying stays and spend, false for bonus points and transfers.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this ledger entry was last updated. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  partner_confirmation_number varchar [note: 'Confirmation or reference number provided by partner program for transfer transactions. Used for reconciliation and dispute resolution. Null for non-transfer transactions.']
  partner_program_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the external partner loyalty program involved in transfer transactions (e.g., airline frequent flyer program, credit card rewards program). Null for internal transactions.']
  points_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Quantity of points involved in this transaction. Positive for accruals (earn, transfer_in, bonus), negative for debits (redeem, transfer_out, expire, adjust).']
  posting_date date [note: 'Date when the points transaction was posted to the member\'s account and became effective in their balance. May differ from activity_date due to processing lag.']
  source_activity_type varchar [note: 'Category of business activity that generated this points transaction: stay (room revenue), fnb (Food and Beverage spend), spa (spa/wellness services), partner_purchase (external partner transaction), promotion (marketing campaign), manual (staff adjustment), transfer (partner program exchange). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: stay|fnb|spa|partner_purchase|promotion|manual|transfer — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that created this points transaction (e.g., OPERA PMS, MICROS POS, Salesforce CRM, Partner API). Used for traceability and reconciliation.']
  tier_at_transaction varchar [note: 'Member\'s loyalty tier status at the time this transaction was posted (e.g., Silver, Gold, Platinum). Captures tier for historical analysis of earning patterns.']
  transaction_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier for this points transaction, used for member inquiries and reconciliation.']
  transaction_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the points transaction: pending (awaiting posting), posted (finalized and reflected in balance), reversed (cancelled), failed (rejected due to validation error).']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Type of points movement: earn (accrual from activity), redeem (points spent), expire (time-based expiration), adjust (manual correction), transfer_out (sent to partner), transfer_in (received from partner), bonus (promotional award), reversal (cancellation of prior transaction). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: earn|redeem|expire|adjust|transfer_out|transfer_in|bonus|reversal — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  transfer_direction varchar [note: 'Direction of partner transfer: inbound (points received from partner into hotel program), outbound (points sent from hotel program to partner). Null for non-transfer transactions.']
  transfer_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Fee charged to member for partner transfer transaction, expressed in points. Null if no fee applies or for non-transfer transactions.']

  Note: 'Authoritative transactional ledger recording every points movement for a loyalty member — including accruals, redemptions, expirations, adjustments, and partner transfers. Each row is an immutable debit or credit entry capturing transaction type (earn, redeem, expire, adjust, transfer_out, transfer_in, bonus), points amount, balance after transaction, source activity (stay, F&B spend, partner purchase, promotion), activity date, posting date, expiry date, originating system reference. For transfer-type entries: partner program reference, transfer direction, conversion rate applied, partner confirmation number, transfer status, and fee charged. This is the SSOT for points balance, movement history, and partner transfer activity.'
}

Table loyalty.accrual_rule {
  accrual_rule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the loyalty points accrual rule. Primary key for the accrual rule configuration entity.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Bonus points accrual rules (e.g., double points during summer campaign) are created specifically for marketing campaigns. Linking accrual_rule to campaign enables campaign ROI reporting inclusive of p']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.channel. Business justification: Loyalty accrual rules are channel-specific (direct booking earns higher multiplier than OTA). The `booking_channel_restriction` plain-text field is a denormalization of channel.channel. A proper FK en']
  fnb_outlet_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fnb.fnb_outlet. Business justification: Loyalty programs configure outlet-specific accrual rules (e.g., double points at the signature restaurant). The POS points-earning engine must evaluate outlet-scoped rules during transaction processin']
  franchise_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.franchise_agreement. Business justification: Franchise agreements define loyalty_fee_pct and brand standard compliance for points earning. Linking accrual_rule to franchise_agreement enables franchise-specific earning rule governance, loyalty fe']
  hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.hierarchy. Business justification: Accrual rules are defined at regional and brand-portfolio levels (e.g., APAC double points, luxury tier multiplier). Linking accrual_rule to hierarchy enables hierarchy-scoped rule management, replace']
  program_config_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.program_config. Business justification: Accrual rules are defined within a program configuration context. This FK links each rule to its governing program instance, enabling program-level rule management and versioning. The program_config t']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.property. Business justification: Property revenue managers need to forecast loyalty liability by analyzing which accrual rules apply to their property. Currently applicable_property_ids is a text list; FK enables property-specific ea']
  spa_treatment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to spa.treatment. Business justification: Loyalty accrual rules are configured per spa treatment type (e.g., \'Hot Stone Massage earns 3x points\'). The loyalty engine must reference the specific treatment to apply the correct multiplier. This ']
  target_guest_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.guest_segment. Business justification: Accrual rules are restricted to specific guest segments (e.g., bonus points only for leisure travelers). accrual_rule.member_segment_restriction is a denormalized text representation of the guest_segm']
  tier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.tier. Business justification: accrual_rule currently stores tier applicability as a free-text string (member_tier_restriction). Normalizing this to a FK tier_id → tier.tier_id enforces referential integrity and enables proper rela']
  applicable_brands varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of brand codes where this rule applies (e.g., luxury, premium, select-service). Used when property_applicability is \'brand_specific\'. Null indicates all brands.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Workflow approval status for the accrual rule configuration. Used for governance and change control before rules are activated in production.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the business owner or system administrator who approved this accrual rule for production use. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this accrual rule was approved for production use. Part of the governance audit trail.']
  blackout_dates varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of date ranges (YYYY-MM-DD:YYYY-MM-DD) during which this accrual rule does not apply. Used for promotional exclusions or high-demand periods. Null indicates no blackouts.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this accrual rule record was first created in the system. Part of the standard audit trail.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the earning basis (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Defines the currency in which the points-per-currency-unit rate applies.']
  day_of_week_restriction varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of days of week (MON, TUE, WED, THU, FRI, SAT, SUN) when this rule applies. Used for promotional rules targeting specific days. Null indicates all days are eligible.']
  earning_basis varchar [note: 'The transaction component or activity on which points accrual is calculated: room_revenue (ADR-based), fnb_spend (Food and Beverage), spa_spend, ancillary_spend (parking, resort fees), partner_spend (co-branded partners), nights_stayed (per-night basis), qualifying_stays (per-stay basis). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: room_revenue|fnb_spend|spa_spend|ancillary_spend|partner_spend|nights_stayed|qualifying_stays — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this accrual rule expires and stops applying to transactions. Null indicates the rule is open-ended with no expiration. Part of the rule\'s temporal validity window.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this accrual rule becomes active and begins applying to qualifying transactions. Part of the rule\'s temporal validity window.']
  eligible_rate_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of rate codes (BAR, corporate, government, promotional) that qualify for points earning under this rule. Null or empty indicates all rate codes are eligible.']
  estimated_annual_points_volume varchar [note: 'Projected total number of loyalty points expected to be issued annually under this accrual rule. Used for program economics forecasting and liability planning.']
  excluded_rate_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of rate codes explicitly excluded from earning points under this rule (e.g., employee rates, complimentary stays, distressed inventory). Takes precedence over eligible_rate_codes.']
  external_rule_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for this accrual rule in the source operational system. Used for cross-system reconciliation and integration mapping.']
  is_stackable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this accrual rule can be combined with other rules for the same transaction (True) or is mutually exclusive (False). Used to control promotional stacking and program economics.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who last modified this accrual rule configuration. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this accrual rule record was last updated. Part of the standard audit trail for configuration change management.']
  marketing_message varchar [note: 'Member-facing promotional message describing the earning opportunity (e.g., \'Earn 2X points on weekend stays!\'). Used in marketing campaigns and member communications.']
  maximum_points_per_transaction varchar [note: 'Cap on the number of points that can be earned in a single transaction under this rule. Used to prevent abuse and manage program liability. Null indicates no cap.']
  minimum_transaction_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Minimum spend threshold required for the accrual rule to apply. Transactions below this amount do not earn points under this rule. Null indicates no minimum.']
  points_per_currency_unit decimal(10,4) [note: 'Base rate of loyalty points earned per unit of currency spent (e.g., 10.0000 points per USD, 5.0000 points per EUR). Used as the multiplier in the points calculation engine.']
  program_liability_impact varchar [note: 'Assessment of the financial liability impact this accrual rule has on the loyalty program\'s balance sheet: low (minimal incremental liability), medium (moderate impact), high (significant liability increase). Used for program economics monitoring.']
  property_applicability varchar [note: 'Scope of properties where this accrual rule applies: all (entire portfolio), brand_specific (luxury/premium/select-service), property_list (specific property IDs), region_specific (geographic regions).']
  rule_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code used as a unique business identifier for the accrual rule in operational systems and reporting (e.g., \'ROOM_BASE\', \'FNB_ELITE\', \'SPA_PROMO\').']
  rule_description varchar [note: 'Detailed business description of the accrual rule, including its purpose, earning mechanics, eligibility criteria, and any special conditions. Used for documentation and business user reference.']
  rule_name varchar [note: 'Business-friendly name of the accrual rule for identification and reporting purposes (e.g., \'Base Room Revenue Earn\', \'Elite Tier Bonus\', \'F&B Spend Multiplier\').']
  rule_priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority ranking used to resolve conflicts when multiple accrual rules apply to the same transaction. Lower numbers indicate higher priority. The points calculation engine applies the highest-priority matching rule.']
  rule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the accrual rule: draft (under development), active (in production), suspended (temporarily disabled), expired (past effective_end_date), retired (permanently deactivated).']
  rule_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the accrual rule by its business purpose: base (standard earning), bonus (additional points), promotional (limited-time campaign), tier_multiplier (elite status boost), partner (third-party earn), seasonal (time-bound recurring).']
  segment_multiplier decimal(5,2) [note: 'Additional multiplier applied based on member segment or customer classification (e.g., corporate, leisure, group). Used for targeted earning incentives.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system where this accrual rule was originally configured (e.g., Salesforce CRM, Oracle OPERA PMS, custom loyalty engine). Used for data lineage and troubleshooting.']
  terms_and_conditions varchar [note: 'Legal terms and conditions text associated with this accrual rule, including disclaimers, exclusions, and member obligations. Used for regulatory compliance and member communication.']
  tier_multiplier decimal(5,2) [note: 'Additional multiplier applied based on member loyalty tier or elite status (e.g., 1.00 for base, 1.25 for silver, 1.50 for gold, 2.00 for platinum). Multiplies the base points calculation.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number for this accrual rule configuration. Incremented with each modification to support change history and rollback capabilities.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who created this accrual rule configuration. Used for audit trail and accountability.']

  Note: 'Master configuration entity defining the rules by which loyalty points are earned. Captures rule name, earning basis (room revenue, F&B spend, spa spend, partner spend), points-per-currency-unit rate, multiplier for tier or segment, eligible rate codes, blackout conditions, effective date range, property applicability, and rule priority. Drives the points calculation engine for all earn transactions.'
}

Table loyalty.redemption_rule {
  redemption_rule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the loyalty redemption rule. Primary key for the redemption rule configuration entity.']
  campaign_offer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign_offer. Business justification: Redemption rules (reduced points cost for a room type) are created as part of campaign offers. Linking redemption_rule to campaign_offer ensures redemption terms align with offer eligibility windows a']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.channel. Business justification: Redemption rules are channel-restricted in real loyalty operations — free night awards may only be redeemable via direct/CRS channels, not OTA. This FK enables the loyalty redemption engine to enforce']
  fnb_outlet_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fnb.fnb_outlet. Business justification: Points redemption rules are scoped to specific F&B outlets (e.g., points-for-dining valid only at the hotel\'s fine dining restaurant). The redemption engine requires outlet FK to enforce eligibility d']
  franchise_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.franchise_agreement. Business justification: Franchise agreements govern award night eligibility and redemption obligations at franchised properties. Linking redemption_rule to franchise_agreement enables compliance reporting on award night obli']
  program_config_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.program_config. Business justification: Redemption rules are defined within a program configuration context. Adding program_config_id links each rule to its governing program instance, enabling program-level rule management and versioning. ']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.property. Business justification: Property finance teams track redemption rule applicability for revenue recognition and liability management. Currently eligible_property_codes is text; FK enables property-specific redemption forecast']
  spa_treatment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to spa.treatment. Business justification: Redemption rules are scoped to specific spa treatments (e.g., \'Free 60-min Swedish Massage redemption\'). The redemption engine must reference the treatment to enforce eligibility, duration, and availa']
  advance_booking_days varchar [note: 'Minimum number of days in advance a reservation must be made for this rule to apply. Used for early booking incentives.']
  blackout_dates varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of date ranges (YYYY-MM-DD:YYYY-MM-DD) when this rule is not applicable. Used for peak season restrictions.']
  calculation_basis varchar [note: 'Defines the monetary or transactional basis for points calculation. Determines which revenue component is used for earn rate application.']
  combinable_with_promotions boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this redemption rule can be combined with other promotional offers. False means this rule is exclusive.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this redemption rule record was first created in the system. Audit trail for rule configuration.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for monetary earn rules. Defines the currency basis for points calculation.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this redemption rule expires and is no longer applied. Nullable for open-ended rules.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this redemption rule becomes active and eligible for application. Part of the rule validity window.']
  eligible_property_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of property codes where this redemption rule is applicable. Defines geographic or brand scope.']
  eligible_rate_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of rate codes eligible for this redemption rule. Defines which booking rate plans qualify for points earning or redemption.']
  excluded_property_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of property codes explicitly excluded from this redemption rule. Takes precedence over eligible properties.']
  excluded_rate_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of rate codes explicitly excluded from this redemption rule. Takes precedence over eligible rate codes.']
  lra_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this redemption rule applies under Last Room Availability conditions. True means members can redeem even when standard inventory is sold out.']
  maximum_earn_per_stay varchar [note: 'Maximum number of points that can be earned per stay under this rule. Cap for earn rules to prevent abuse.']
  maximum_los varchar [note: 'Maximum number of nights allowed for this redemption rule to apply. Used to cap extended stay benefits.']
  maximum_redemption_per_transaction varchar [note: 'Maximum number of points that can be redeemed in a single transaction under this rule. Cap for burn rules.']
  minimum_los varchar [note: 'Minimum number of nights required for this redemption rule to apply. Used for stay-based earn or burn rules.']
  minimum_points_balance varchar [note: 'Minimum loyalty points balance required in member account to be eligible for this redemption rule. Applies to burn rules only.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or system account that last modified this redemption rule. Audit trail for accountability.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this redemption rule record was last modified. Audit trail for rule configuration changes.']
  partner_code varchar [note: 'External partner identifier for partner-based earn or burn rules (e.g., airline, car rental, credit card partner). Nullable for non-partner rules.']
  points_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'For earn rules: points earned per currency unit spent (e.g., 10 points per USD). For burn rules: points required per unit of benefit (e.g., 25000 points for free night).']
  redemption_rule_description varchar [note: 'Detailed business description of the redemption rule including terms, conditions, and member-facing explanation.']
  redemption_rule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the redemption rule. Only active rules are applied by the points calculation engine.']
  rounding_method varchar [note: 'Method used to round calculated points values. Ensures consistent points calculation across the loyalty program.']
  rule_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the redemption rule used across systems and reporting. Externally-known identifier for rule configuration.']
  rule_direction varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this rule governs points earning (earn) or points redemption (burn). Core classification for the rule engine.']
  rule_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the redemption rule for display and reporting purposes.']
  rule_priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority for rule application when multiple rules are eligible. Lower numbers indicate higher priority. Used by the rules engine for conflict resolution.']
  rule_type varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the rule basis. For earn rules: room_revenue, fnb_spend, partner_spend. For burn rules: free_night, upgrade, statement_credit, merchandise, experience. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: room_revenue|fnb_spend|partner_spend|free_night|upgrade|statement_credit|merchandise|experience — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  terms_and_conditions varchar [note: 'Legal terms and conditions text associated with this redemption rule. Displayed to members during redemption flow.']
  tier_multiplier_base decimal(5,2) [note: 'Base tier multiplier applied to the points rate for base-tier members. Default is 1.0.']
  tier_multiplier_gold decimal(5,2) [note: 'Tier multiplier applied to the points rate for gold-tier members. Typically 1.5 to 2.0.']
  tier_multiplier_platinum decimal(5,2) [note: 'Tier multiplier applied to the points rate for platinum-tier members. Typically 2.0 to 3.0.']
  tier_multiplier_silver decimal(5,2) [note: 'Tier multiplier applied to the points rate for silver-tier members. Typically 1.25 to 1.5.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or system account that created this redemption rule. Audit trail for accountability.']

  Note: 'Unified master configuration entity defining all rules governing how loyalty points are earned and redeemed (earn/burn rules engine). Captures rule direction (earn or burn), rule name, basis (room revenue, F&B spend, partner spend for earn; free night, upgrade, credit for burn), rate or points cost, tier multipliers, eligible rate codes and properties, blackout conditions, minimum balance requirements, LRA applicability, caps, effective date range, and rule priority. Single source of truth for the points calculation and redemption eligibility engines.'
}

Table loyalty.redemption {
  redemption_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the loyalty points redemption transaction. Primary key.']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.channel. Business justification: Redemptions must link to distribution channel for channel-specific redemption performance analysis, understanding which channels drive points burn vs. cash bookings, and calculating true channel profi']
  event_booking_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.event_booking. Business justification: Members redeem loyalty points against event bookings (offsetting space rental, F&B minimums). redemption links to reservation_booking_id but not event_booking_id. Direct link enables event revenue rec']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Redemptions extinguish deferred revenue liability — a key IFRS 15/ASC 606 revenue recognition event. Finance requires redemptions mapped to fiscal periods for period-close revenue recognition reportin']
  guest_experience_enrollment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to experience.guest_experience_enrollment. Business justification: When a member redeems points for an experience program, a guest_experience_enrollment is created. The redemption record must reference the enrollment it funded for points liability reconciliation, ful']
  ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Point redemptions reduce loyalty liability and may trigger revenue recognition. Monthly financial close requires posting redemption transactions to specific GL liability accounts. Essential for balanc']
  member_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the loyalty program member who initiated the redemption.']
  pos_check_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the POS transaction associated with this redemption, if applicable (e.g., F&B voucher redemption). Nullable for non-POS redemptions.']
  redemption_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.redemption_rule. Business justification: Redemptions should reference the rule that governed the redemption calculation and eligibility. Enables rule-level redemption reporting and ensures redemptions can be audited against their governing r']
  revenue_rate_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to revenue.revenue_rate_plan. Business justification: Loyalty redemptions for free night awards or discounted stays must link to the specific rate plan used for the redemption. This FK enables revenue displacement analysis, rate plan performance tracking']
  reward_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.reward_catalog. Business justification: Redemptions of type \'reward\' should reference the catalog item being redeemed. Currently redemption has reward_description and reward_category (STRING) but no FK. Adding reward_catalog_id formalizes t']
  spa_package_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to spa.package. Business justification: When a member redeems points for a spa package, the redemption record must directly reference the spa package for fulfillment, availability management, and redemption reporting. This mirrors how redem']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for redemption cancellation (e.g., member request, system error, fraud detection, reward unavailable). Nullable if not cancelled.']
  cancellation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the redemption was cancelled. Nullable if not cancelled.']
  cash_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cash component paid by the member for cash-and-points redemptions. Zero for pure points redemptions.']
  confirmation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the redemption was confirmed and points were deducted from the member account. Nullable if still pending.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this redemption record was first created in the source system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the cash component (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date by which the redemption must be fulfilled or the reward will expire. Nullable for redemptions without expiration.']
  fulfillment_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the reward was delivered: at property, physical mail, email, partner fulfillment, digital delivery, or in-app delivery.']
  fulfillment_date date [note: 'Date when the reward was delivered or service was rendered to the member. Nullable if not yet fulfilled.']
  fulfillment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the reward was fulfilled. Nullable if not yet fulfilled.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this redemption record was last updated in the source system.']
  monetary_equivalent_value decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated monetary value of the redeemed reward in the currency specified, used for program economics and LTV (Lifetime Value) analysis.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes captured during redemption processing, including special requests, service recovery actions, or fulfillment instructions.']
  partner_code varchar [note: 'Code of the partner organization fulfilling the reward (e.g., airline, car rental, merchandise vendor). Nullable for internal redemptions.']
  points_redeemed varchar [note: 'Total number of loyalty points deducted from the member account for this redemption.']
  points_refunded varchar [note: 'Number of points returned to the member account due to cancellation or reversal. Zero if no refund occurred.']
  property_code varchar [note: 'Code of the property where the redemption was fulfilled, if applicable. Nullable for non-property redemptions (e.g., partner rewards, merchandise).']
  redemption_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible unique business identifier for the redemption transaction, used for guest communication and service recovery.']
  redemption_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the redemption: pending approval, confirmed and awaiting fulfillment, fulfilled and completed, cancelled by member or system, expired before fulfillment, or reversed due to service recovery or fraud.']
  redemption_type varchar [note: 'Category of reward being redeemed: free night award, room upgrade, F&B voucher, partner airline/car rental reward, merchandise, curated experience, points transfer to another member, cash plus points combination, or gift certificate. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: free_night|room_upgrade|fnb_voucher|partner_reward|merchandise|experience|points_transfer|cash_and_points|gift_certificate — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  refund_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when points were refunded to the member account. Nullable if no refund occurred.']
  request_date date [note: 'Date when the member submitted the redemption request. Business event date for analytics and reporting.']
  request_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the redemption request was initiated by the member, including time zone offset.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system that originated this redemption record (e.g., Salesforce CRM, Oracle OPERA PMS, loyalty platform).']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of this redemption in the source operational system, used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  tier_at_redemption varchar [note: 'Loyalty program tier of the member at the time of redemption (e.g., Silver, Gold, Platinum, Diamond). Used for tier-specific redemption analysis.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a loyalty member redeeming points for a reward. Captures redemption request date, fulfillment date, redemption type (free night, upgrade, F&B voucher, partner reward, merchandise), points deducted, monetary equivalent value, redemption status (pending, confirmed, fulfilled, cancelled, expired), associated reservation or POS transaction reference, and fulfillment channel. SSOT for all reward redemption activity.'
}

Table loyalty.reward_catalog {
  reward_catalog_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the reward catalog entry. Primary key for the reward catalog product.']
  campaign_offer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign_offer. Business justification: Reward catalog items (free nights, upgrades) are activated as part of specific campaign offers. Linking reward_catalog to campaign_offer enables offer performance reporting, eligibility enforcement, a']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.channel. Business justification: Reward catalog items (free nights, upgrades) are redeemable only through specific channels (e.g., mobile app, direct web). The `redemption_channel` plain-text field is a denormalization of channel.cha']
  fnb_outlet_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fnb.fnb_outlet. Business justification: F&B reward catalog items (complimentary dinner, dining credit) must reference the specific outlet for operational fulfillment, inventory reservation, and outlet-level redemption reporting. Loyalty ope']
  function_space_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.function_space. Business justification: Loyalty reward catalogs include complimentary function space rentals (meeting rooms, boardrooms redeemable with points). Linking reward_catalog to the specific function_space enables real-time invento']
  program_config_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.program_config. Business justification: The reward catalog is part of program configuration - rewards available for redemption are defined at the program level. Adding program_config_id links catalog items to their governing program, enabli']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to experience.program. Business justification: Reward catalog items (curated experience packages, spa programs) are fulfilled through experience programs. Loyalty operations teams need to report which experience programs drive points redemption vo']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.property. Business justification: Property operations teams manage inventory and fulfillment of property-specific rewards (spa treatments, dining credits, room upgrades). Currently property_restriction is text; FK enables property-lev']
  room_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.room_type. Business justification: Free night and room upgrade rewards in the catalog must specify the eligible room_type (e.g., \'Standard King\', \'Junior Suite\'). Redemption processing, availability checking, and reward fulfillment all']
  spa_fitness_class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to spa.fitness_class. Business justification: Fitness classes are redeemable loyalty rewards (e.g., \'Redeem 500 points for a yoga class\'). reward_catalog already links to treatments and packages; fitness classes are a distinct spa service categor']
  spa_package_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to spa.package. Business justification: Reward catalog items include spa packages redeemable with loyalty points (e.g., \'Redeem 20,000 points for the Couples Retreat Package\'). reward_catalog already links to individual treatments; package-']
  availability_type varchar [note: 'Classification of reward availability pattern determining inventory management and member access rules.']
  blackout_dates varchar [note: 'Specific dates or date ranges when the reward cannot be redeemed or used, typically for high-demand periods.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the reward catalog entry was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the monetary value of the reward.']
  display_order varchar [note: 'Numeric sequence controlling the presentation order of rewards in catalog listings and member interfaces.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the reward is no longer available for redemption. Null indicates no planned end date for always-on rewards.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the reward becomes available for redemption in the loyalty program catalog.']
  eligible_tier_base boolean [note: 'Indicates whether base tier members are eligible to redeem this reward.']
  eligible_tier_gold boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Gold tier members are eligible to redeem this reward.']
  eligible_tier_platinum boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Platinum tier members are eligible to redeem this reward.']
  eligible_tier_silver boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Silver tier members are eligible to redeem this reward.']
  featured_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this reward is featured or promoted in member communications and catalog displays.']
  geographic_restriction varchar [note: 'Geographic limitations on where the reward can be redeemed, specified as regions, countries, or property lists.']
  image_url varchar [note: 'URL path to the primary marketing image for the reward displayed in digital catalogs and member portals.']
  inventory_count varchar [note: 'Current available quantity of the reward for redemption. Null indicates unlimited availability for always-on rewards.']
  inventory_threshold varchar [note: 'Minimum inventory level that triggers replenishment alerts or availability status changes for limited inventory rewards.']
  maximum_stay_nights varchar [note: 'Maximum number of nights allowed for hotel stay rewards. Null indicates no maximum restriction.']
  minimum_stay_nights varchar [note: 'Minimum number of nights required for hotel stay rewards. Null for non-accommodation rewards.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or system account that last modified the reward catalog entry.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to any attribute of the reward catalog entry.']
  monetary_value decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated retail or face value of the reward in the base currency, used for program economics and liability calculations.']
  points_cost_base varchar [note: 'Base loyalty points required for redemption by standard tier members. Foundation for tier-based pricing calculations.']
  points_cost_gold varchar [note: 'Loyalty points required for redemption by Gold tier members, reflecting enhanced tier benefits and discounts.']
  points_cost_platinum varchar [note: 'Loyalty points required for redemption by Platinum tier members, offering maximum tier discount benefits.']
  points_cost_silver varchar [note: 'Loyalty points required for redemption by Silver tier members, typically discounted from base tier pricing.']
  property_restriction varchar [note: 'Defines which properties or property segments are eligible for redemption of hotel-related rewards.']
  redemption_validity_days varchar [note: 'Number of days from issuance that a redeemed reward certificate or voucher remains valid for use before expiration.']
  reward_category varchar [note: 'Primary classification of the reward type determining redemption process and fulfillment workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hotel_stay|room_upgrade|dining|spa|experience|merchandise|partner|travel — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  reward_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the reward item used across systems and member communications.']
  reward_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the reward including benefits, usage instructions, and any special features or limitations.']
  reward_name varchar [note: 'Display name of the reward as presented to loyalty program members in catalogs and redemption interfaces.']
  reward_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the reward in the catalog determining visibility and redemption availability.']
  reward_subcategory varchar [note: 'Secondary classification providing granular categorization within the primary reward category for enhanced filtering and reporting.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system of record for the reward catalog entry, typically the loyalty management platform or CRM system.']
  terms_and_conditions varchar [note: 'Legal terms, restrictions, blackout dates, usage limitations, and other conditions governing reward redemption and usage.']
  total_redemptions varchar [note: 'Cumulative count of all redemptions of this reward since its introduction, used for popularity analytics and program economics.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or system account that created the reward catalog entry.']

  Note: 'Master catalog of all rewards available for redemption within the loyalty program, including issued reward instances (certificates and vouchers). Captures reward name, category (hotel stay, upgrade, dining, spa, experiences, merchandise, partner), points cost by tier, monetary value, availability type (always-on, limited, seasonal), inventory count, eligible member tiers, redemption channel, terms and conditions, supplier/partner reference, and active status. For issued instances: certificate type, face value, issue date, expiry date, redemption status (issued, redeemed, expired, voided), associated reservation, and issuing channel. SSOT for both what rewards are available and what reward instruments have been issued to members.'
}

Table loyalty.enrollment {
  enrollment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the loyalty program enrollment transaction. Primary key.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Enrollments driven by \'join and earn\' marketing campaigns must be linked to the originating campaign for campaign ROI reporting on enrollment volume and quality. loyalty_enrollment.campaign_code is a ']
  campaign_offer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign_offer. Business justification: Enrollment campaigns include specific offers (welcome bonus points, eligible rate plans). Linking loyalty_enrollment to campaign_offer enables offer fulfillment tracking and ensures welcome bonus term']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.channel. Business justification: Enrollments must link to distribution channel for measuring channel effectiveness in driving new member acquisition, calculating member acquisition cost by channel, and optimizing enrollment campaigns']
  initial_tier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.tier. Business justification: loyalty_enrollment currently stores the initial tier granted at enrollment as a free-text string (initial_tier). Normalizing this to a FK initial_tier_id → tier.tier_id enforces referential integrity,']
  primary_loyalty_member_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the guest who enrolled in the loyalty program. Links to the guest master record.']
  program_config_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.program_config. Business justification: A loyalty enrollment event is always associated with a specific loyalty program configuration (program version, terms version, points policy in effect at enrollment time). loyalty_enrollment currently']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property where the enrollment occurred. Null for digital enrollments not associated with a specific property.']
  reservation_booking_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reservation.reservation_booking. Business justification: Enrollment at check-in or during a stay is a primary acquisition channel in hospitality loyalty programs. Linking loyalty_enrollment to the triggering reservation_booking enables welcome-bonus points ']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country from which the enrollment was initiated.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this enrollment record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'Type of device used to complete the enrollment. Used for channel and user experience analysis.']
  documentation_submitted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member submitted proof of status from the competitor program. Populated only for status match enrollments.']
  email_consent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member consented to receive email communications at the time of enrollment.']
  enrollment_date date [note: 'The date the guest enrolled in the loyalty program. Represents the business event date of program membership initiation.']
  enrollment_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible unique enrollment transaction number used for tracking and customer service reference.']
  enrollment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the enrollment transaction. Tracks the lifecycle state of the enrollment record.']
  enrollment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the enrollment was completed, including timezone information. Used for operational tracking and SLA measurement.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address from which the enrollment was submitted. Used for fraud detection and geographic analysis. May be considered PII in some jurisdictions.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter ISO 639-1 language code indicating the language preference selected during enrollment.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this enrollment record was last updated. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  match_expiry_date date [note: 'The date when the status match tier expires and the member must requalify. Populated only for status match enrollments.']
  match_tier_granted varchar [note: 'The tier level granted in our program as a result of the status match. Populated only for status match enrollments.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments recorded by the enrollment agent or system regarding special circumstances or details of the enrollment.']
  privacy_policy_accepted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member accepted the privacy policy at enrollment. Required for GDPR and CCPA compliance.']
  privacy_policy_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the privacy policy that the member accepted at enrollment. Used for audit and compliance tracking.']
  push_notification_consent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member consented to receive mobile app push notifications at the time of enrollment.']
  referral_code varchar [note: 'Referral code used by the enrolling member if they were referred by an existing member. Used for member-get-member program tracking.']
  requalification_requirement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member must meet standard tier qualification criteria to retain the matched tier after expiry. Populated only for status match enrollments.']
  sms_consent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member consented to receive SMS text message communications at the time of enrollment.']
  source_program_name varchar [note: 'Name of the competitor loyalty program from which status was matched. Populated only for status match enrollments.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system that originated the enrollment record (e.g., Salesforce CRM, OPERA PMS, Mobile App).']
  source_tier_claimed varchar [note: 'The tier level the member claimed to hold in the source competitor program. Populated only for status match enrollments.']
  status_match_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this enrollment was a competitive status match acquisition from another loyalty program.']
  terms_accepted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member accepted the loyalty program terms and conditions at enrollment. Required for legal compliance.']
  terms_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the terms and conditions that the member accepted at enrollment. Used for audit and compliance tracking.']
  welcome_bonus_points decimal(18,2) [note: 'Number of loyalty points awarded as a welcome bonus upon enrollment. Zero if no welcome bonus was offered.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the event of a guest enrolling in the loyalty program, including status match acquisitions.
