Captures enrollment date, enrollment channel (front desk, website, mobile app, OTA, call center, partner, status match), enrollment property, enrollment campaign or promotion code, initial tier assigned, welcome bonus points awarded, consent flags (email, SMS, push), enrollment agent, and for status match enrollments: source program, source tier claimed, documentation submitted, match tier granted, match expiry date, and requalification requirement. Enables enrollment funnel analysis, channel attribution, and competitive member acquisition tracking.'
}

Table loyalty.benefit_entitlement {
  benefit_entitlement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the benefit entitlement record. Primary key.']
  campaign_offer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign_offer. Business justification: Benefit entitlements (complimentary upgrades, lounge access) are frequently granted as fulfillment of a targeted campaign offer. This link supports offer attribution reporting and ensures entitlement ']
  fnb_outlet_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fnb.fnb_outlet. Business justification: Loyalty benefits such as complimentary breakfast or F&B dining credits are tied to specific outlets for operational delivery. Front-of-house staff need outlet-scoped entitlement records to validate an']
  meeting_space_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.meeting_space. Business justification: Elite member benefits include complimentary meeting room access (e.g., Platinum boardroom entitlement). Linking benefit_entitlement to meeting_space enables operational fulfillment teams to confirm av']
  member_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the loyalty program member who holds this benefit entitlement.']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to experience.program. Business justification: Tier benefits frequently include complimentary access to specific experience programs (e.g., platinum members receive a complimentary culinary program enrollment). Benefit entitlement must reference t']
  promotion_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.promotion. Business justification: benefit_entitlement has an entitlement_source (STRING) field that describes how the benefit was granted. When a benefit is granted as a result of a promotion (e.g., a promotional upgrade benefit, a bo']
  spa_treatment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to spa.treatment. Business justification: Loyalty benefit entitlements for Platinum/Gold members frequently specify a particular spa treatment (e.g., \'Complimentary 60-min Deep Tissue Massage\'). Linking to spa.treatment enables automated book']
  tier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the loyalty tier that grants this benefit as a standard entitlement. Null if the benefit is individually granted outside of tier benefits.']
  advance_booking_required_days varchar [note: 'The minimum number of days in advance that a reservation must be made to redeem this benefit. Null if no advance booking requirement exists.']
  auto_apply_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this benefit is automatically applied to eligible reservations or requires manual member selection. True if auto-applied, False if opt-in required.']
  benefit_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the benefit, including terms, conditions, and any restrictions or exclusions.']
  benefit_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the benefit entitlement for display and communication purposes.']
  benefit_type_code varchar [note: 'The category of benefit granted to the member. Examples include complimentary breakfast, room upgrade, late checkout, lounge access, welcome amenity, or bonus points multiplier.']
  blackout_dates varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of date ranges during which this benefit cannot be redeemed, typically during peak demand periods or special events.']
  brand_restriction varchar [note: 'The brand code if this benefit is restricted to properties within a specific brand. Null if no brand restriction applies.']
  combinable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this benefit can be combined with other benefits or promotions during the same stay. True if combinable, False if mutually exclusive.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the monetary value of this benefit.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date from which this benefit entitlement becomes valid and can be redeemed by the member.']
  eligible_property_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of property codes where this benefit can be redeemed. Populated only when property_restriction is SPECIFIC.']
  entitlement_source varchar [note: 'The origin or reason for granting this benefit. Tier standard benefits are automatic based on member tier; status match benefits are granted when matching status from another program; manual grants are discretionary; promotion benefits are campaign-driven; compensation benefits are service recovery; partner benefits come from co-branded or partner integrations.']
  entitlement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the benefit entitlement. Active entitlements can be redeemed; suspended entitlements are temporarily unavailable; expired entitlements have passed their validity period; revoked entitlements have been withdrawn; pending entitlements are awaiting activation.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which this benefit entitlement expires and can no longer be redeemed. Null for benefits with no expiration.']
  granted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this benefit entitlement was created or granted to the member.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this benefit entitlement record was last updated in the system.']
  maximum_stay_nights varchar [note: 'The maximum length of stay (LOS) in nights for which this benefit can be applied. Null if no maximum stay restriction exists.']
  minimum_stay_nights varchar [note: 'The minimum length of stay (LOS) in nights required to redeem this benefit. Null if no minimum stay requirement exists.']
  monetary_value decimal(10,2) [note: 'The estimated monetary value of this benefit in the property\'s base currency, used for program economics tracking and liability calculation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments, special instructions, or contextual information about this benefit entitlement.']
  points_multiplier decimal(5,2) [note: 'The bonus points multiplier applied to eligible stays when this benefit is active. For example, 1.5 means the member earns 50% bonus points. Null if this benefit does not include a points multiplier.']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'The priority order for applying this benefit when multiple benefits are available. Lower numbers indicate higher priority. Used to resolve conflicts when benefits cannot be combined.']
  property_restriction varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this benefit can be redeemed at all properties, only at specific properties, within a specific brand, or within a specific geographic region.']
  region_restriction varchar [note: 'The geographic region code if this benefit is restricted to properties within a specific region. Null if no region restriction applies.']
  remaining_usage varchar [note: 'The number of redemptions remaining for this benefit. Calculated as usage_limit minus usage_count. Null if usage is unlimited.']
  revocation_reason varchar [note: 'The business reason or justification for revoking this benefit entitlement. Null if the benefit has not been revoked.']
  revoked_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this benefit entitlement was revoked. Null if the benefit has not been revoked.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The name of the operational system that originated this benefit entitlement record (e.g., Salesforce CRM, Oracle OPERA PMS).']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of this benefit entitlement in the source operational system.']
  transferable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this benefit can be transferred to another loyalty member. True if transferable, False if non-transferable.']
  usage_count varchar [note: 'The number of times this benefit has been redeemed to date. Used to track remaining usage against the usage limit.']
  usage_limit varchar [note: 'The maximum number of times this benefit can be redeemed during its validity period. Null indicates unlimited usage.']

  Note: 'Master record of the specific benefits a loyalty member is entitled to based on their current tier and any individually granted benefits. Captures benefit type (complimentary breakfast, room upgrade, late checkout, lounge access, welcome amenity, bonus points multiplier), entitlement source (tier standard, status match, manual grant, promotion), effective date, expiry date, usage limit, and remaining usage count. Enables benefit delivery and tracking at the property level.'
}

Table loyalty.benefit_redemption {
  benefit_redemption_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the benefit redemption record. Primary key for tracking the complete lifecycle of a loyalty member benefit from entitlement through consumption or expiry.']
  benefit_entitlement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.benefit_entitlement. Business justification: benefit_redemption tracks the consumption/usage of a benefit entitlement. Currently it duplicates benefit_type_code, benefit_name, benefit_description from the entitlement. Adding benefit_entitlement_']
  campaign_offer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign_offer. Business justification: Benefit redemptions (complimentary upgrades, amenities) delivered as part of a targeted campaign offer must be linked to that offer for campaign attribution and fulfillment tracking. This is standard ']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Benefit redemptions carry cost_to_property_amount (e.g., complimentary breakfast charged to F&B cost center, room upgrade to Rooms). USALI departmental P&L reporting requires benefit redemption costs ']
  event_booking_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.event_booking. Business justification: Loyalty benefits (complimentary upgrades, welcome amenities, F&B credits) are redeemed in event booking contexts for MICE guests. benefit_redemption links to reservation_booking but not event_booking.']
  ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Benefit redemptions with monetary_value_amount and cost_to_property_amount must post to the GL for accurate expense recognition. Hotel finance teams post benefit redemption costs to specific GL accoun']
  loyalty_member_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the loyalty program member who holds this benefit entitlement.']
  pos_check_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fnb.pos_check. Business justification: When F&B loyalty benefits (complimentary meals, dining credits) are redeemed, the redemption record must link to the originating POS check for financial reconciliation, audit compliance, and points re']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the hotel property where the benefit was delivered or redeemed.']
  spa_appointment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to spa.appointment. Business justification: Loyalty elite benefits include complimentary spa treatments redeemed at specific appointments. Critical for benefit entitlement validation, spa treatment delivery tracking, and elite member spa benefi']
  acknowledgment_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the member acknowledged benefit receipt: digital signature, email confirmation, mobile app, verbal confirmation, or automatic system acknowledgment.']
  acknowledgment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the member acknowledged receipt of the benefit. Null if not acknowledged.']
  actual_delivery_minutes varchar [note: 'Actual time in minutes taken to deliver the benefit from the moment of request or check-in. Used for SLA compliance measurement.']
  benefit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the benefit: active (available for use), redeemed (fully consumed), expired (past validity date), cancelled (revoked), or pending (awaiting activation).']
  cancellation_date date [note: 'Date when the benefit was cancelled or revoked. Null if benefit was not cancelled.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the benefit was cancelled (e.g., member downgrade, policy violation, program termination, member request).']
  confirmation_number varchar [note: 'Reservation confirmation number associated with the benefit redemption. Used for cross-reference with PMS (Property Management System) records.']
  cost_to_property_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Actual cost incurred by the property to deliver this benefit. Used for GOP (Gross Operating Profit) and program ROI (Return on Investment) analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this benefit redemption record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the monetary value amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  delivery_location varchar [note: 'Specific location within the property where the benefit was delivered (e.g., \'Front Desk\', \'Executive Lounge\', \'Room 1205\', \'Restaurant - The Grill\').']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'First date on which the benefit can be redeemed or used by the member.']
  entitlement_date date [note: 'Date when the benefit was granted or became available to the member.']
  entitlement_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the benefit entitlement: tier standard (automatic with membership level), status match (from competitor program), manual grant (by staff), promotion (campaign-driven), or compensation (service recovery).']
  exception_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an exception occurred during benefit fulfillment (True) or the benefit was delivered as standard (False).']
  exception_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes explaining the exception circumstances, substitution details, or any special handling required during benefit delivery.']
  exception_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the type of exception: benefit unavailable, substitution provided, upgrade given, downgrade applied, partial fulfillment, or member declined benefit.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Last date on which the benefit can be redeemed. After this date, the benefit becomes void and cannot be used.']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'Identifier from the source system for cross-system reconciliation and audit trail purposes.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this benefit redemption record was last updated or modified.']
  member_acknowledgment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member acknowledged receipt of the benefit (True) or not (False). Used for service quality tracking and dispute resolution.']
  monetary_value_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated monetary value of the benefit in the property\'s local currency. Used for program economics tracking and LTV (Lifetime Value) calculations.']
  points_equivalent varchar [note: 'Loyalty points value equivalent of this benefit. Used when members have the option to choose between benefits or points.']
  redemption_date date [note: 'Date when the benefit was redeemed or consumed by the member. Null if not yet redeemed.']
  redemption_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the benefit was redeemed, including time zone. Used for operational tracking and SLA (Service Level Agreement) compliance.']
  remaining_usage_count varchar [note: 'Number of redemptions still available for this benefit. Decrements with each usage. Null indicates unlimited remaining usage.']
  sla_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the benefit was delivered within the program\'s SLA (Service Level Agreement) standards (True) or not (False). Used for elite service quality tracking.']
  sla_target_minutes varchar [note: 'Target time in minutes within which the benefit should be delivered according to program SLA standards.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of record that originated this benefit redemption record: Salesforce CRM (loyalty management), OPERA PMS (property operations), dedicated loyalty engine, or manual entry.']
  substitution_benefit_type varchar [note: 'Type of benefit provided as a substitute when the original benefit was unavailable. Null if no substitution occurred.']
  usage_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum number of times this benefit can be redeemed during its validity period. Null indicates unlimited usage within the validity window.']

  Note: 'Unified lifecycle entity for loyalty member benefits — from entitlement through consumption or expiry. Captures benefit type (complimentary breakfast, room upgrade, late checkout, lounge access, welcome amenity, bonus points multiplier), entitlement source (tier standard, status match, manual grant, promotion), effective date, expiry date, usage limit, remaining usage count, and delivery records including delivery date, property, reservation reference, delivering staff, member acknowledgment, and exception notes. This is the SSOT for \'what benefits does this member have and which have been used\' — enabling benefit fulfillment tracking, elite SLA compliance, and property-level benefit delivery operations.'
}

Table loyalty.promotion {
  promotion_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the loyalty promotion. Primary key.']
  accrual_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.accrual_rule. Business justification: Promotions in a loyalty program frequently define bonus points accrual mechanics (bonus_points_multiplier, bonus_points_amount on the promotion table). When a promotion governs a specific accrual rule']
  budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.finance_budget. Business justification: Promotions consume approved marketing budget. Promotion approval workflow validates budget_consumed_amount against allocated budget. Monthly variance reporting compares actual promotion spend to budge']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent marketing campaign in the CRM (Customer Relationship Management) system. Links this promotion to broader campaign tracking, budget, and performance reporting.']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.channel. Business justification: Loyalty promotions are configured with channel eligibility (e.g., bonus points only for direct bookings, not OTA). The `marketing_channel` plain-text field is a denormalization of channel.channel. Thi']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Promotions are funded by specific departments (marketing, revenue management). Departmental P&L reporting requires allocating promotion costs to responsible cost centers. Essential for budget accounta']
  hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.hierarchy. Business justification: Loyalty promotions are routinely scoped to geographic regions or brand portfolios (hierarchy nodes) rather than individual properties. Linking promotion to hierarchy enables regional promotion managem']
  program_config_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.program_config. Business justification: Promotions operate within a program configuration context. Adding program_config_id links each promotion to its governing program instance, enabling program-level promotion management and ensuring pro']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to experience.program. Business justification: Loyalty promotions are routinely tied to experience programs (e.g., \'earn 5,000 bonus points when you enroll in our wellness program\'). Promotion managers need to link promotions to specific experienc']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.property. Business justification: Property marketing teams analyze which promotions drive enrollment and stays at their property for campaign ROI measurement. Currently qualifying_property_list is text; FK enables property-level promo']
  seasonal_calendar_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.seasonal_calendar. Business justification: Revenue management and loyalty teams co-design promotions aligned to seasonal demand periods (e.g., double points during shoulder season). Linking promotion to seasonal_calendar enables yield-integrat']
  target_guest_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.guest_segment. Business justification: Loyalty promotions target specific marketing guest segments (e.g., high-value leisure travelers, business frequent guests). Campaign design requires defining which guest segment qualifies for each loy']
  tier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the loyalty tier required to participate in this promotion. Null if promotion is open to all tiers. Used to restrict offers to specific membership levels (e.g., Gold, Platinum, Diamond).']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when this promotion was formally approved for launch. Null if promotion is still in draft or pending approval. Used for campaign governance and audit trail.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Username or employee ID of the manager or director who approved this promotion for launch. Null if promotion is still in draft status. Used for governance and approval workflow tracking.']
  award_fulfillment_date date [note: 'Target date by which bonus points or nights will be posted to qualifying members\' accounts after promotion completion. Used for member communication and fulfillment SLA (Service Level Agreement) tracking.']
  bonus_nights_credit varchar [note: 'Number of bonus elite-qualifying nights awarded upon promotion completion. Used for tier progression acceleration. Null if promotion does not award night credits.']
  bonus_points_amount varchar [note: 'Fixed number of bonus loyalty points awarded upon successful completion of promotion criteria. Null if bonus is not a fixed point amount (e.g., multiplier-based).']
  bonus_points_multiplier decimal(5,2) [note: 'Multiplier applied to base points earned during the promotion period. Example: 2.0 for double points, 1.5 for 50% bonus. Null if promotion awards fixed points rather than a multiplier.']
  budget_cap_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum financial budget allocated for this promotion (total cost of all bonuses awarded). Promotion may be paused or closed early if budget is exhausted. Measured in base currency (typically USD).']
  budget_consumed_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cumulative financial cost of bonuses awarded to date under this promotion. Updated as members complete qualifying activity and bonuses are posted. Used for budget tracking and ROI (Return on Investment) analysis.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the promotion was cancelled or terminated early. Null if promotion completed normally or is still active. Examples: \'Budget exhausted\', \'Low engagement\', \'Business priority change\', \'Legal compliance issue\'.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this promotion record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when the promotion expires. Qualifying activity after this date does not count toward the promotion. Null for open-ended promotions.']
  exclusion_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of member IDs or segments explicitly excluded from this promotion (e.g., employees, test accounts, members who opted out). Null if no exclusions apply.']
  minimum_nights varchar [note: 'Minimum number of qualifying nights required to earn the promotion bonus. Null if nights are not a qualifying criterion.']
  minimum_spend_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum total spend (in base currency) required to qualify for the promotion. Null if spend is not a qualifying criterion. Typically measured in USD or property local currency.']
  promotion_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique alphanumeric code for the promotion, used in marketing materials and member enrollment. Example: BONUS2024, WELCOME500.']
  promotion_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the promotion displayed to members and in marketing channels. Example: \'Double Points Summer Getaway\', \'Elite Status Fast Track\'.']
  promotion_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the promotion. Values: draft (being designed), scheduled (approved, not yet live), active (currently running), paused (temporarily suspended), completed (ended successfully), cancelled (terminated early).']
  promotion_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the promotion mechanism. Values: bonus_points_multiplier (earn extra points per dollar), double_nights (accelerate tier qualification), targeted_offer (personalized to segment), welcome_back (re-engagement), birthday_bonus (member birthday reward), spend_challenge (gamified spending goal).']
  qualifying_criteria_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative of the requirements a member must meet to earn the promotion bonus. Examples: \'Complete 3 stays between June 1 and August 31\', \'Spend minimum $500 on F&B (Food and Beverage) during stay\', \'Book via mobile app using rate code MOBILE2024\'.']
  qualifying_rate_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of rate codes (BAR - Best Available Rate, corporate codes, promotional codes) that qualify for this promotion. Null or \'ALL\' if all rate codes qualify. Example: \'BAR,CORP,PROMO2024\'.']
  registration_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether members must explicitly register or opt-in to participate in this promotion. True = registration required before qualifying activity counts; False = automatic enrollment for eligible members.']
  stackable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this promotion can be combined with other active promotions. True = member can earn bonuses from multiple promotions simultaneously; False = this promotion cannot be stacked with others.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when the promotion becomes active and members can begin earning toward the bonus. Qualifying activity before this date does not count.']
  terms_and_conditions_url varchar [note: 'Web link to the full legal terms and conditions document for this promotion. Displayed to members during enrollment and in promotional materials.']
  total_completions varchar [note: 'Count of members who have successfully completed all qualifying criteria and earned the promotion bonus. Used for conversion rate and campaign effectiveness analysis.']
  total_enrollments varchar [note: 'Count of unique members who have enrolled or been auto-enrolled in this promotion. Used for campaign reach and engagement metrics.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this promotion record was last modified. Updated whenever any attribute changes. Used for change tracking and audit trail.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Username or employee ID of the marketing or loyalty team member who created this promotion record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']

  Note: 'Master entity defining loyalty program promotions and bonus offers, including member participation tracking. Captures promotion name, type (bonus points multiplier, double nights, targeted offer, welcome back, birthday bonus, spend challenge), target segment or tier, qualifying criteria (minimum nights, minimum spend, specific properties, specific rate codes), bonus awarded, registration requirement, start/end dates, and budget cap. For member participation: enrollment date, enrollment channel, member promotion status (enrolled, in-progress, completed, expired, awarded), qualifying activity progress, bonus awarded flag, award date, and opt-out date. Drives personalized offer delivery, campaign economics, and promotion fulfillment tracking.'
}

Table loyalty.promotion_enrollment {
  promotion_enrollment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the promotion enrollment record. Primary key.']
  campaign_execution_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign_execution. Business justification: Tracks which specific marketing campaign execution (email send, paid ad, social post) drove a member to enroll in a loyalty promotion. Critical for marketing attribution and campaign ROI measurement.']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.channel. Business justification: Promotion enrollments must link to distribution channel for campaign channel attribution analysis, measuring which channels drive highest promotion participation rates, and calculating channel-specifi']
  event_booking_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.event_booking. Business justification: MICE loyalty promotions (e.g., earn bonus points for booking events) enroll members at event booking time. promotion_enrollment has property_code (text) but no event_booking_id. Direct link enables MI']
  guest_experience_enrollment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to experience.guest_experience_enrollment. Business justification: Promotions requiring experience program participation (e.g., \'complete a spa program enrollment to earn bonus points\') need the promotion_enrollment to reference the qualifying guest_experience_enroll']
  member_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the loyalty member who enrolled in the promotion.']
  promotion_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific promotion the member enrolled in.']
  award_date date [note: 'Date when the promotion bonus was awarded to the member\'s account.']
  award_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number or transaction ID for the bonus award, used for reconciliation and member inquiries.']
  award_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the bonus was posted to the member\'s loyalty account.']
  bonus_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the bonus awarded (e.g., account credit, voucher value).']
  bonus_awarded_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the promotion bonus has been awarded to the member (True/False).']
  bonus_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the bonus amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  bonus_points varchar [note: 'Number of bonus loyalty points awarded to the member upon promotion completion.']
  cancellation_date date [note: 'Date when the enrollment was cancelled by the system or staff.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for cancellation of the enrollment (e.g., member ineligibility, promotion terminated, fraud detection).']
  communication_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether enrollment confirmation communication was sent to the member (True/False).']
  communication_sent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the enrollment confirmation communication was sent to the member.']
  completion_date date [note: 'Date when the member completed the qualifying activity requirements for the promotion.']
  completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the promotion requirements were completed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this enrollment record was first created in the data platform.']
  enrollment_date date [note: 'Date when the member enrolled or was enrolled in the promotion.']
  enrollment_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the enrollment occurred (opt-in by member, auto-enrolled by system, staff-enrolled, imported from external source).']
  enrollment_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier for this promotion enrollment, used for member communication and tracking.']
  enrollment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the member\'s participation in the promotion (enrolled, in-progress, completed, expired, awarded, cancelled, opted-out). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: enrolled|in_progress|completed|expired|awarded|cancelled|opted_out — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  enrollment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the enrollment was recorded in the system.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the enrollment expires if the member has not completed the promotion requirements.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about the enrollment, used for special circumstances or staff annotations.']
  opt_out_date date [note: 'Date when the member opted out of the promotion, if applicable.']
  opt_out_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided by the member or system for opting out of the promotion.']
  progress_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of qualifying activity completed toward the promotion threshold (0.00 to 100.00).']
  progress_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current progress value toward the qualifying threshold (e.g., 3 nights completed out of 5 required).']
  property_code varchar [note: 'Property code where the enrollment occurred, if applicable (e.g., front desk enrollment).']
  qualifying_activity_type varchar [note: 'Type of activity required to complete the promotion (nights stayed, number of stays, revenue spent, points earned, transactions, referrals).']
  qualifying_threshold decimal(18,2) [note: 'Target threshold value the member must achieve to complete the promotion (e.g., 5 nights, 1000 points, 500 USD revenue).']
  qualifying_threshold_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the qualifying threshold (nights, stays, points, currency, transactions, referrals).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that created this enrollment record (e.g., Salesforce CRM, OPERA PMS, Mobile App).']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of this enrollment record in the source system, used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  tier_at_enrollment varchar [note: 'Loyalty tier of the member at the time of enrollment, used for tier-specific promotion eligibility and tracking.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this enrollment record was last modified in the data platform.']

  Note: 'Transactional association record capturing a loyalty member registering or being enrolled in a specific promotion. Captures enrollment date, enrollment channel, promotion status for the member (enrolled, in-progress, completed, expired, awarded), qualifying activity progress (nights completed, spend accumulated), bonus awarded flag, award date, and opt-out date if applicable. Enables promotion fulfillment tracking and member communication triggers.'
}

Table loyalty.program_config {
  program_config_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the loyalty program configuration record. Primary key. This is the single source of truth configuration governing program-wide business rules and economics.']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.brand. Business justification: A loyalty program configuration is scoped to a brand (e.g., Marriott Bonvoy for Marriott brands). Linking program_config to brand enables brand-level loyalty program governance, co-branded marketing r']
  liability_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Hotel loyalty programs maintain a dedicated deferred revenue liability GL account configured at the program level (IFRS 15 / ASC 606 compliance). Role-prefix \'liability_\' used because program_config h']
  api_integration_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether external systems can integrate with this loyalty program via API. True = API access available for partners and properties; False = manual integration only.']
  breakage_assumption_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Estimated percentage of earned points that will never be redeemed (e.g., 0.1500 = 15%). Used for USALI-compliant revenue recognition and liability estimation. Based on historical redemption patterns.']
  cash_plus_points_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether members can combine cash and points for redemptions. True = hybrid payment allowed; False = points-only or cash-only redemptions.']
  ccpa_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this program configuration meets CCPA requirements for California resident data. True = CCPA controls implemented; False = non-California program or pending compliance.']
  configuration_effective_date date [note: 'Date when this specific configuration version became effective. Used for time-based rule application and member communication of program changes.']
  configuration_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when this configuration version expires and is replaced by a new version. Null if currently active with no planned end date.']
  contact_center_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for member inquiries and loyalty program support. Used for member communication and published on program website.']
  contact_center_phone varchar [note: 'Primary phone number for member services and loyalty program support. Used for member communication materials and website display.']
  conversion_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the points-to-currency conversion rate (e.g., \'USD\', \'EUR\', \'GBP\'). Defines the monetary unit for program economics.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this configuration record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_name varchar [note: 'Name of the loyalty currency unit (e.g., \'Points\', \'Miles\', \'Credits\', \'Stars\'). This is the term displayed to members for their balance.']
  data_retention_policy_months varchar [note: 'Number of months member data is retained after account closure, per GDPR and CCPA requirements. After this period, PII is purged per right-to-erasure regulations.']
  family_pooling_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether members can pool points with family members into a shared account. True = pooling allowed; False = pooling disabled.']
  gdpr_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this program configuration meets GDPR requirements for EU member data. True = GDPR controls implemented; False = non-EU program or pending compliance.']
  gifting_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether members can gift points to other members. True = gifting allowed; False = gifting disabled. Subject to anti-fraud controls.']
  inactivity_period_months varchar [note: 'Number of months of account inactivity (no earn or redeem transactions) before points expire, if points_expiry_policy is \'inactivity_based\'. Null otherwise.']
  liability_estimation_method varchar [note: 'Accounting method for estimating loyalty program liability. incremental_cost = cost to fulfill; fair_value = market value of rewards; redemption_value = points * conversion rate. Must align with USALI and ASC 606.']
  lifetime_status_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the program offers lifetime elite status after achieving cumulative thresholds. True = lifetime tiers available; False = annual re-qualification required.']
  maximum_earn_per_stay varchar [note: 'Maximum number of points a member can earn from a single stay, regardless of spend. Null if no cap applies. Used to manage program liability.']
  minimum_redemption_threshold varchar [note: 'Minimum number of points required to initiate any redemption transaction. Prevents micro-redemptions and reduces operational costs.']
  mobile_app_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a dedicated mobile application is available for this loyalty program. True = mobile app available; False = web-only access.']
  partner_transfer_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether members can transfer points to partner programs (airlines, car rentals, etc.). True = transfers allowed; False = transfers disabled.']
  points_expiry_policy varchar [note: 'Rule governing when points expire. no_expiry = points never expire; inactivity_based = expire after N months of no activity; fixed_period = expire after N months from earn date; rolling_expiry = expire on a rolling window.']
  points_purchase_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether members can purchase additional points with cash. True = purchase allowed; False = earn-only program. Subject to regulatory limits in some jurisdictions.']
  points_to_currency_conversion_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Exchange rate for converting loyalty points to monetary value (e.g., 0.0100 means 100 points = 1 USD). Used for redemption valuation and breakage accounting per USALI standards.']
  program_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this loyalty program in operational systems (e.g., \'HNRS\', \'ELITE01\'). Used for system integration and reporting.']
  program_launch_date date [note: 'Date when the loyalty program was originally launched. Used for program anniversary communications and historical analysis.']
  program_name varchar [note: 'Official marketing name of the loyalty program (e.g., \'Honors Rewards\', \'Elite Guest Club\'). This is the customer-facing brand name.']
  program_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the loyalty program. Active programs accept enrollments and transactions; suspended programs are temporarily paused; sunset programs are being phased out.']
  program_terms_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the legal terms and conditions document governing this program configuration. Links to the published T&C document for member consent and compliance.']
  program_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the program configuration (e.g., \'2.1\', \'3.0.1\'). Incremented when program rules or economics change materially.']
  program_website_url varchar [note: 'Primary website URL for the loyalty program where members can view terms, check balances, and redeem rewards. Used for marketing and member self-service.']
  program_year_start_month varchar [note: 'Month number (1-12) when the program year begins if tier_year_definition is \'program_year\'. Null for calendar or rolling definitions. 1=January, 12=December.']
  rolling_expiry_window_months varchar [note: 'Number of months from earn date after which points expire, if points_expiry_policy is \'rolling_expiry\'. Null otherwise. Example: 24 means points earned on Jan 1, 2023 expire Jan 1, 2025.']
  status_match_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the program offers status matching from competitor loyalty programs. True = status match campaigns allowed; False = earn-only tier qualification.']
  terms_effective_date date [note: 'Date when the current program terms and conditions became effective. Used for member communication and regulatory compliance.']
  tier_rollover_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether qualifying nights/stays can roll over to the next qualification period if a member exceeds their tier threshold. True = rollover allowed; False = no rollover.']
  tier_year_definition varchar [note: 'Method for calculating the qualification period for tier status. Calendar = Jan 1 - Dec 31; rolling_12_month = trailing 12 months from any date; program_year = custom fiscal period.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this configuration record was last modified. Used for audit trail, change tracking, and data synchronization.']

  Note: 'Reference master storing the global configuration parameters of the loyalty program. Captures program name, program version, currency name (points, miles, credits), points-to-currency conversion rate, tier year definition (calendar vs. rolling 12-month), minimum redemption threshold, maximum earn per stay, points expiry policy (inactivity period, rolling expiry window), program terms version, GDPR/CCPA data retention policy, breakage assumption rate, and active status. Single configuration record governing program-wide business rules and program economics assumptions.'
}

Table channel.channel {
  channel_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for channel']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Distribution channels incur operating costs (connectivity fees, commissions, marketing) that must be allocated to cost centers for USALI-compliant departmental P&L reporting and budget variance analys']
  ota_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.ota_partner. Business justification: Many distribution channels are operated by OTA partners (e.g., Expedia operates multiple channels/brands). This FK establishes the ownership relationship. Removes redundant platform_name which can be ']
  activation_date date [note: 'Date on which this distribution channel was first activated and began accepting live bookings. Distinct from contract_start_date which reflects the commercial agreement date. Used for channel onboarding tracking and performance benchmarking from go-live.']
  bar_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this channel is eligible to display and sell the Best Available Rate (BAR) — the lowest publicly available rate at the time of booking. True = BAR rates are distributed to this channel; False = channel receives only specific contracted or negotiated rates. Drives rate distribution strategy in IDeaS G3 RMS and Sabre SynXis CRS.']
  booking_fee_usd decimal(8,2) [note: 'Per-transaction booking fee charged by the channel in addition to or instead of a commission percentage, expressed in USD. Common in GDS channels (e.g., GDS segment fee per booking). Used in cost-of-acquisition and channel profitability analysis.']
  cancellation_policy_code varchar [note: 'Code referencing the default cancellation policy applied to bookings made through this channel (e.g., \'24H_FREE\', \'48H_PENALTY\', \'NON_REFUNDABLE\'). Cancellation policies may differ by channel based on contractual agreements. Managed in Oracle OPERA PMS and Sabre SynXis CRS.']
  channel_category varchar [note: 'High-level grouping of the channel by booking relationship: DIRECT = guest books directly with the property (web, mobile, voice, corporate direct); INDIRECT = third-party intermediary involved (OTA, GDS, wholesaler, metasearch); OPAQUE = rate and property identity concealed until after booking (e.g., Hotwire, Priceline opaque). Drives cost-of-acquisition analysis and channel mix strategy.']
  channel_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the distribution channel across enterprise systems including Sabre SynXis CRS, Oracle OPERA PMS, and IDeaS G3 RMS. Used for channel mapping and cross-system reconciliation.']
  channel_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the distribution channel, including its market positioning, target guest segment, special contractual terms, or operational notes. Used for channel management documentation and onboarding reference.']
  channel_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the distribution channel (e.g., \'Expedia Partner Central\', \'Booking.com Extranet\', \'Sabre GDS\', \'Direct Web\', \'Voice Reservations\'). Used in reporting, dashboards, and channel mix analysis.']
  channel_status varchar [note: 'Current operational lifecycle status of the distribution channel. active = channel is live and accepting bookings; inactive = channel is configured but not currently selling; suspended = channel temporarily halted (e.g., rate parity violation, connectivity issue); pending_activation = channel onboarded but not yet live; decommissioned = channel permanently retired.']
  channel_type varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the distribution channel by its market segment and technology interface. OTA = Online Travel Agency (e.g., Expedia, Booking.com); GDS = Global Distribution System (e.g., Sabre, Amadeus, Galileo); DIRECT_WEB = brand.com web booking; DIRECT_MOBILE = brand mobile app; VOICE = call center reservations; CORPORATE_DIRECT = negotiated corporate accounts; WHOLESALER = tour operators and bed banks; METASEARCH = Google Hotel Ads, Trivago. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: OTA|GDS|DIRECT_WEB|DIRECT_MOBILE|VOICE|CORPORATE_DIRECT|WHOLESALER|METASEARCH — promote to reference product]']
  commission_basis varchar [note: 'The commercial model under which commission is calculated for this channel. NET_RATE = channel marks up a net rate provided by the property; GROSS_RATE = commission deducted from the gross rate charged to the guest; MARKUP = channel applies a markup over the property\'s net rate; FLAT_FEE = fixed fee per booking regardless of rate. Critical for accurate revenue and cost-of-acquisition reporting.']
  commission_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Standard commission percentage charged by this distribution channel on each booking, expressed as a percentage of the room revenue (e.g., 15.00 for 15%). Drives cost-of-acquisition calculations and net revenue reporting per USALI standards. Confidential as it reflects negotiated commercial terms.']
  connectivity_fee_usd decimal(10,2) [note: 'Annual or periodic connectivity/subscription fee charged by the channel or switch provider for maintaining the technical integration, expressed in USD. Included in total cost-of-acquisition analysis alongside commission. Confidential as it reflects negotiated commercial terms.']
  connectivity_method varchar [note: 'Technical integration method used to connect the property management system or CRS to this distribution channel. API = RESTful or SOAP API integration; XML = XML-based messaging (e.g., OTA XML); SFTP = file-based batch transfer; DIRECT_CONNECT = proprietary direct connection; SWITCH = via a channel manager switch (e.g., SiteMinder, Channex); MANUAL = manually managed extranet updates. Impacts SLA and rate update latency.']
  content_distribution_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether property content (room descriptions, images, amenities, policies) is actively distributed to this channel via a content API or content management system. True = content is pushed/synced to this channel; False = content is managed manually or not distributed. Relevant for OTA and metasearch channels.']
  contract_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the commercial agreement with this distribution channel expires or is scheduled to terminate. Null for evergreen/open-ended agreements. Used for contract renewal management and channel lifecycle planning. Stored in yyyy-MM-dd format.']
  contract_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the commercial agreement with this distribution channel became effective. Used for contract lifecycle management, commission audit trails, and regulatory compliance. Stored in yyyy-MM-dd format.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this distribution channel record was first created in the enterprise data platform. Stored in yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX format. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and record lifecycle management.']
  crs_channel_code varchar [note: 'Channel identifier as configured in the Sabre SynXis Central Reservation System (CRS). Used for cross-system reconciliation between the enterprise data lakehouse and the CRS source of record. Enables traceability of bookings back to the originating CRS channel configuration.']
  deactivation_date date [note: 'Date on which this distribution channel was deactivated or decommissioned. Null for currently active channels. Used for channel lifecycle management, historical reporting, and audit trails. Stored in yyyy-MM-dd format.']
  gdpr_data_processor boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this distribution channel acts as a GDPR data processor for guest personal data collected during the booking process. True = a Data Processing Agreement (DPA) is in place with this channel; False = channel does not process personal data on behalf of the property. Required for GDPR compliance and data governance.']
  gds_chain_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter GDS chain code assigned to the hotel brand or property group within Global Distribution Systems (Sabre, Amadeus, Galileo/Travelport, Worldspan). Required for GDS-type channels to route availability and rate requests correctly. Null for non-GDS channels.']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic reach of this distribution channel. GLOBAL = channel operates worldwide; REGIONAL = channel serves a specific geographic region (e.g., Asia-Pacific, Europe); DOMESTIC = channel serves a single country; LOCAL = channel serves a specific city or metro area. Used for channel mix strategy and market segmentation analysis.']
  inventory_allocation_method varchar [note: 'Method by which room inventory is allocated to this distribution channel. POOLED = channel draws from the shared central inventory pool (real-time); ALLOTMENT = a fixed block of rooms is pre-allocated to the channel; FREE_SALE = channel can sell without prior allocation up to a defined limit; ON_REQUEST = bookings require manual confirmation. Managed in IDeaS G3 RMS and Sabre SynXis CRS.']
  last_room_availability boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this channel has Last Room Availability (LRA) rights — meaning the property must make the last available room accessible through this channel at the contracted rate. LRA is a common contractual obligation in corporate and GDS agreements. True = LRA applies; False = LRA does not apply.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this distribution channel record was most recently modified in the enterprise data platform. Stored in yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX format. Used for change tracking, data freshness monitoring, and audit compliance.']
  loyalty_bookings_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether bookings made through this channel are eligible for loyalty program points accrual and redemption. True = loyalty points are awarded and redeemable; False = bookings through this channel do not qualify for loyalty benefits (common for opaque OTA channels). Managed in Salesforce CRM Loyalty Management.']
  mice_bookings_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this distribution channel supports group and MICE (Meetings, Incentives, Conferences, Exhibitions) bookings in addition to transient reservations. True = MICE/group bookings can be sourced through this channel; False = transient bookings only. Relevant for event sales and Delphi by Amadeus integration.']
  nrr_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Non-Refundable Rate (NRR) products are distributed through this channel. True = NRR fences are available on this channel; False = only flexible/refundable rates are distributed. Impacts revenue management yield strategy and cancellation policy enforcement.']
  payment_model varchar [note: 'Defines who collects payment from the guest for bookings made through this channel. HOTEL_COLLECT = property collects payment directly at check-in/check-out; CHANNEL_COLLECT = the OTA or intermediary collects payment and remits net to the property; SPLIT = partial payment collected by each party. Impacts accounts receivable and revenue recognition per USALI and GAAP/IFRS.']
  pci_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this distribution channel is certified as compliant with PCI DSS (Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard) for handling cardholder data. True = channel holds current PCI DSS certification; False = channel is not PCI compliant or certification has lapsed. Required for risk management and payment security governance.']
  primary_market_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the primary market served by this distribution channel (e.g., \'USA\', \'GBR\', \'DEU\'). Used for geographic channel mix analysis, market penetration reporting, and regulatory compliance (e.g., GDPR for EU-based channels).']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'Numeric rank indicating the strategic priority of this channel in the distribution mix, where lower numbers indicate higher priority (e.g., 1 = highest priority, typically direct web). Used by revenue management to determine inventory release sequencing and rate strategy. Configured in IDeaS G3 RMS and Sabre SynXis CRS.']
  rate_parity_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this distribution channel contractually requires rate parity — i.e., the property must offer the same or lower rates on this channel as on any other channel. True = rate parity clause is active in the channel agreement; False = no rate parity obligation. Critical for revenue management compliance and rate strategy.']
  rate_parity_type varchar [note: 'Specifies the scope of the rate parity obligation for this channel. BROAD = property must match rates across all channels including direct; NARROW = property must match rates on other OTAs but may offer lower rates on direct channels (common post-EU regulatory changes); NONE = no rate parity obligation. Drives revenue management and direct booking strategy.']
  remittance_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the channel remits collected payments to the property for CHANNEL_COLLECT payment model bookings. Applicable only when payment_model is CHANNEL_COLLECT or SPLIT. Drives accounts receivable forecasting and cash flow management in SAP S/4HANA.']
  sla_response_time_seconds varchar [note: 'Maximum allowable response time in seconds for availability and rate queries from this channel, as defined in the channel\'s Service Level Agreement (SLA). Used to monitor channel connectivity performance and enforce SLA compliance. Typical values: 3 seconds for OTA/GDS, 5 seconds for XML channels.']
  sla_uptime_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Contractually committed uptime percentage for this channel\'s connectivity, expressed as a percentage (e.g., 99.90 for 99.9% uptime). Used to monitor channel reliability and enforce SLA penalties. Managed against actual uptime metrics in channel performance reporting.']
  supported_currencies varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ISO 4217 three-letter currency codes supported by this distribution channel for booking and payment (e.g., \'USD,EUR,GBP,JPY\'). Relevant for multi-currency rate distribution and foreign exchange management. Managed in Sabre SynXis CRS.']
  supported_languages varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ISO 639-1 two-letter language codes supported by this channel for content display and guest communication (e.g., \'en,fr,de,zh,ja\'). Relevant for content localization strategy and international market reach.']

  Note: 'Master registry of all distribution channels through which Travel Hospitality properties sell inventory, including OTA platforms (Expedia, Booking.com), GDS networks (Sabre, Amadeus, Galileo), direct web/mobile, voice/call center, corporate direct, wholesalers, and metasearch engines. Captures channel type, connectivity method, commission tier, rate parity obligations, and channel-level SLA parameters. This is the SSOT for channel identity across the enterprise.'
}

Table channel.ota_partner {
  ota_partner_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the OTA partner record in the silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for the ota_partner master data product.']
  marketing_brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.brand. Business justification: OTA partnerships are brand-specific in multi-brand hospitality companies; Marriott Bonvoy partners differ from Ritz-Carlton partners. Brand-level partnership management, commission structures, and con']
  affiliate_network boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the OTA partner operates an affiliate or sub-distribution network through which third-party websites or agents can book on behalf of the OTA. True = affiliate network active; False = direct OTA platform only. Relevant for booking source attribution accuracy.']
  base_commission_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Standard commission percentage charged by the OTA partner on each confirmed booking, expressed as a percentage of the gross booking value (e.g., 15.00 = 15%). Foundational input for cost-of-acquisition (COA) and channel profitability analysis.']
  cancellation_policy_type varchar [note: 'Default cancellation policy type applied to bookings made through this OTA channel. Flexible = free cancellation up to 24-48 hours; Moderate = partial penalty; Strict = high penalty; Non_Refundable = NRR (Non-Refundable Rate) — no refund on cancellation. Impacts revenue management and guest experience.']
  channel_manager_platform varchar [note: 'Name of the channel manager or switch platform used to manage the technical connectivity to this OTA partner (e.g., Sabre SynXis, Siteminder, Amadeus iHotelier, RateGain). Relevant when connectivity is not direct API-to-API.']
  commission_model varchar [note: 'Commercial model governing how the OTA partner earns revenue. Net Rate = hotel sells at net, OTA marks up; Merchant = OTA collects full payment and remits net; Agency = OTA collects commission on hotel-collected payment; Retail = standard published rate with commission; Opaque = rate hidden from consumer until post-booking (e.g., Hotwire).']
  connectivity_fee_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the connectivity fee is billed by the OTA partner or channel manager. Annual = yearly flat fee; Monthly = recurring monthly; Per_Booking = transactional fee per confirmed booking; One_Time = setup/onboarding fee only.']
  connectivity_fee_usd decimal(10,2) [note: 'Annual or per-transaction connectivity fee charged by the OTA partner or channel manager for maintaining the technical integration (API/XML/GDS switch). Expressed in USD. Used for total cost-of-acquisition (COA) calculation and channel profitability reporting per USALI.']
  connectivity_protocol varchar [note: 'Technical protocol used for two-way inventory, rate, and availability (IRA) connectivity between the hotel\'s CRS/PMS and the OTA partner. XML = legacy XML push/pull; REST_API = modern RESTful API; GDS = Global Distribution System switch; SFTP = file-based batch; OTA_XML = OpenTravel Alliance XML standard; HTNG = HTNG web services.']
  content_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score (typically 0–100) representing the completeness and quality of the hotel\'s content (photos, descriptions, amenities) on the OTA partner\'s platform. Higher scores correlate with improved search ranking and conversion rates. Sourced from OTA partner reporting APIs.']
  contract_effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the master distribution agreement with the OTA partner becomes legally binding and operational. Governs the start of commission obligations and rate parity clauses.']
  contract_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the master distribution agreement with the OTA partner expires or is scheduled for renewal. Null indicates an evergreen/open-ended contract. Used for contract renewal management and risk monitoring.']
  contract_reference varchar [note: 'Unique reference number of the master distribution agreement or contract executed with the OTA partner. Links to the contract management repository in SAP S/4HANA for financial and legal audit purposes.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the OTA partner record was first created in the data platform. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and silver layer ingestion tracking. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  data_sharing_agreement boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal data sharing agreement (DSA) is in place with the OTA partner governing the exchange of guest booking data, anonymised analytics, and performance reporting. Required for GDPR and CCPA compliance when guest PII is transmitted.']
  gdpr_data_processor boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the OTA partner is formally designated as a GDPR Data Processor under Article 28, processing guest personal data on behalf of the hotel as Data Controller. True = Data Processing Agreement (DPA) executed; False = not designated as processor.']
  gds_chain_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter chain code assigned by the Global Distribution System (GDS) to identify the hotel brand/chain within GDS networks (Sabre, Amadeus, Travelport). Applicable only when connectivity_protocol is GDS. Null for non-GDS OTA partners.']
  hq_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country where the OTA partner\'s legal headquarters is registered. Used for tax treaty application, withholding tax determination, and regulatory jurisdiction identification.']
  inventory_allocation_model varchar [note: 'Model governing how room inventory is made available to the OTA partner. Free_Sell = real-time availability from CRS with no pre-allocated block; Allotment = pre-contracted room block allocated to the OTA; On_Request = bookings require manual confirmation. Managed in IDeaS G3 RMS and Sabre SynXis.']
  last_room_availability boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the OTA partner contract includes a Last Room Availability (LRA) clause, requiring the hotel to make its last available room accessible to this channel. LRA clauses impact revenue management inventory controls and are tracked in IDeaS G3 RMS.']
  legal_entity_name varchar [note: 'Full registered legal name of the OTA partner organisation as it appears on the master distribution agreement (e.g., Expedia, Inc.; Booking Holdings B.V.). Used for contract management, AP reconciliation, and regulatory filings.']
  market_coverage varchar [note: 'Description of the primary geographic markets or regions where the OTA partner has significant consumer reach and booking volume (e.g., \'North America, Western Europe\'; \'Asia-Pacific\'; \'Global\'). Used for channel mix optimisation and market-specific distribution strategy.']
  mobile_app_channel boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the OTA partner operates a dedicated mobile application channel through which bookings are made. True = mobile app channel is active and tracked separately for attribution; False = desktop/web only. Supports channel mix and mobile booking trend analysis.']
  ota_account_manager_email varchar [note: 'Business email address of the OTA partner\'s designated account manager. Used for commercial communications, promotional campaign coordination, and escalation. Classified as confidential PII per GDPR Article 4.']
  ota_account_manager_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the OTA partner\'s designated account manager or market manager assigned to the hotel account. Primary point of contact for commercial negotiations, promotional campaigns, and issue resolution.']
  ota_account_manager_phone varchar [note: 'Business phone number of the OTA partner\'s designated account manager. Used for urgent escalations and commercial negotiations. Classified as confidential PII per GDPR Article 4.']
  partner_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the OTA partner within the Central Reservation System (CRS) and Property Management System (PMS). Used for channel mapping, rate distribution, and booking source attribution in Sabre SynXis and Oracle OPERA.']
  partner_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the OTA partner relationship. Active = live and distributing inventory; Pending = contract under negotiation or connectivity setup in progress; Suspended = temporarily halted (e.g., rate parity breach); Terminated = contract ended.']
  partner_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the distribution partner by channel category. OTA = Online Travel Agency (Expedia, Booking.com); GDS = Global Distribution System (Sabre, Amadeus); METASEARCH = price comparison engine (Google Hotel Ads, Trivago); DIRECT = brand.com or call centre; WHOLESALE = tour operator/bed bank; AFFILIATE = sub-affiliate network. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: OTA|GDS|METASEARCH|DIRECT|WHOLESALE|AFFILIATE — promote to reference product if additional types emerge]']
  payment_collection_party varchar [note: 'Identifies which party collects payment from the guest. Hotel = property collects at check-in/check-out (agency model); OTA = OTA collects full payment and remits net to hotel (merchant model); Split = partial payment collected by each party.']
  preferred_commission_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Negotiated commission rate applicable when the property holds preferred partner or sponsored placement status with the OTA. Typically lower than the base rate in exchange for enhanced visibility or placement. Null if preferred status is not applicable.']
  preferred_partner boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the hotel or brand holds preferred partner or sponsored placement status with this OTA, typically in exchange for a higher commission rate or marketing investment. Preferred partners receive enhanced search ranking and visibility on the OTA platform.']
  primary_market_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the OTA partner\'s primary consumer market or country of dominant booking volume (e.g., USA, GBR, SGP). Used for geographic channel attribution and market penetration analysis.']
  rate_parity_clause boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the OTA partner contract includes a rate parity clause requiring the hotel to offer rates no lower on other channels than those offered on this OTA. True = rate parity clause is active; False = no rate parity obligation. Critical for revenue management and legal compliance monitoring.']
  rate_parity_type varchar [note: 'Specifies the scope of the rate parity obligation. Broad = hotel cannot offer lower rates on ANY channel including its own website; Narrow = hotel cannot offer lower rates on OTHER OTAs but may offer lower rates on its own direct channels; None = no parity obligation. Relevant for EU regulatory compliance following rate parity investigations.']
  remittance_cycle_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after the guest check-out date within which the OTA partner remits commission payments or net booking proceeds to the hotel. Used for cash flow forecasting and AP/AR reconciliation in SAP S/4HANA (e.g., 30 = net-30 terms).']
  review_count varchar [note: 'Total number of guest reviews published on the OTA partner\'s platform for the hotel. Used alongside review_score to assess statistical significance and platform engagement. Sourced from OTA partner reporting APIs.']
  review_score decimal(4,2) [note: 'Average guest review score for the hotel as published on the OTA partner\'s platform (e.g., Booking.com score out of 10, Expedia score out of 5). Used for reputation management, competitive benchmarking, and NPS/GSS correlation analysis via Medallia.']
  tax_registration_number varchar [note: 'VAT, GST, or equivalent tax registration number of the OTA partner\'s legal entity. Required for tax invoice processing, withholding tax compliance, and AP reconciliation in SAP S/4HANA.']
  trade_name varchar [note: 'Consumer-facing brand name under which the OTA partner operates (e.g., Hotels.com, Agoda). May differ from the legal entity name when the OTA operates multiple consumer brands under one legal umbrella.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the OTA partner record was most recently modified in the data platform. Used for change data capture (CDC), incremental ETL processing, and audit compliance. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']

  Note: 'Master data for Online Travel Agency partners including Expedia, Booking.com, Agoda, Hotels.com, Airbnb, and regional OTAs. Captures partner legal entity, contract reference, commission rate tiers, connectivity protocol (XML/API/GDS), rate parity clause status, preferred partner designation, market coverage, and account management contacts. Distinct from the channel record — an OTA partner may operate multiple channels (desktop, mobile app, affiliate).'
}

Table channel.gds_connection {
  gds_connection_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each GDS connection configuration record in the silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for this master configuration entity.']
  booking_source_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.booking_source. Business justification: GDS connections are associated with specific booking source codes for attribution when reservations come through GDS channels. Many GDS connections per booking source. Removes redundant booking_source']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent distribution channel record associated with this GDS connection, enabling channel mix analysis and cost-of-acquisition reporting.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: GDS connectivity fees (segment fees, monthly charges) are departmental operating expenses requiring cost center allocation for accurate distribution cost tracking and budget management per USALI stand']
  ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: GDS connections incur monthly connectivity fees posted to specific GL ledger accounts. gds_connection already has cost_center_id but lacks ledger_id for direct GL account mapping. Finance teams requir']
  market_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to revenue.market_segment. Business justification: GDS connections are configured per market segment (corporate, consortia, leisure) for rate loading and segment fee billing. FK enables segment-level GDS performance analysis and rate loading complianc']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property for which this GDS connection is configured. Links the GDS connectivity record to the specific hotel or resort property.']
  activation_date date [note: 'Date on which this GDS connection was first activated and made live for bookings. Used for contract term tracking, connectivity health history, and channel distribution reporting.']
  availability_sync_method varchar [note: 'Method by which room availability is synchronized between the property management system and the GDS. Real-time provides instant updates; batch updates on a scheduled interval; on_request queries availability at booking time; seamless provides live two-way availability.']
  chain_code varchar [note: 'Two- to four-character hotel chain code registered with the GDS network, identifying the hotel brand or management company within the GDS. Assigned by the GDS provider and used for inventory and rate loading.']
  commission_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage commission payable to travel agents or GDS intermediaries on bookings made through this GDS connection. Used for commission accrual, accounts payable processing, and channel cost analysis per USALI standards.']
  connection_status varchar [note: 'Current operational lifecycle status of the GDS connection. Active indicates live booking capability; suspended indicates temporary halt; pending_activation indicates setup in progress; decommissioned indicates permanently retired.']
  connectivity_fee_monthly_usd decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fixed monthly fee charged by the GDS provider or switch vendor for maintaining the technical connectivity of this GDS connection. Distinct from per-segment fees; used for OpEx budgeting and channel cost allocation.']
  connectivity_type varchar [note: 'Technical connectivity model used for this GDS connection. Direct connect provides real-time two-way communication; switch connect routes through a switch provider; seamless provides full availability and rate transparency; standard uses batch updates.']
  contract_end_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the GDS connectivity contract. Null for evergreen contracts. Used for renewal alerts, contract renegotiation planning, and channel distribution continuity management.']
  contract_start_date date [note: 'Effective start date of the GDS connectivity contract governing this connection. Used for contract lifecycle management, renewal planning, and financial obligation tracking.']
  corporate_rate_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether negotiated corporate rates are loaded and accessible through this GDS connection. Corporate rates are a primary revenue stream for GDS channels and require specific rate plan configuration in the CRS.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this GDS connection configuration record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and record lifecycle tracking in the silver layer lakehouse.']
  crs_interface_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the CRS-to-GDS interface configuration within Sabre SynXis CRS. Links the GDS connection to the specific CRS interface instance managing rate distribution and availability synchronization.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which rates are loaded and displayed to travel agents in this GDS connection. Ensures rate accuracy and currency consistency for international GDS distribution.']
  deactivation_date date [note: 'Date on which this GDS connection was or is scheduled to be deactivated. Null for currently active connections. Used for contract lifecycle management and channel distribution planning.']
  display_priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority ranking that determines the property\'s display order in GDS search results for travel agents. Lower values indicate higher priority. Influenced by GDS tier, participation level, and performance metrics.']
  gds_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the GDS provider (Sabre, Amadeus, Galileo, Worldspan, Travelport). Used for reporting, dashboards, and channel mix analysis.']
  gds_network_code varchar [note: 'Industry-standard two-character GDS network identifier assigned by IATA/ARC. 1S=Sabre, 1A=Amadeus, 1G=Galileo/Travelport, 1P=Worldspan. Used for routing and segment fee attribution.']
  gds_tier varchar [note: 'Participation tier level for this GDS connection, reflecting the level of GDS merchandising, display priority, and content features contracted. Preferred tier provides enhanced display and content capabilities; standard is base participation; basic is minimal listing.']
  health_check_status varchar [note: 'Result of the most recent automated health check for this GDS connection. Healthy indicates full two-way connectivity; degraded indicates partial functionality; failed indicates connectivity loss; unknown indicates health check has not been performed.']
  last_health_check_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent automated connectivity health check performed for this GDS connection. Used for SLA monitoring, connectivity uptime reporting, and proactive issue detection.']
  lra_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Last Room Availability (LRA) is enabled for this GDS connection, requiring the property to make the last available room accessible to GDS bookers. LRA is a contractual obligation with some GDS providers and corporate accounts.']
  max_advance_booking_days varchar [note: 'Maximum number of days in advance that reservations can be booked through this GDS connection. Controls the booking window loaded to the GDS and aligns with the Revenue Management System (RMS) inventory control horizon.']
  min_advance_booking_days varchar [note: 'Minimum number of days in advance that reservations must be booked through this GDS connection. Supports Last Room Availability (LRA) and advance purchase rate controls managed by the Revenue Management System.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text operational notes regarding this GDS connection, including special configuration requirements, known issues, contractual exceptions, or migration history. Used by channel distribution and revenue management teams.']
  nrr_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Non-Refundable Rates (NRR) can be loaded and booked through this GDS connection. NRR support is a key revenue management capability enabling advance purchase rate strategies via GDS.']
  property_code_gds varchar [note: 'The unique property identifier as registered within the specific GDS network. May differ from the internal PMS property code. Used for inventory display and booking routing within the GDS.']
  pseudo_city_code varchar [note: 'GDS-assigned Pseudo City Code (PCC) that identifies the travel agency or booking office within the GDS network. Used for commission tracking, segment attribution, and agency relationship management.']
