  is_modified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this booking has been modified after initial confirmation (True = modified, False = original booking). Used to track modification rates by channel and assess channel data quality.']
  is_rate_parity_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the booked rate is compliant with the rate parity agreement for this channel (True = compliant, False = parity violation detected). Critical for OTA contract compliance monitoring and channel rate parity management.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this channel booking transaction record was last updated in the Silver layer data product. RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED per TRANSACTION_HEADER canonical role. Used for change data capture, incremental load processing, and audit compliance.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'The number of days between the booking timestamp and the check-in date. Stored as a business attribute to support lead time distribution analysis by channel, demand forecasting, and last-minute booking pattern identification.']
  length_of_stay_nights varchar [note: 'The number of nights for the booked stay, derived from check-in and check-out dates. Stored as a business attribute to support LOS-based rate restriction analysis, minimum stay controls, and ALOS reporting without requiring date arithmetic at query time.']
  net_revenue_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The net revenue retained by the property after deduction of channel commission from the gross booking value. Primary metric for channel contribution analysis and cost-of-acquisition benchmarking. Part of MONETARY_TRIPLET per TRANSACTION_HEADER canonical role.']
  number_of_guests varchar [note: 'The total number of guests included in this booking. Used for occupancy analysis, per-guest revenue calculations, and channel demand pattern reporting.']
  number_of_rooms varchar [note: 'The number of rooms included in this booking transaction. Typically 1 for FIT (Free Independent Traveler) bookings; may be greater for group or multi-room bookings received through a channel.']
  ota_confirmation_number varchar [note: 'The booking confirmation number assigned by the Online Travel Agency (OTA) platform (e.g., Booking.com, Expedia). Used for reconciliation with OTA partner systems and commission invoice matching.']
  pms_confirmation_number varchar [note: 'The externally-communicated confirmation number generated by the Property Management System (Oracle OPERA PMS) for this booking. Serves as the primary guest-facing booking reference. BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER per TRANSACTION_HEADER canonical role.']
  rate_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the rate type applied to this booking. BAR (Best Available Rate), LRA (Last Room Availability), NRR (Non-Refundable Rate), corporate, package, promotional, or group. Critical for rate parity monitoring and revenue strategy analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: BAR|LRA|NRR|corporate|package|promotional|group — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  source_country varchar [note: 'The ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the origin from which the booking was made through the channel. Used for geographic demand analysis, international vs. domestic channel mix reporting, and targeted marketing attribution.']
  travel_agent_iata_number varchar [note: 'The 8-digit IATA (International Air Transport Association) accreditation number of the travel agency that made this booking. Used for travel agent commission processing, GDS attribution, and agency performance reporting.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of each booking received through a distribution channel, capturing channel-level booking metadata including channel identifier, booking source, OTA confirmation number, GDS segment number, booking timestamp, property, room type, rate plan, stay dates, gross booking value, channel commission amount, net revenue to property, and booking modification/cancellation flags. Primary fact table for channel contribution and cost-of-acquisition analysis.'
}

Table channel.commission_schedule {
  commission_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each commission or fee schedule record in the channel distribution cost management system. Primary key for the commission_schedule data product.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Commission schedules are negotiated as part of OTA co-marketing campaigns; tracking which campaign drove specific commission structures is essential for marketing spend reconciliation, contract compli']
  campaign_offer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign_offer. Business justification: Promotional campaigns frequently include elevated commission incentives for OTA partners tied to specific offers. Finance and channel teams need to link commission schedules to the campaign offer that']
  channel_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.channel_contract. Business justification: Commission schedules define the commission terms negotiated in channel contracts with OTA partners and GDS providers. Adding nullable channel_contract_id FK links commission schedules to their governi']
  channel_rate_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific rate plan (BAR, LRA, NRR, package, negotiated corporate) to which this commission schedule is scoped. Null indicates the schedule applies to all rate plans for the channel partner.']
  corporate_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to guest.corporate_account. Business justification: Commission schedules are negotiated per corporate account (e.g., TMC override rates, volume-tiered commissions). Account managers and finance teams need this link to apply the correct commission sched']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Commission schedules define expense structures that must be allocated to cost centers for departmental P&L reporting, enabling accurate distribution cost analysis and budget variance tracking.']
  distribution_channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.channel. Business justification: Commission schedules define fee structures for specific distribution channels. Many commission schedules per channel (e.g., tiered by volume, rate plan, market segment). Removes redundant channel_type']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Commission schedules are aligned to fiscal periods for budget vs. actual commission expense analysis. Revenue managers and finance teams use this link to evaluate channel commission costs within defin']
  market_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to revenue.market_segment. Business justification: Commission schedules are differentiated by market segment (e.g., corporate negotiated vs. leisure OTA vs. group). Proper FK enables segment-scoped commission schedule lookup and rate loading validatio']
  ota_partner_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the distribution channel partner (OTA, GDS, CRS, channel manager, direct booking engine) to which this commission or fee schedule applies. Links to the channel partner master record.']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific property or hotel to which this commission schedule applies. Null or a portfolio-level sentinel value indicates the schedule applies across all properties (chain-wide agreement).']
  room_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.room_type. Business justification: OTA and travel agent contracts frequently specify differentiated commission rates by room type (e.g., suites vs. standard rooms). A room_type_id FK on commission_schedule enables room-type-specific co']
  applies_to_cancellations boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the commission or fee is still payable when a booking is cancelled (True = commission owed on cancellations, False = commission waived on cancellations). Critical for NRR (Non-Refundable Rate) commission accrual and cancellation cost management.']
  applies_to_no_shows boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the commission or fee is payable on no-show bookings (True = commission owed on no-shows, False = commission waived). Affects no-show revenue net-of-commission calculations and OTA contract compliance.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the authorized approver (e.g., VP Revenue Management, CFO) who approved this commission schedule for execution. Required for SOX financial controls compliance and audit trail.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this commission schedule was formally approved for activation. Part of the SOX-compliant approval audit trail for financial commitments.']
  auto_accrual_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether commission and fee accruals under this schedule are automatically calculated and posted to the GL upon booking confirmation (True) or require manual accrual processing (False). Supports automated AP accrual workflows in SAP S/4HANA.']
  billing_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the channel partner invoices the property for commissions or fees: monthly (most common for OTA commissions), weekly, per_booking (real-time deduction), quarterly, annual (common for connectivity subscription fees). Drives AP accrual scheduling and cash flow forecasting.']
  booking_window_max_days varchar [note: 'Maximum advance booking window (in days before arrival) for which this commission schedule is applicable. Null indicates no maximum booking window restriction. Used in conjunction with booking_window_min_days for last-minute rate commission scoping.']
  booking_window_min_days varchar [note: 'Minimum advance booking window (in days before arrival) for which this commission schedule is applicable. Used for LOS (Length of Stay) and advance purchase rate plan commission scoping. Null indicates no minimum booking window restriction.']
  commission_basis varchar [note: 'The unit basis on which the commission or fee is calculated and accrued: per_booking (one charge per reservation), per_room_night (charge per occupied room night), per_segment (GDS per-segment charge), per_transaction (CRS per-transaction charge), per_period (fixed charge per billing period regardless of volume). Drives accrual engine logic.']
  commission_rate_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Negotiated commission rate expressed as a percentage of the applicable revenue base (e.g., 15.0000 = 15%). Applicable when fee_structure is \'percentage\'. Represents the contractually agreed OTA or agent commission rate. Confidential as it reflects negotiated commercial terms.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this commission schedule record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and SOX financial controls compliance.']
  effective_from date [note: 'The calendar date from which this commission or fee schedule becomes contractually binding and applicable to bookings. Used to determine which schedule version applies to a given booking date for accrual and invoice reconciliation.']
  effective_until date [note: 'The calendar date on which this commission or fee schedule expires and is no longer applicable. Null indicates an open-ended agreement with no defined expiry. Used for schedule versioning and contract renewal tracking.']
  fee_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the flat fee or tiered threshold amounts are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency AP reconciliation and cost-of-acquisition reporting.']
  fee_structure varchar [note: 'Defines how the commission or fee is calculated: percentage (applied as a % of booking revenue or room revenue), flat (fixed monetary amount per booking or period), tiered (rate varies by volume or revenue thresholds), per_unit (fixed amount per room-night, segment, or transaction unit). Determines the accrual calculation method.']
  fee_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the cost type captured by this schedule: commission (percentage of booking revenue paid to OTA/agent), per_segment_fee (GDS per-segment charge), per_transaction_fee (CRS per-booking transaction fee), connectivity_fee (channel manager or switch connectivity charge), subscription_fee (fixed periodic platform fee), override_commission (negotiated override above standard rate). Drives AP coding and cost-of-acquisition (COA) categorization.']
  flat_fee_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Fixed monetary fee amount per booking, segment, transaction, or billing period, expressed in the schedule currency. Applicable when fee_structure is \'flat\' or \'per_unit\'. Used for GDS per-segment fees, CRS per-transaction fees, and connectivity subscription fees. Confidential as it reflects negotiated commercial terms.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'The General Ledger account code in SAP S/4HANA to which commission and fee expenses are posted for financial reporting under USALI. Ensures correct P&L classification of distribution costs (e.g., OTA commission expense, GDS fee expense) per USALI departmental accounting.']
  los_min_nights varchar [note: 'Minimum Length of Stay (LOS) in nights required for this commission schedule to apply. Null indicates no LOS restriction. Supports commission scoping for extended-stay or minimum-stay rate plans.']
  max_commission_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Contractual cap on the commission or fee amount payable per booking or per billing period. Applicable for schedules with a commission ceiling clause. Expressed in fee_currency_code. Confidential as it reflects negotiated commercial terms.']
  min_commission_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Contractual minimum commission or fee amount payable per booking or per billing period, regardless of the calculated percentage amount. Applicable for schedules with a commission floor clause. Expressed in fee_currency_code. Confidential as it reflects negotiated commercial terms.']
  negotiated_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the internal team or individual (e.g., Revenue Management, Channel Distribution, Procurement) responsible for negotiating this commission schedule. Used for accountability tracking and contract ownership.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing additional context, special contractual conditions, negotiation history notes, or exceptions associated with this commission schedule. Not used for structured data; intended for operational reference by Revenue Management and AP teams.']
  payment_terms_days varchar [note: 'Number of days from invoice receipt within which payment to the channel partner is due (e.g., 30 = Net 30). Used in AP aging analysis and cash flow management per SAP S/4HANA payment terms configuration.']
  rate_parity_monitored boolean [note: 'Indicates whether rate parity compliance is contractually required and actively monitored for bookings under this commission schedule (True = rate parity clause in effect). Supports rate parity monitoring workflows and OTA contract compliance management.']
  revenue_base varchar [note: 'Defines the revenue component on which the commission percentage is calculated: room_revenue (room charges only), total_revenue (all charges including F&B and ancillaries), net_revenue (after discounts and taxes), gross_revenue (before deductions), f_and_b_revenue (food and beverage charges only). Critical for accurate commission accrual and invoice reconciliation.']
  schedule_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this commission or fee schedule, used in AP reconciliation, contract references, and channel partner communications (e.g., OTA-EXP-COMM-2024, GDS-SAB-SEG-FEE).']
  schedule_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name for the commission or fee schedule, used in reporting dashboards, AP workflows, and channel profitability analysis (e.g., \'Expedia Standard OTA Commission FY2024\', \'Sabre GDS Per-Segment Fee Q1 2024\').']
  schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the commission schedule: active (currently in force), inactive (temporarily suspended), pending (approved but not yet effective), expired (past effective_until date), terminated (cancelled before expiry), draft (under negotiation, not yet approved). Drives AP processing eligibility and accrual inclusion.']
  tier_1_rate_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Commission rate percentage applicable when booking volume or revenue falls within the Tier 1 threshold band in a tiered fee structure. Confidential as it reflects negotiated commercial terms.']
  tier_1_threshold decimal(18,4) [note: 'Lower volume or revenue threshold (in booking count or currency amount) that triggers the Tier 1 commission or fee rate in a tiered fee structure. Applicable when fee_structure is \'tiered\'. Confidential as it reflects negotiated commercial terms.']
  tier_2_rate_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Commission rate percentage applicable when booking volume or revenue falls within the Tier 2 threshold band in a tiered fee structure. Confidential as it reflects negotiated commercial terms.']
  tier_2_threshold decimal(18,4) [note: 'Upper volume or revenue threshold that triggers the Tier 2 commission or fee rate in a tiered fee structure. Applicable when fee_structure is \'tiered\'. Confidential as it reflects negotiated commercial terms.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this commission schedule record was last modified. Used for change tracking, audit trail, and incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks Silver Layer.']

  Note: 'Master and transactional records for all channel distribution costs — the single source of truth for OTA commissions, GDS segment fees, CRS transaction fees, channel manager connectivity fees, and per-booking cost accruals. Captures commission/fee structures (percentage, flat, tiered), negotiated rates per partner, applicable rate plans, effective date ranges, property scope, fee types, billing frequencies, and per-booking accrual records with amounts in local and base currency. Supports commission invoice reconciliation, AP processing, total cost-of-acquisition calculation, technology cost management, and channel profitability analysis. Encompasses all cost types previously tracked separately (connectivity fees, per-segment fees, per-transaction fees) in a unified cost management product.'
}

Removes redundant booking_source_code which can be retriev']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the distribution channel through which the booking was made (e.g., OTA, GDS, direct web, voice). Links to the channel master in the channel domain for channel mix and cost-of-acquisition reporting.']
  channel_rate_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.channel_rate_plan. Business justification: Commission rates vary by channel rate plan. The commission_accrual table has rate_plan_code (STRING) which should be normalized to channel_rate_plan via FK. This links commission accruals to the speci']
  commission_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the commission schedule or rate agreement applied to this accrual record. Determines the applicable commission rate tier, calculation method, and contractual terms with the OTA or GDS partner.']
  corporate_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to guest.corporate_account. Business justification: Corporate bookings generate commissions that must be attributed to the corporate account for AR reconciliation, account performance reporting, and annual room-night target tracking. Finance and accoun']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Commission accruals are distribution expenses requiring cost center allocation for accurate departmental financial statements, enabling proper matching of channel costs to revenue per USALI reporting ']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Commission accruals must be posted to the correct fiscal period for USALI-compliant P&L reporting and period-end close. Finance controllers use this link to produce period-accurate commission expense ']
  market_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to revenue.market_segment. Business justification: Commission cost-of-acquisition analysis by market segment is a standard revenue management report. Linking accruals to market_segment enables segment-level distribution cost benchmarking and USALI-com']
  negotiated_rate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to revenue.revenue_negotiated_rate. Business justification: Negotiated rates carry specific commission terms (commission_pct, iata_number). Linking commission_accrual to revenue_negotiated_rate enables verification that accrued commissions match the contracted']
  ota_partner_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the OTA or GDS partner entity responsible for the booking. Populated when the channel type is OTA or GDS; null for direct bookings. Supports OTA partnership performance and commission reconciliation reporting.']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the hotel or resort property for which the commission is being accrued. Enables property-level commission cost reporting and CPOR (Cost Per Occupied Room) analysis.']
  reservation_booking_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reservation.reservation_booking. Business justification: Commission accruals must link directly to reservations for reconciliation, dispute resolution, cancellation-driven commission reversals, and complete audit trail from booking to financial posting. Cur']
  accrual_date date [note: 'The accounting date on which the commission liability was recognized in the general ledger. Aligns with USALI period-end accrual practices and SAP S/4HANA GL posting date. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP category.']
  accrual_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the commission accrual record. Drives AP (Accounts Payable) workflow in SAP S/4HANA: \'accrued\' = liability recognized; \'invoiced\' = OTA invoice received; \'paid\' = payment disbursed; \'disputed\' = under reconciliation review; \'cancelled\' = booking cancelled before stay; \'reversed\' = accrual reversed due to no-show or refund. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER LIFECYCLE_STATUS category.']
  adr decimal(18,4) [note: 'The Average Daily Rate of the booking, calculated as gross_booking_value divided by room_nights. Stored for channel performance benchmarking and to support STR STAR Report comparisons without requiring recalculation from source.']
  cancellation_date date [note: 'The date on which the reservation was cancelled, if applicable. When populated, triggers commission accrual reversal logic per the cancellation policy terms. Null for active or completed bookings.']
  channel_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the distribution channel through which the booking originated. Drives commission rate tier selection and channel mix reporting. OTA = Online Travel Agency (e.g., Expedia, Booking.com); GDS = Global Distribution System (e.g., Sabre, Amadeus); direct_web = brand.com; voice = call center; corporate = negotiated corporate account; wholesale = tour operator/wholesaler.']
  commission_amount_base decimal(18,4) [note: 'The commission amount converted to the company\'s functional base currency (typically USD) for consolidated financial reporting and USALI-compliant P&L statements. Enables cross-property and cross-currency commission cost analysis. Part of TRANSACTION_HEADER MONETARY_TRIPLET.']
  commission_amount_local decimal(18,4) [note: 'The calculated commission amount payable to the distribution channel partner in the local transaction currency (gross_booking_value × commission_rate). Used for OTA invoice reconciliation and AP liability posting. Part of TRANSACTION_HEADER MONETARY_TRIPLET.']
  commission_basis varchar [note: 'Defines the revenue base on which the commission rate is applied: \'room_revenue\' = room charges only; \'total_revenue\' = all charges including F&B and ancillary; \'net_revenue\' = revenue net of taxes and fees; \'flat_fee\' = fixed amount per booking regardless of revenue.']
  commission_rate decimal(8,6) [note: 'The contractual commission rate applied to the gross booking value, expressed as a decimal fraction (e.g., 0.15 = 15%). Sourced from the applicable commission schedule. Used to calculate the commission amount and for rate parity and cost-of-acquisition analysis.']
  commission_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the commission arrangement: \'standard\' = default contractual rate; \'override\' = manually overridden rate approved by revenue management; \'promotional\' = time-limited promotional rate; \'group\' = group booking commission; \'negotiated\' = bilaterally negotiated rate outside standard schedule.']
  connectivity_fee_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The GDS or channel connectivity fee charged by the distribution technology provider (e.g., Sabre SynXis transaction fee) associated with this booking, in local currency. Included in total cost-of-acquisition (CoA) reporting alongside commission.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this commission accrual record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for audit trail and SOX financial controls compliance. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category.']
  dispute_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes describing the nature of a commission dispute with the OTA or GDS partner. Populated when accrual_status is \'disputed\'. Supports the channel dispute resolution workflow and AP reconciliation process.']
  fx_rate decimal(18,8) [note: 'The exchange rate applied to convert the local currency commission amount to the base currency at the time of accrual. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA treasury rates. Required for accurate multi-currency financial reporting and audit trail.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'The SAP S/4HANA GL account code to which the commission expense is posted (e.g., USALI Schedule 6 - Sales and Marketing / Distribution Costs). Ensures correct financial statement classification per USALI and GAAP/IFRS.']
  gross_booking_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'The total gross booking value of the reservation on which commission is calculated, in the local transaction currency. Represents the full room revenue amount before any discounts or adjustments. Forms the base for commission rate application. Part of TRANSACTION_HEADER MONETARY_TRIPLET.']
  invoice_date date [note: 'The date on the commission invoice received from the OTA or GDS partner. Used for AP aging analysis, payment due date calculation, and period-end accrual reconciliation in SAP S/4HANA.']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'The OTA or GDS partner\'s invoice reference number received for commission payment. Used to match accrued commission liabilities against received invoices in SAP S/4HANA AP for three-way reconciliation. Null until invoice is received.']
  is_commissionable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the booking is eligible for commission payment (True) or is a non-commissionable booking (False), such as direct loyalty redemptions, staff rates, or complimentary stays. Drives commission accrual logic and AP posting.']
  local_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the transaction currency in which the booking was made and commission is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency commission reconciliation and FX conversion. Part of TRANSACTION_HEADER MONETARY_TRIPLET.']
  payment_date date [note: 'The actual date on which the commission payment was disbursed to the OTA or GDS partner. Populated when accrual_status transitions to \'paid\'. Used for AP reconciliation and cash flow reporting.']
  payment_due_date date [note: 'The contractual due date by which the commission payment must be disbursed to the OTA or GDS partner per the commission schedule terms. Used for AP cash flow planning and SLA compliance monitoring.']
  payment_reference varchar [note: 'The payment transaction reference or bank transfer reference number from SAP S/4HANA AP when the commission is disbursed. Enables end-to-end traceability from accrual to cash payment for audit and reconciliation purposes.']
  reversal_reason varchar [note: 'The reason code for reversing a previously accrued commission. Populated when accrual_status is \'reversed\' or \'cancelled\'. Supports AP reconciliation and channel dispute management. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: cancellation|no_show|rate_correction|duplicate|dispute_resolved|other — promote to reference product if additional values are needed]']
  room_nights varchar [note: 'The number of room nights associated with the booking for which commission is being accrued. Used to calculate CPOR (Cost Per Occupied Room) and to normalize commission costs across bookings of varying LOS (Length of Stay).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this commission accrual record originated: \'OPERA_PMS\' = Oracle OPERA PMS cashiering module; \'SYNXIS_CRS\' = Sabre SynXis CRS channel management; \'SAP_AP\' = SAP S/4HANA AP accrual; \'MANUAL\' = manually entered adjustment. Supports data lineage and ETL audit.']
  total_cost_of_acquisition decimal(18,4) [note: 'The total distribution cost for this booking, combining commission_amount_local and connectivity_fee_amount. Represents the all-in cost of acquiring the booking through this channel. Used for channel profitability and net RevPAR analysis.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this commission accrual record, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports change tracking, reconciliation audit trails, and SOX financial controls. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED category.']

  Note: 'Transactional records of commission amounts accrued per booking for each distribution channel. Captures booking reference, channel, OTA partner, stay date, commission schedule applied, gross booking value, commission rate, commission amount in local and base currency, accrual date, and payment status. Feeds into finance AP for commission invoice reconciliation and channel cost-of-acquisition reporting.'
}

Table channel.channel_contract {
  channel_contract_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the channel contract record in the lakehouse silver layer. Primary key for this entity.']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the distribution channel (OTA, GDS, direct, etc.) that is party to this contract.']
  corporate_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to guest.corporate_account. Business justification: Channel contracts are frequently negotiated on behalf of a corporate account (e.g., TMC master agreement, corporate GDS contract). Linking channel_contract to corporate_account enables contract compli']
  gds_connection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.gds_connection. Business justification: Channel contracts may reference specific GDS connection configurations, particularly for chain-level GDS agreements that are implemented at the property connection level. Many contracts per GDS connec']
  ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Channel contracts create financial obligations requiring GL account assignment for proper revenue/expense recognition, audit trail compliance, and financial statement preparation per accounting standa']
  ota_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.ota_partner. Business justification: Channel contracts are commercial agreements signed with OTA partners, defining commission rates, rate parity obligations, and terms. Many contracts per OTA partner (e.g., master agreement, property-sp']
  ownership_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.ownership_entity. Business justification: Legal contract counterparty process: in ownership-managed hotel structures, the ownership entity (not the operating property) is the legal signatory on channel distribution contracts. Finance and lega']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property or portfolio of properties covered by this contract. Null if the contract applies at the brand or enterprise level.']
  bar_access boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the channel is contractually entitled to receive the Best Available Rate (BAR) as defined in the Revenue Management System. Drives rate loading rules in Sabre SynXis CRS.']
  cancellation_policy_code varchar [note: 'Code referencing the contracted cancellation and no-show policy applicable to bookings made through this channel (e.g., \'24H\', \'48H\', \'NON_REF\'). Loaded into Oracle OPERA PMS and Sabre SynXis CRS.']
  commission_basis varchar [note: 'Defines the revenue base on which the commission rate is applied: net rate (after discounts), gross rate (before discounts), room revenue only, or total revenue including F&B and ancillaries.']
  commission_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Base commission rate (expressed as a decimal, e.g., 0.1500 = 15%) payable to the channel partner per confirmed booking under this contract. Distinct from the commission_schedule product which captures tiered or conditional rate structures.']
  connectivity_fee decimal(15,2) [note: 'Fixed or per-transaction fee charged by the channel or GDS for technical connectivity and distribution services. Distinct from commission which is percentage-based. Used in cost-of-acquisition (COA) analysis.']
  connectivity_fee_type varchar [note: 'Specifies how the connectivity fee is structured: per booking transaction, per GDS segment, annual flat fee, or monthly flat fee.']
  content_requirements varchar [note: 'Free-text description of contractual obligations regarding property content (images, descriptions, amenities) that must be maintained on the channel platform, including minimum image counts, refresh frequency, and accuracy standards.']
  contract_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name for the contract (e.g., \'Expedia 2025 Master Distribution Agreement — Americas\').']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known legal contract reference number assigned by the contracting parties or the legal department. Used for correspondence, audits, and dispute resolution.']
  contract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the contract. Drives whether the channel is eligible to receive inventory and rates via the CRS.']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the commercial agreement by channel category. Determines applicable commission structures, rate parity obligations, and connectivity requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ota_distribution|gds_participation|direct_connect|wholesale|bed_bank|metasearch|corporate_negotiated — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this contract record was first created in the system of record. Used for audit trail and SOX compliance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which all monetary values in this contract are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  data_sharing_agreement boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract includes a data sharing or data processing agreement governing the exchange of guest and booking data with the channel partner. Required for GDPR Article 28 compliance.']
  dispute_resolution_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the legal jurisdiction governing dispute resolution and contract interpretation.']
  dispute_resolution_mechanism varchar [note: 'Contractually agreed mechanism for resolving commercial disputes between the hotel and the channel partner.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the contract becomes legally binding and the channel is authorized to distribute inventory. Aligns with USALI period reporting.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the contract ceases to be binding. Null for evergreen contracts. Used to trigger renewal workflows and rate parity audits.']
  gdpr_dpa_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or document identifier for the GDPR Data Processing Agreement (DPA) executed with the channel partner. Mandatory for EU-based OTA and GDS partnerships under GDPR Article 28.']
  invoice_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which commission invoices or settlement statements are issued between the hotel and the channel partner.']
  lra_obligation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract includes a Last Room Availability (LRA) obligation, requiring the hotel to make the last available room accessible to this channel even when inventory is restricted for other channels.']
  marketing_coop_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Annual or contract-period monetary commitment for co-operative marketing spend with the channel partner (e.g., sponsored listings, display advertising on OTA platform). Expressed in the contract currency.']
  nrr_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract permits the hotel to offer Non-Refundable Rates (NRR) through this channel. Relevant for revenue management strategy and cancellation policy governance.']
  payment_model varchar [note: 'Commercial payment model governing how the channel collects and remits payment: merchant model (channel collects from guest and remits net), agency model (hotel collects and pays commission), net rate (channel marks up a net rate), or wholesale.']
  pci_compliance_confirmed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the channel partner has confirmed PCI DSS compliance as required by the contract terms for handling payment card data.']
  preferred_partner_tier varchar [note: 'Tier designation within the OTA or GDS preferred partner program (e.g., Expedia Preferred Plus, Booking.com Preferred). Higher tiers typically confer better placement, lower commission rates, or co-op marketing benefits.']
  rate_parity_clause boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract includes a rate parity obligation requiring the hotel to offer the channel the same or better rates as other distribution channels. Critical for revenue management compliance and OTA relationship governance.']
  rate_parity_type varchar [note: 'Specifies the scope of the rate parity obligation: broad parity (match all channels including direct), narrow parity (match only other OTAs, not direct), or no parity obligation. Reflects EU regulatory distinctions post-2015 investigations.']
  renewal_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days prior to expiration_date by which either party must provide written notice of intent to renew or terminate. Drives automated contract renewal alerts.']
  renewal_type varchar [note: 'Specifies whether the contract auto-renews at expiration, requires manual renegotiation, or is a one-time non-renewable agreement.']
  signed_date date [note: 'Date on which all parties executed the contract. Used for legal audit trails and SOX compliance.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this contract record was ingested into the lakehouse (e.g., SAP S/4HANA Contract Management, Sabre SynXis CRS, Oracle OPERA PMS, or manually entered).']
  termination_date date [note: 'Actual date the contract was terminated early, if applicable. Distinct from expiration_date which is the scheduled end. Populated only when contract_status is \'terminated\'.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this contract record. Used for change data capture (CDC) in the lakehouse ETL pipeline and audit compliance.']

  Note: 'Master records for commercial contracts with OTA partners and GDS providers, capturing contract type, effective and expiration dates, contracted commission rates, rate parity clauses, last-room-availability (LRA) obligations, preferred partner program terms, marketing co-op commitments, and dispute resolution terms. Distinct from commission_schedule (which captures the rate structure) — this is the legal agreement record.'
}

Table channel.stop_sell {
  stop_sell_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each channel stop-sell, close-out, or restriction control record in the Silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for this transactional entity.']
  booking_source_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.booking_source. Business justification: Stop-sell restrictions can be applied to specific booking sources within a channel for granular inventory control. While stop_sell already has channel_id for channel-level restrictions, adding nullabl']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the distribution channel (OTA, GDS, direct web, voice, etc.) to which this stop-sell restriction is applied. Links to the channel master record.']
  channel_rate_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the rate plan (BAR, NRR, LRA, package, etc.) to which this restriction is scoped. Null indicates the restriction applies across all rate plans for the channel and room type combination.']
  inventory_control_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to revenue.inventory_control. Business justification: Stop-sell actions are triggered by inventory_control records (hurdle rates exceeded, overbooking caps reached, sell limits hit). FK links the channel-level stop-sell action to the revenue-side invento']
  market_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to revenue.market_segment. Business justification: Stop-sell restrictions are segment-specific in revenue management — closing OTA transient while keeping corporate open is a standard tactic. FK enables segment-scoped restriction audit and RMS-driven ']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the hotel or resort property to which this stop-sell restriction applies. Links to the property master record in the Property domain.']
  room_type_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific room type (e.g., King Deluxe, Double Standard) subject to the stop-sell restriction. Null indicates the restriction applies to all room types at the property.']
  strategy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to revenue.strategy. Business justification: Stop-sell actions are tactical executions of revenue strategies (closing channels when demand exceeds forecast thresholds). FK links each stop-sell to the governing strategy, enabling strategy-level a']
  action_type varchar [note: 'Type of inventory control action recorded in this transaction. apply: restriction manually applied; lift: restriction manually removed; modify: restriction parameters changed; auto_apply: restriction applied by RMS automation rule; auto_lift: restriction lifted by RMS automation rule.']
  adr_at_apply decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average Daily Rate (ADR) for the affected room type and date range at the time the restriction was applied. Provides revenue management context for the stop-sell decision and supports yield optimization analysis.']
  applied_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or employee identifier of the revenue management or channel management user who applied this restriction. Supports accountability and audit trail for inventory control actions.']
  approval_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this restriction action required managerial approval before being applied. Supports internal control workflows and SOX compliance for material inventory control decisions.']
  approved_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or employee identifier of the manager or supervisor who approved this restriction action when approval_required is True. Null if no approval was required or if the action was system-generated.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the restriction action was approved by the designated manager or supervisor. Null if no approval was required. Supports SOX audit trail for material inventory control decisions.']
  channel_manager_reference varchar [note: 'External reference identifier assigned by the channel manager or connectivity platform (e.g., SiteMinder, Amadeus Central Reservations) when the restriction was transmitted to the distribution channel. Supports cross-system reconciliation.']
  channel_type varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the distribution channel. ota: Online Travel Agency (OTA); gds: Global Distribution System (GDS); direct: brand.com or direct booking; voice: call center or CRS voice; wholesaler: tour operator or bed bank; metasearch: Google Hotels, Trivago; corporate: negotiated corporate accounts; group: group/MICE bookings. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ota|gds|direct|voice|wholesaler|metasearch|corporate|group — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp recording when this stop-sell restriction record was first captured in the system. Distinct from effective_timestamp which records when the restriction became operationally active.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to the ADR and any monetary values recorded in this restriction record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency property operations.']
  days_out varchar [note: 'Number of days between the date the restriction was applied and the first affected stay date (stay_date_from). Represents the booking window horizon at which the restriction was enacted, used for revenue strategy analysis.']
  effective_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The real-world date and time when this restriction became active and was enforced across the channel. This is the principal business event timestamp for this transactional record, distinct from the audit creation timestamp.']
  is_all_channels boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this restriction applies to all distribution channels simultaneously rather than a single specific channel. True when a property-wide close-out is executed across all channels; False when scoped to a specific channel.']
  is_all_room_types boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this restriction applies to all room types at the property rather than a specific room type. True when the restriction is property-wide across all room categories.']
  is_system_generated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this restriction was automatically generated by the Revenue Management System (RMS) or channel manager automation rule, as opposed to being manually applied by a revenue manager.']
  lift_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the business reason for lifting the restriction. Examples: DEMAND_SOFTENED, RATE_PARITY_RESTORED, MANUAL_OVERRIDE, SCHEDULED_EXPIRY, CHANNEL_RENEGOTIATED. Null if restriction has not been lifted. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: DEMAND_SOFTENED|RATE_PARITY_RESTORED|MANUAL_OVERRIDE|SCHEDULED_EXPIRY|CHANNEL_RENEGOTIATED|SYSTEM_AUTO — promote to reference product]']
  lift_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the restriction was lifted or removed, re-opening inventory on the affected channel. Null if the restriction is still active or has not yet been lifted.']
  lifted_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or employee identifier of the user who lifted or removed this restriction. Null if the restriction has not been lifted or was lifted automatically by the system.']
  max_los varchar [note: 'Maximum number of nights a guest is permitted to stay when booking on this channel during the restricted date range. Applicable when restriction_type is max_los. Null for non-LOS restriction types.']
  min_los varchar [note: 'Minimum number of nights a guest must stay to be eligible for booking on this channel during the restricted date range. Applicable when restriction_type is min_los. Null for non-LOS restriction types.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes or comments entered by the revenue manager or channel manager regarding this restriction. May include context about competitive positioning, demand events, or channel negotiation details.']
  occupancy_at_apply decimal(5,2) [note: 'Property or room-type occupancy percentage at the time the restriction was applied. Provides context for the revenue management decision and supports post-hoc analysis of restriction effectiveness. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 85.50 = 85.50%).']
  rate_parity_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this stop-sell action was triggered by a rate parity breach on the affected channel. True when the restriction is applied to enforce rate parity compliance across distribution channels.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the business reason for applying the stop-sell or restriction. Examples include: HIGH_DEMAND, RATE_PARITY_BREACH, CHANNEL_PERFORMANCE, OVERBOOKING_PROTECTION, RENOVATION, ALLOTMENT_EXHAUSTED. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: HIGH_DEMAND|RATE_PARITY_BREACH|CHANNEL_PERFORMANCE|OVERBOOKING_PROTECTION|RENOVATION|ALLOTMENT_EXHAUSTED|SYSTEM_AUTO — promote to reference product]']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative providing additional context or justification for the restriction beyond the standardized reason code. Entered by the revenue manager or channel manager at the time of action.']
  restriction_duration_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days spanned by the restriction date range (stay_date_to minus stay_date_from + 1). Supports analysis of restriction scope and revenue impact assessment.']
  restriction_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for this stop-sell or restriction control action, as generated by the CRS (Sabre SynXis) or channel manager. Used for cross-system traceability and audit reconciliation.']
  restriction_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the stop-sell or restriction control record. active: restriction is currently enforced; lifted: restriction has been removed; pending: restriction is queued for activation; cancelled: restriction was voided before activation; expired: restriction passed its scheduled end without explicit lift.']
  restriction_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the inventory restriction being applied to the channel. stop_sell: no new bookings accepted; closed_to_arrival (CTA): no check-ins on the stay date; closed_to_departure (CTD): no check-outs on the stay date; min_los: minimum Length of Stay (LOS) required; max_los: maximum LOS allowed; close_out: full inventory closure.']
  revpar_at_apply decimal(10,2) [note: 'Revenue Per Available Room (RevPAR) for the affected property or room type at the time the restriction was applied. Key revenue management KPI used to contextualize the stop-sell decision and evaluate channel mix optimization outcomes.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record that originated this stop-sell or restriction control action. Identifies whether the action was initiated from the CRS (Sabre SynXis), RMS (IDeaS G3), PMS (Oracle OPERA), demand forecasting system (Infor EzRMS), or entered manually.']
  stay_date_from date [note: 'First stay date (inclusive) in the date range to which this restriction applies. Represents the beginning of the affected inventory window in the property calendar.']
  stay_date_to date [note: 'Last stay date (inclusive) in the date range to which this restriction applies. Represents the end of the affected inventory window in the property calendar.']
  transmission_status varchar [note: 'Status of the restriction transmission to the distribution channel via the CRS or channel manager. pending: queued for transmission; transmitted: sent to channel; confirmed: acknowledged by channel; failed: transmission error; retrying: re-attempting after failure. Critical for ensuring inventory control actions are successfully propagated.']
  transmission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the restriction was successfully transmitted to the distribution channel via the CRS or channel manager. Null if transmission is pending or failed. Supports SLA monitoring for inventory control propagation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp recording the most recent modification to this restriction record, such as a status change, date range extension, or reason code update.']

  Note: 'Transactional records of stop-sell, close-out, and restriction controls applied to specific channels, room types, and date ranges. Captures channel, property, room type, stay date range, restriction type (stop-sell, minimum LOS, closed to arrival, closed to departure), reason code, applied-by user, effective timestamp, and lift timestamp. Supports revenue management inventory control actions executed through the CRS and channel manager to optimize channel mix and protect direct booking rates.'
}

Table event.account {
  account_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the event account. Primary key for the B2B client entity that books MICE (Meetings, Incentives, Conferences, Exhibitions) events.']
  guest_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.guest_segment. Business justification: Corporate event accounts are classified into segments (high-value, regional, industry vertical) for targeted marketing. Enables account-based marketing, personalized campaign targeting, and segment-sp']
  parent_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent event account if this account is part of a corporate hierarchy or national account structure. Enables roll-up reporting and consolidated billing.']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary or preferred property where the event account typically books events. Used for account assignment and relationship management.']
  tier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.tier. Business justification: Corporate event accounts carry loyalty tier classifications (e.g., Platinum corporate) determining pricing concessions, service levels, and benefit entitlements. The existing plain-text \'account_tier\'']
  account_name varchar [note: 'Legal or operating name of the corporate, association, or group client organization that books MICE events.']
  account_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the event account. Used in contracts, proposals, and invoicing. Typically assigned by the CRS (Central Reservation System) or Delphi system.']
  account_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the event account. Active accounts can book new events; suspended accounts require approval; closed accounts are archived.']
  account_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the event account based on the nature of the client organization. Determines pricing strategies, contract terms, and service offerings.']
  average_event_spend_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Average revenue per event for this account. Calculated as lifetime event spend divided by total events booked. Used for forecasting and opportunity sizing.']
  billing_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the billing address for the event account, including street number and name.']
  billing_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the billing address for additional address details such as suite, floor, or building number.']
  billing_city varchar [note: 'City name for the billing address of the event account.']
  billing_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the billing address of the event account.']
  billing_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the billing address of the event account.']
  billing_state_province varchar [note: 'State or province code for the billing address of the event account.']
  closed_date date [note: 'Date when the event account was closed or deactivated. Null for active accounts. Used for account lifecycle reporting and reactivation analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the event account record was first created in the system. Used for account age analysis and audit trails.']
  credit_limit_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum credit amount approved for the event account. Used to control exposure and manage accounts receivable risk.']
  credit_status varchar [note: 'Current credit approval status for the event account. Determines whether the account can book events on credit terms or requires prepayment.']
  external_account_reference varchar [note: 'Account identifier from the source system (e.g., Delphi Account ID, Salesforce Account ID). Used for system integration and data lineage tracking.']
  industry_vertical varchar [note: 'Primary industry sector or vertical market of the client organization (e.g., Technology, Healthcare, Finance, Education, Manufacturing). Used for market segmentation and targeted sales strategies.']
  is_national_account boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this is a national or enterprise-wide account with negotiated rates and terms across multiple properties.']
  is_vip_account boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this is a VIP or strategic account requiring special handling, executive attention, or premium service levels.']
  last_event_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent event held by this account. Used for recency analysis and account reactivation campaigns.']
  lifetime_event_spend_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total cumulative revenue generated from all events booked by this account since inception. Key metric for account tier classification and relationship value assessment.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing special requirements, preferences, historical context, or relationship management information for the event account.']
  payment_terms_days varchar [note: 'Number of days allowed for payment after invoice date (e.g., Net 30, Net 60). Defines the credit period for the event account.']
  preferred_event_type varchar [note: 'Most frequently booked event type by this account (e.g., Conference, Training, Board Meeting, Product Launch). Used for targeted marketing and service customization.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address of the main contact person for event inquiries, proposals, and communications.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary contact person at the client organization responsible for event planning and booking decisions.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number of the main contact person for event coordination and urgent communications.']
  sales_territory_code varchar [note: 'Geographic or market-based sales territory code to which this event account is assigned for sales planning and performance tracking.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record where this event account was originally created (e.g., Delphi by Amadeus, Salesforce CRM).']
  tax_id_number varchar [note: 'Tax identification number or employer identification number (EIN) of the client organization for tax reporting and compliance.']
  total_events_booked_count varchar [note: 'Total number of events booked by this account since inception. Used for relationship depth analysis and loyalty assessment.']
  typical_attendee_count varchar [note: 'Average number of attendees for events booked by this account. Used for capacity planning and function space allocation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the event account record was last modified. Used for data freshness tracking and change audit.']

  Note: 'Master record for the corporate, association, or group client account that books MICE events. Captures organization name, industry vertical, account tier, primary and secondary contacts, credit terms, preferred properties, historical event spend, and assigned account/sales manager. Serves as the B2B client identity anchor for the event domain — distinct from the guest domain\'s individual traveler profile and from the finance domain\'s AR customer record. All inquiries, proposals, bookings, and revenue link to this entity for relationship management, account-level performance reporting, and sales territory assignment.'
}

Linking enables lead source attribution and conversion funnel analysis by channel — a standard event sales reporting requirement.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Event inquiries are generated by marketing campaigns (email campaigns, paid search, social media). Critical for lead source tracking, campaign effectiveness measurement, and calculating inquiry-to-boo']

Critical for lead source tracking, channel effectiveness a']

Business justification: Hospitality sales teams classify incoming inquiries against CRM guest segments to enable segment-level pipeline conversion reporting and targeted follow-up. Business justification: Event inquiries frequently originate from loyalty members leveraging their status for group bookings.   client_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the primary contact person from the client organization.']
  client_organization_name varchar [note: 'Name of the organization or company submitting the MICE inquiry.']
  competitor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the competitor property or venue that won the business if the inquiry was lost to competition.']

  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the inquiry record was first created in the Delphi by Amadeus system.']

  event_name varchar [note: 'Name or title of the proposed event as provided by the prospective client.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Type of MICE event requested (meeting, conference, incentive, exhibition, wedding, social event, or other). Aligns with MICE classification standards. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: meeting|conference|incentive|exhibition|wedding|social|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  function_space_requirements varchar [note: 'Description of function space requirements including room types, capacities, setup styles, and special configurations needed for the event.']
  group_room_block_estimate varchar [note: 'Estimated number of guest rooms required for the group block to accommodate event attendees.']
  inquiry_date date [note: 'Date when the MICE inquiry or RFP was received by the property or sales team.']
  inquiry_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique inquiry reference number generated by Delphi by Amadeus for tracking and communication with prospective clients.']
  inquiry_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the MICE inquiry in the sales pipeline. Tracks progression from initial lead through conversion or closure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: new|qualified|proposal_sent|negotiation|converted|lost|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  lead_owner_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the sales manager or event coordinator assigned as the owner of this inquiry.']
  lead_owner_name varchar [note: 'Name of the sales manager or event coordinator assigned as the owner of this inquiry.']
  lead_score varchar [note: 'Numeric score assigned to the inquiry based on qualification criteria such as budget fit, date flexibility, and conversion probability. Used for prioritization.']
  lost_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why the inquiry was lost or not converted (e.g., budget constraints, dates unavailable, competitor selected). Populated when inquiry_status is \'lost\'.']
  market_segment varchar [note: 'Market segment classification for the inquiry (corporate, association, government, SMERF, incentive, other). Used for revenue management and reporting.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the inquiry record was last modified or updated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing additional details, special requests, or context provided by the prospective client or recorded by the sales team.']
  preferred_end_date date [note: 'Preferred or requested end date for the event as specified by the prospective client.']
  preferred_start_date date [note: 'Preferred or requested start date for the event as specified by the prospective client.']
  qualification_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the inquiry has been qualified as a viable sales opportunity based on budget, dates, and requirements alignment.']
  referral_source varchar [note: 'Name or description of the referral source if the inquiry came through a referral (e.g., past client, partner organization, industry association).']
  room_nights_estimate varchar [note: 'Estimated total room nights (rooms × nights) for the group block.']
  special_requirements varchar [note: 'Any special requirements or requests from the client including accessibility needs, branding, security, or unique setup configurations.']

  Note: 'Initial MICE lead or request for proposal (RFP) received from a prospective event client. Captures the inquiry source channel (OTA, direct, GDS, referral), event type (meeting, conference, incentive, exhibition), estimated attendance, preferred dates, function space requirements, catering needs, group room block estimate, budget range, decision timeline, and lead owner. Represents the top-of-funnel stage in the Delphi by Amadeus event sales pipeline.'
}

  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary contact person for event communications.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary contact person for the event booking.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the primary contact person for event coordination.']
  proposal_sent_date date [note: 'Date when the event proposal was sent to the client.']
  room_block_count varchar [note: 'Total number of guest rooms blocked for the event group.']
  room_block_cutoff_date date [note: 'Date by which attendees must book rooms from the group block before unreserved rooms are released back to general inventory.']
  room_block_pickup_count varchar [note: 'Number of rooms from the block that have been reserved by attendees.']
  special_requirements varchar [note: 'Any special requests, accommodations, or requirements for the event such as ADA accessibility, dietary restrictions, or technical needs.']
  status_changed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the booking status was last changed. Critical for tracking booking lifecycle progression.']

  Note: 'Confirmed or tentative event booking representing a MICE engagement at a specific property. Acts as the master transactional record tying together all sub-components: group room blocks, function space allocations, BEOs, contracts, invoices, revenue, and attendee registrations. Captures event name, MICE category, contracted dates, total contracted value, deposit schedule, cancellation terms, attrition clauses, cutoff dates, booking status lifecycle (tentative, definite, cancelled, completed, lost) with status change history, owning sales manager, event coordinator, concession tracking, and commission details. Absorbs status audit trail, concession management, and commission tracking as embedded attributes rather than separate entities. The central hub entity for all event operations, reporting, and cross-domain integration with workforce (staffing assignments) and finance (AR posting).'
}

  event_group_block_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the event group room block. Primary key.']
  booking_source_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.booking_source.   cancellation_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reservation.cancellation_policy.   distribution_channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.channel. Business justification: Group room blocks may be distributed through specific channels (wholesaler channels, OTA group inventory allocation, direct corporate booking portals).
