  event_booking_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent event booking for which this group room block is allocated. Links to the master event contract.']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property where the group room block is allocated.']
  reservation_rate_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reservation.reservation_rate_plan. Business justification: Group block rate management, commission calculation, and loyalty eligibility determination require the authoritative reservation_rate_plan record. event_group_block.rate_plan_code is a denormalized te']
  room_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.room_type. Business justification: Event group blocks allocate specific room type inventory (king suite, double queen, etc.) for attendee accommodations. Revenue managers track contracted vs. picked-up rooms by type; yield management a']
  actual_total_revenue decimal(12,2) [note: 'Actual total room revenue generated from picked-up rooms in this group block. Calculated as picked_up_room_count multiplied by group_rate_amount and number of nights. Used for post-event revenue analysis.']
  attrition_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Monetary penalty or fee amount charged if the group fails to meet the contracted room pickup threshold. Calculated based on attrition percentage and contracted value.']
  attrition_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Contractual attrition threshold percentage. If pickup falls below this percentage of contracted rooms, the group may incur attrition fees or penalties.']
  available_room_count varchar [note: 'Number of rooms still available for pickup within the group block. Calculated as contracted minus picked up.']
  block_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code assigned to the group room block for reservation booking and tracking. Used by guests and agents to access group rates.']
  block_end_date date [note: 'Last night of the group room block allocation period. Represents the latest departure date for group attendees.']
  block_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the group room block, typically including the event or organization name.']
  block_notes varchar [note: 'Internal operational notes and comments related to the group room block management. Used for communication between sales, reservations, and operations teams.']
  block_release_policy varchar [note: 'Policy governing how unused rooms are released back to general inventory. Automatic releases at cutoff date, manual requires explicit action, rolling releases incrementally, none retains all rooms until event end.']
  block_start_date date [note: 'First night of the group room block allocation period. Represents the earliest arrival date for group attendees.']
  block_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the group room block. Tentative indicates preliminary hold, definite indicates contracted commitment, actual indicates active pickup phase, cancelled indicates terminated block, waitlist indicates overflow demand, released indicates inventory returned to general availability.']
  commission_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage commission paid to the booking agent, planner, or intermediary for this group block. Applied to total room revenue generated.']
  complimentary_room_count varchar [note: 'Number of complimentary (free) rooms provided to the group as part of the contract negotiation. Typically based on a ratio of paid rooms (e.g., 1 comp per 50 paid).']
  contracted_room_count varchar [note: 'Total number of rooms contracted in the group block for this room type. Represents the commitment between the property and the group organizer.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the system user who created the group room block record. Audit trail for accountability.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the group room block record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record creation.']
  cutoff_date date [note: 'Date by which the group must pick up their contracted room block or release unused rooms back to general inventory. Critical for revenue management and inventory control.']
  deposit_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Monetary deposit amount required to secure the group room block. May be a flat fee or percentage of total contracted value.']
  deposit_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the group deposit must be received to maintain the block reservation.']
  deposit_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a deposit is required to secure the group room block.']
  estimated_total_revenue decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated total room revenue for this group block if fully picked up. Calculated as total_room_nights multiplied by group_rate_amount. Used for group pace reporting and revenue forecasting.']
  group_rate_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Negotiated nightly room rate for this group block, excluding taxes and fees. Represents the group ADR (Average Daily Rate) for revenue analysis.']
  market_segment_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the market segment classification for this group block (e.g., CORPORATE, ASSOCIATION, SMERF, GOVERNMENT). Used for revenue segmentation and analysis.']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the system user who last modified the group room block record. Audit trail for accountability.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the group room block record was last modified. Audit trail for change tracking.']
  owner_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the sales manager, event coordinator, or staff member responsible for managing this group block.']
  picked_up_room_count varchar [note: 'Current number of rooms actually reserved by group attendees against this block. Used to calculate pickup-to-block ratio and attrition risk.']
  pickup_to_block_ratio decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of contracted rooms that have been picked up by group attendees. Key performance metric for group pace analysis and attrition risk assessment. Calculated as (picked_up_room_count / contracted_room_count) * 100.']
  rate_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the group rate amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  rebate_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage rebate or kickback paid to the group organizer based on total room revenue generated. Common in association and corporate group contracts.']
  shoulder_dates_allowed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether group attendees are permitted to book rooms at the group rate on dates outside the official block period (shoulder nights).']
  special_instructions varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing special requests, requirements, or notes related to the group room block (e.g., VIP treatment, accessibility needs, billing instructions).']
  synced_to_crs_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the group room block was last synchronized to the Central Reservation System (Sabre SynXis CRS) for distribution and rate availability.']
  total_room_nights varchar [note: 'Total number of room nights represented by this group block. Calculated as contracted_room_count multiplied by the number of nights in the block period. Key metric for group revenue contribution analysis.']

  Note: 'Group room block associated with an event booking, representing the contracted allocation of guest rooms for event attendees. Captures the room type mix, contracted room count by night, group rate (group ADR), pickup-to-block ratio, cutoff date, attrition percentage, block release policy, and current pickup count. Integrates with Oracle OPERA PMS for room inventory allocation and Sabre SynXis CRS for group rate distribution. Critical for group pace reporting and group ADR contribution analysis.'
}

Table event.function_space {
  function_space_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the function space. Primary key.']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fixed_asset. Business justification: Function spaces are physical assets tracked in the fixed asset register for depreciation, CapEx approval, and PIP (Property Improvement Plan) reporting. A hospitality asset manager expects function sp']
  meeting_space_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.meeting_space. Business justification: Event sales systems (Delphi/CI) map function_space records to physical meeting_space assets in the property system (Opera/PMS) for real-time availability checks, capacity validation, and cross-system ']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property where this function space is located.']
  seasonal_calendar_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.seasonal_calendar. Business justification: Function space rental rates and availability are governed by the property\'s seasonal calendar (blackout dates, demand tiers, minimum rental periods). Revenue managers use this link to apply season-spe']
  accessibility_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the function space meets ADA (Americans with Disabilities Act) accessibility requirements including wheelchair access, ramps, and accessible restrooms.']
  av_infrastructure varchar [note: 'Description of built-in AV equipment and infrastructure including screens, projectors, sound systems, lighting rigs, and control systems.']
  capacity_banquet varchar [note: 'Maximum guest capacity when the space is configured for banquet-style seating (round tables with chairs).']
  capacity_cabaret varchar [note: 'Maximum guest capacity when the space is configured in cabaret-style seating (round tables with chairs on three sides only).']
  capacity_classroom varchar [note: 'Maximum guest capacity when the space is configured in classroom-style seating (rows of tables and chairs).']
  capacity_hollow_square varchar [note: 'Maximum guest capacity when the space is configured in hollow square seating (tables arranged in square formation).']
  capacity_reception varchar [note: 'Maximum guest capacity when the space is configured for reception-style standing events (cocktail, networking).']
  capacity_theater varchar [note: 'Maximum guest capacity when the space is configured in theater-style seating (rows of chairs facing front).']
  capacity_u_shape varchar [note: 'Maximum guest capacity when the space is configured in U-shape seating (tables arranged in U formation).']
  catering_kitchen_access boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the function space has direct access to a catering kitchen or prep area for Food and Beverage (F&B) service.']
  ceiling_height_feet decimal(6,2) [note: 'Height of the ceiling in feet, critical for AV setup, lighting design, and event feasibility assessment.']
  climate_control varchar [note: 'Type of climate control system available in the function space for temperature and air quality management.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this function space record was first created in the system.']
  divisible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the function space can be divided into smaller sub-spaces using partitions or airwalls.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when this function space record became active and available for booking in the event management system.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when this function space record expires or is no longer available for booking, nullable for spaces with indefinite availability.']
  floor_level varchar [note: 'Physical floor or level where the function space is located within the property (e.g., Ground Floor, Mezzanine, 2nd Floor, Rooftop).']
  internet_connectivity varchar [note: 'Type of internet connectivity available in the function space for event attendees and organizers.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this function space record was last updated or modified in the system.']
  natural_light_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the function space has natural daylight through windows, skylights, or outdoor exposure.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional operational notes, special features, restrictions, or important information about the function space for event planners and sales teams.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational availability status of the function space for booking and event allocation.']
  outdoor_space boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the function space is an outdoor venue or has outdoor components (terrace, garden, rooftop).']
  partition_configuration varchar [note: 'Description of how the space can be divided, including the number of sub-spaces and their names (e.g., Ballroom A, B, C).']
  rental_rate_full_day decimal(12,2) [note: 'Standard rental rate for booking the function space for a full day (typically 8-12 hours), in property base currency.']
  rental_rate_half_day decimal(12,2) [note: 'Standard rental rate for booking the function space for a half day (typically 4-6 hours), in property base currency.']
  rental_rate_hourly decimal(12,2) [note: 'Standard rental rate for booking the function space on an hourly basis, in property base currency.']
  setup_time_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Standard time required in hours for event setup including furniture arrangement, AV installation, and decoration before the event start.']
  space_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the function space within the property, used in PMS and event management systems.']
  space_name varchar [note: 'Full business name of the function space (e.g., Grand Ballroom, Executive Boardroom, Terrace Garden).']
  space_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the function space by its primary use and configuration characteristics.']
  square_footage decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total floor area of the function space measured in square feet, used for capacity planning and space utilization reporting.']
  teardown_time_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Standard time required in hours for event teardown including furniture removal, AV breakdown, and space restoration after the event end.']

  Note: 'Master record for each bookable function space (ballroom, boardroom, breakout room, exhibit hall, pre-function area, outdoor terrace, rooftop venue) at a property. Captures the space name, space type, maximum capacity by setup style (theater, classroom, banquet, reception, U-shape, hollow square, cabaret), square footage, ceiling height, natural light availability, AV infrastructure (built-in screens, sound systems, lighting rigs), divisibility into sub-spaces with partition configurations, rental rate by daypart, and operational status. Serves as the inventory catalog for event space allocation and function space utilization reporting.'
}

Business justification: Function space usage drives departmental cost allocation for utilities, housekeeping, and setup labor. Hotels allocate space-related costs to booking departments for internal charge-backs and space ut']

Business justification: Event space allocations frequently use property facilities (pools, fitness centers, outdoor terraces) for breakout sessions, receptions, team-building activities.   allocation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the space allocation. Tentative indicates provisional hold, definite indicates confirmed booking, released indicates space returned to inventory, cancelled indicates booking cancelled, waitlisted indicates pending availability.']
  booking_segment varchar [note: 'Market segment classification for the event booking. Used for revenue analysis and market mix reporting aligned with MICE segmentation.']
  complimentary_reason varchar [note: 'Business justification for providing complimentary space rental (e.g., minimum F&B spend met, VIP client, promotional package, contract terms). Populated when is_complimentary is true.']
  confirmed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the space allocation status changed from tentative to definite. Represents the point at which the booking became contractually binding.']
  end_time timestamp [note: 'The scheduled end date and time when the event concludes in the allocated space. Represents the actual event end, not teardown end.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the event purpose (e.g., meeting, conference, wedding, banquet, training, exhibition, social). Used for space utilization analysis and market segmentation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: meeting|conference|wedding|banquet|training|exhibition|social|corporate|association|government — promote to reference product]']
  expected_attendance varchar [note: 'The anticipated number of attendees for the event as communicated by the client during booking. Used for capacity planning and setup configuration.']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'External system identifier or client reference number for cross-system reconciliation and client communication. May contain third-party booking platform IDs or client internal event codes.']
  guaranteed_attendance varchar [note: 'The contractually guaranteed minimum number of attendees for billing purposes. Typically finalized closer to the event date per contract terms.']
  is_complimentary boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the space rental is provided at no charge as part of a promotional offer, loyalty benefit, or contractual agreement. True if complimentary, false if charged.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to any field in this space allocation record. Used for change tracking and audit trail.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to this space allocation for conflict resolution and resource allocation decisions. High priority typically assigned to definite bookings with high revenue or strategic importance.']
  released_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the space allocation was released back to inventory. Populated when status changes to released or cancelled.']
  rental_charge_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The monetary charge for renting the function space for the specified time period. May be zero if complimentary or included in package pricing.']
  rental_charge_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the rental charge amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  setup_start_time timestamp [note: 'The date and time when setup activities begin for the event. Used to calculate total space occupancy time including pre-event preparation.']
  setup_style varchar [note: 'The physical arrangement configuration of the function space (e.g., theater, classroom, banquet rounds, U-shape, hollow square, boardroom, reception). Determines capacity and operational requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: theater|classroom|banquet_rounds|u_shape|hollow_square|boardroom|reception|cocktail|conference|chevron|herringbone — promote to reference product]']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operational system from which this space allocation record originated (e.g., Delphi, OPERA, proprietary event management system). Used for data lineage and integration troubleshooting.']
  space_block_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the space allocation is exclusive to this event or shared with other concurrent activities. Exclusive means sole use, shared means multiple events may use portions, concurrent means back-to-back scheduling, overflow means secondary space for capacity overflow.']
  special_requirements varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing specific client requests or operational notes for the space allocation (e.g., AV equipment, accessibility needs, temperature preferences, security requirements).']
  start_time timestamp [note: 'The scheduled start date and time when the event begins in the allocated space. Represents the actual event start, not setup start.']
  teardown_end_time timestamp [note: 'The date and time when teardown activities are completed and the space is released. Used to calculate total space occupancy time including post-event breakdown.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a specific function space being allocated to an event booking for a defined date and time period. Captures the setup style, expected attendance, actual attendance, setup start time, teardown end time, rental charge, complimentary status, and allocation status (tentative, definite, released). Enables function space utilization tracking, double-booking prevention, and space revenue reporting. Sourced from Delphi by Amadeus space diary.'
}

  service_charge_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage service charge applied to food and beverage charges for this function. Typically ranges from 18% to 24%.']
  setup_style varchar [note: 'The room configuration and seating arrangement style for this function. Determines furniture requirements and capacity. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: theater|classroom|banquet|hollow_square|u_shape|boardroom|reception|cocktail|other — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  setup_time timestamp [note: 'The time when setup crew should begin preparing the function space. Critical for housekeeping and banquet operations scheduling.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The name of the source system from which this BEO record originated (e.g., \'Delphi by Amadeus\', \'Opera PMS\'). Used for data lineage and integration tracking.']
  special_instructions varchar [note: 'Additional operational notes, client preferences, VIP requirements, security needs, or any other special considerations for executing this function.']
  start_time timestamp [note: 'The scheduled start date and time for this function, including timezone context. Used for setup scheduling and guest communication.']
  tax_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The applicable tax rate percentage for this function based on jurisdiction and tax regulations.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version number of this BEO document. Increments with each revision to track changes and ensure operations teams work from the latest version.']

  Note: 'Banquet Event Order (BEO) — the operational master document for executing a specific event function. Captures the function name, event date, start and end times, guaranteed attendance count, setup style, menu selections, beverage package, AV requirements, décor notes, special instructions, billing instructions, and BEO status (draft, issued, confirmed, completed). The BEO is the primary communication document between event sales, catering, kitchen, and operations teams. Sourced from Delphi by Amadeus BEO module.'
}

The catering_menu is the']

Business justification: USALI F&B reporting requires BEO line items to post to the correct F&B revenue center for P&L attribution and departmental accounting. beo_item has gl_account_code and cost_center as plain text but no']
  treatment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to spa.treatment. Business justification: Wellness retreat and spa day event BEOs line-item individual spa treatments for pricing, scheduling, and execution. Event operations staff need beo_item→spa.treatment to retrieve treatment duration, p']

  allergen_information varchar [note: 'Free-text field documenting known allergens present in this item. Critical for food safety compliance and guest health protection.']
  billing_status varchar [note: 'Current billing status of this line item. Tracks progression from event execution through invoicing, payment, and dispute resolution.']
  cost_center varchar [note: 'Accounting cost center responsible for fulfilling this item. Used for departmental P&L allocation and operational cost tracking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this BEO line item was first created in the system. Audit trail for event planning timeline.']

  dietary_restriction_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this item accommodates special dietary requirements. Triggers kitchen alerts and special handling procedures.']
  dietary_restriction_type varchar [note: 'Specific dietary accommodation provided by this item. Critical for food safety, allergen management, and guest satisfaction. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: vegetarian|vegan|gluten_free|dairy_free|nut_free|kosher|halal|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  item_status varchar [note: 'Current status of this line item within the BEO lifecycle. Tracks changes, cancellations, and substitutions during event planning and execution.']
  item_type varchar [note: 'Specific type of line item. Distinguishes between menu selections, bundled packages, equipment rentals, labor services, additional charges, and credit adjustments.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential line number within the BEO document. Determines the order of items on the BEO and production sheets.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who last modified this line item. Audit trail for change management.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to this line item. Tracks BEO revisions and change history.']
  overage_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage above guaranteed quantity that the kitchen prepares as buffer. Standard practice to ensure sufficient food availability. Typically 3-5% for plated meals, higher for buffets.']
  package_code varchar [note: 'Reference to a bundled catering package (e.g., breakfast buffet, coffee break, AV package). Links to package master for component breakdown and pricing.']
  quantity decimal(10,2) [note: 'Number of units ordered for this line item. May represent guest count, number of pieces, hours of service, or equipment units depending on the item type and unit of measure.']
  revenue_category varchar [note: 'Revenue classification for financial reporting and USALI compliance. Determines which revenue account this item posts to in the general ledger.']
  service_charge_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Calculated service charge amount for this line item. Derived from extended amount and service charge rate.']
  service_charge_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a service charge or gratuity applies to this line item. Common for food, beverage, and labor items.']
  service_charge_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Service charge percentage applied to this line item. Stored as decimal (e.g., 0.22 for 22%). May be mandatory or discretionary depending on property policy.']
  service_time timestamp [note: 'Scheduled time when this item should be served or delivered to the event. Drives kitchen timing, service staff deployment, and guest experience.']
  setup_time timestamp [note: 'Scheduled time when this item should be set up or prepared. Critical for kitchen production scheduling, AV setup coordination, and labor planning.']
  special_instructions varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional instructions, presentation requirements, or service notes. Communicated to kitchen, service staff, and AV technicians.']
  tax_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Calculated tax amount for this line item. Derived from extended amount and tax rate.']
  tax_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this line item is subject to sales tax or VAT. Determines tax calculation during billing.']
  tax_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Tax rate percentage applied to this line item. Stored as decimal (e.g., 0.0825 for 8.25%). May vary by jurisdiction, item category, or tax exemption status.']
  total_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Grand total for this line item including extended amount, taxes, and service charges. Used for event billing and revenue recognition.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit in which the quantity is measured. Critical for accurate kitchen production planning, inventory consumption, and billing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: person|piece|hour|day|each|dozen|gallon|bottle|case|pound — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  unit_price decimal(12,2) [note: 'Price per unit of measure. Base price before taxes, service charges, or gratuities. Used to calculate extended amount.']
  vendor_source varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the item is provided by the property, an external vendor, or supplied by the client. Affects procurement, liability, and cost allocation.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the catering sales manager or event coordinator who created this line item. Audit trail for accountability.']

  Note: 'Line-item detail within a Banquet Event Order capturing each individual service, menu selection, equipment item, or charge. Captures item category (food, beverage, AV, décor, labor, rental, miscellaneous), item description, menu/package reference, quantity, unit of measure, unit price, extended amount, tax applicability, service charge rate, dietary flags, and vendor source. Enables granular event cost tracking, catering production planning, kitchen prep sheets, and post-event billing reconciliation. Also serves as the transactional record linking catalog offerings (menus, AV packages) to actual event consumption.'
}

Table event.catering_menu {
  catering_menu_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the catering menu item. Primary key for the catering menu catalog.']
  banquet_menu_package_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fnb.banquet_menu_package. Business justification: Event catering menus are derived from standardized F&B banquet packages. Linking catering_menu to banquet_menu_package enables menu engineering analysis, package compliance reporting, and accurate per']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Catering menus are managed by F&B departments as cost centers for menu engineering and contribution margin analysis. Hotels track menu costs, labor, and pricing by department for profitability optimiz']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the property where this catering menu is available. Links to the property master data.']
  property_outlet_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.property_outlet. Business justification: Catering menus are fulfilled by specific property outlets (restaurants, banquet kitchens, poolside bars). Critical for kitchen capacity planning, outlet revenue attribution, health permit compliance t']
  revenue_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fnb.revenue_center. Business justification: Catering menus must be attributed to an F&B revenue center for USALI event catering revenue reporting and food cost analysis. catering_menu has property_outlet_id but no direct F&B revenue center FK; ']
  seasonal_calendar_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.seasonal_calendar. Business justification: Catering menus have seasonal_availability; the property\'s seasonal_calendar drives which menus are active during which periods. F&B managers use this link to automate menu activation/deactivation and ']
  active_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the catering menu. Only active menus are available for new BEO (Banquet Event Order) bookings. Seasonal menus activate/deactivate based on availability windows.']
  allergen_declarations varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of major food allergens present in the menu (e.g., peanuts, tree nuts, dairy, eggs, soy, wheat, fish, shellfish). Required for guest safety and regulatory compliance.']
  catering_menu_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the catering menu package including courses, signature items, presentation style, and service inclusions. Used in proposals and BEO (Banquet Event Order) documentation.']
  contains_alcohol boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the menu includes alcoholic beverages or alcohol-infused dishes. Impacts licensing requirements, service protocols, and pricing.']
  contribution_margin_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage contribution margin for the menu calculated as (price - cost) / price. Key metric for menu engineering and profitability optimization. Confidential business data.']
  cost_per_person decimal(10,2) [note: 'Internal food and beverage cost per person for this menu. Used for GOP (Gross Operating Profit) analysis, menu engineering, and contribution margin calculations. Confidential business data.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the catering director or system user who created this menu record. Audit trail for accountability and menu curation governance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this catering menu record was first created in the system. Audit trail for menu catalog lifecycle tracking.']
  cuisine_style varchar [note: 'Culinary theme or regional cuisine classification of the menu (e.g., Italian, Asian Fusion, Mediterranean, Contemporary American). Used for menu curation and guest preference matching.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for menu pricing (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency property operations and international group bookings.']
  dietary_accommodations varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of dietary restrictions and preferences this menu can accommodate (e.g., vegetarian, vegan, gluten-free, kosher, halal, dairy-free). Critical for inclusive event planning and guest satisfaction.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this menu version is no longer available for new bookings. Nullable for open-ended menus. Existing BEO (Banquet Event Order) commitments honor the booked menu version.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this menu version becomes available for booking. Used for seasonal menu transitions and pricing change management.']
  labor_intensity_rating varchar [note: 'Classification of the labor effort required to prepare and serve this menu. Impacts staffing requirements, CPOR (Cost Per Occupied Room) calculations, and operational feasibility for concurrent events.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who last updated this menu record. Audit trail for change management and version control.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this catering menu record. Used for change tracking and data synchronization between Delphi and MICROS POS systems.']
  maximum_capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum number of guests that can be served with this menu given kitchen production capacity and service constraints. Used for function space allocation and operational feasibility checks.']
  menu_category varchar [note: 'Tier classification of the menu based on ingredient quality, presentation complexity, and price point. Used for market segmentation and group sales targeting.']
  menu_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the catering menu used in Delphi and MICROS POS systems for quick lookup and BEO (Banquet Event Order) item selection.']
  menu_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the catering menu package displayed to event planners and clients during MICE (Meetings, Incentives, Conferences, Exhibitions) booking process.']
  menu_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the catering menu by meal period or service style. Determines function space setup requirements and service staffing levels. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: breakfast|lunch|dinner|reception|coffee_break|buffet|plated|cocktail|hors_doeuvres — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  minimum_guarantee varchar [note: 'Minimum number of guests required to book this catering menu. Used for kitchen production planning and revenue yield management. Client is charged for minimum even if actual attendance is lower.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for internal operational notes, special preparation instructions, or catering director comments. Not visible to clients but used by banquet operations and kitchen staff.']
  preparation_lead_time_hours varchar [note: 'Minimum advance notice required in hours for kitchen to prepare this menu. Critical for BEO (Banquet Event Order) cutoff times and last-minute booking feasibility.']
  presentation_image_url varchar [note: 'URL to professional photograph of the menu presentation. Used in digital proposals, event planning portals, and marketing materials to enhance visual appeal and booking conversion.']
  price_per_person decimal(10,2) [note: 'Standard per-person pricing for the catering menu package. Base rate before service charges, gratuities, and taxes. Used for group pricing calculations and revenue forecasting.']
  requires_specialty_equipment boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this menu requires specialty kitchen or service equipment beyond standard banquet capabilities (e.g., carving stations, flambé equipment, liquid nitrogen). Impacts feasibility and rental costs.']
  seasonal_availability varchar [note: 'Seasonal availability window for the menu based on ingredient sourcing, culinary calendar, and demand patterns. Menus may be versioned seasonally by the catering director.']
  service_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Typical duration in minutes for service of this menu from start to completion. Used for event timeline planning and function space utilization optimization.']
  service_style varchar [note: 'Method of food service delivery for the catering menu. Impacts labor costs, function space layout, and guest experience.']
  setup_time_minutes varchar [note: 'Estimated time in minutes required to set up the function space for this menu service style. Used for event scheduling, function space turnover planning, and labor allocation.']
  sustainability_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the menu meets sustainability certification standards (e.g., organic ingredients, local sourcing, sustainable seafood). Increasingly important for corporate MICE (Meetings, Incentives, Conferences, Exhibitions) clients with ESG requirements.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the menu catalog (e.g., 2024-Q2, 2024-DEC). Menus are versioned seasonally by the catering director to reflect ingredient availability, pricing updates, and culinary trends.']

  Note: 'Master catalog of catering menus and packages available for MICE event bookings at a property. Captures the menu name, menu type (breakfast, lunch, dinner, reception, coffee break, buffet, plated), cuisine style, minimum guarantee, price per person, seasonal availability, dietary accommodation options, allergen declarations, and active status. Referenced by BEO items when food and beverage selections are made for a specific event function. Managed within Delphi by Amadeus and Oracle Hospitality MICROS POS. Distinct from the foodandbeverage domain\'s operational outlet menu management — this catalog is curated specifically for MICE event packages, group pricing tiers, and banquet-style service, and is versioned seasonally by the catering director.'
}

Table event.event_contract {
  event_contract_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the event contract. Primary key.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Event contracts are financial commitments requiring cost center attribution for departmental P&L reporting and budget control. A hospitality finance controller expects contracts to carry a cost_center']
  event_booking_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent event booking that this contract governs.']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Event contracts span fiscal periods; deposit schedules, attrition clauses, and final payment recognition are tracked by fiscal period. MICE segment contracted revenue is reported by fiscal period for ']
  package_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to spa.package. Business justification: Corporate retreat and incentive event contracts explicitly include contracted spa packages with attrition and cancellation obligations. Legal and contracting teams need event_contract→spa.package to e']
  amendment_history varchar [note: 'Chronological record of all contract amendments, addendums, and modifications.']
  attrition_clause varchar [note: 'Terms governing penalties if the client fails to meet minimum room block or revenue commitments.']
  attrition_threshold_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum percentage of contracted room block or revenue that must be achieved to avoid attrition penalties.']
  av_equipment_revenue decimal(15,2) [note: 'Contracted revenue from audio-visual equipment and technical services.']
  cancellation_penalty_schedule varchar [note: 'Tiered schedule of financial penalties based on cancellation timing relative to event date.']
  cancellation_policy varchar [note: 'Detailed terms governing cancellation penalties and refund conditions.']
  client_signatory_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the authorized signatory representing the client organization.']
  client_signatory_title varchar [note: 'Job title or role of the client signatory.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique contract identifier used for legal and business reference.']
  contract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the contract.']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the contract based on scope and commitment level.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the contract record was first created in the system.']
  credit_approval_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the client has been approved for credit terms rather than advance payment.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in the contract.']
  deposit_schedule varchar [note: 'Structured schedule of deposit payment milestones and due dates.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the contract terms expire or are fulfilled.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the contract terms become legally binding and enforceable.']
  execution_date date [note: 'Date when the contract was legally executed and signed by all parties.']
  fb_revenue decimal(15,2) [note: 'Contracted revenue from food and beverage services including catering and banquets.']
  final_payment_due_date date [note: 'Date by which all remaining contracted amounts must be paid in full.']
  force_majeure_clause varchar [note: 'Legal provisions addressing contract obligations in the event of unforeseeable circumstances beyond parties control.']
  initial_deposit_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Amount of the first deposit required to secure the event booking.']
  initial_deposit_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the initial deposit must be received.']
  last_amendment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent contract amendment or modification.']
  legal_review_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract has undergone formal legal review and approval.']
  legal_reviewer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the legal counsel or attorney who reviewed the contract.']
  master_account_billing_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether all charges should be consolidated to a single master account for billing.']
  master_account_number varchar [note: 'Account number designated for consolidated billing of all event-related charges.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the contract record was last modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form notes capturing special terms, conditions, or considerations specific to this contract.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Detailed terms governing payment methods, schedules, and conditions.']
  property_signatory_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the authorized signatory representing the property or hotel.']
  property_signatory_title varchar [note: 'Job title or role of the property signatory.']
  room_revenue decimal(15,2) [note: 'Contracted revenue from guest room accommodations.']
  space_rental_revenue decimal(15,2) [note: 'Contracted revenue from meeting and event space rental fees.']
  total_contracted_revenue decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total revenue amount committed across all categories in the contract.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version number of the contract to track amendments and revisions.']

  Note: 'Legally executed contract governing the terms and conditions of a confirmed event booking. Captures the contract execution date, signatory details, total contracted revenue by category (rooms, F&B, space rental, AV, other), deposit schedule and amounts, cancellation penalty schedule, attrition clause terms, force majeure provisions, master account billing authorization, and contract amendment history. Distinct from the event_booking operational record — this is the legal instrument. Sourced from Delphi by Amadeus contract management.'
}

This enables unified b']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the corporate or individual client account being billed for the event. Links to the party responsible for payment.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Event invoices must be attributed to a cost center for departmental P&L and USALI reporting. Finance teams allocate event invoice revenue to the correct department (Catering, Banquets, AV). A hospital']
  event_booking_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent event booking for which this invoice was generated. Links to the event booking that contains the BEO (Banquet Event Order) and contract details.']
  event_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.event_contract. Business justification: A post-event invoice is issued against the terms of the executed event contract (attrition clauses, cancellation penalties, deposit schedules, payment terms). Linking invoice.event_contract_id -> even']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Standard hospitality accounti']

Business justification: Event invoices include redeemed promotional offers and campaign discounts that must be reconciled for accurate revenue recognition and offer performance tracking. Essential for discount validation, re']

Finance and operations teams require this link for financial reconciliation of recovery costs, GL posting of compe']
  tax_posting_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.tax_posting. Business justification: Event invoices carry tax amounts (VAT, service charges, occupancy tax) that must be reconciled against tax_posting records for regulatory filing. A hospitality finance controller expects each invoice\'']
  amount_paid decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total amount that has been paid against this invoice to date. Used to track partial payments and outstanding balance.']
  ar_posting_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier for the corresponding accounts receivable ledger entry in the finance system (SAP S/4HANA AR). Used for cross-domain reconciliation.']
  av_equipment_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Revenue from audio-visual equipment rental and technical services provided for the event.']

  billing_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the billing address (suite, floor, building, etc.).']

  billing_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the billing contact for payment-related inquiries.']
  billing_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code of the billing address.']
  billing_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the period covered by this invoice. Typically the last day of the event or the end of the service period.']
  billing_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the period covered by this invoice. Typically the first day of the event or the start of the service period.']
  billing_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the billing address.']
  billing_state_province varchar [note: 'State or province of the billing address.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the invoice record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the invoice is denominated.']
  dispute_date date [note: 'Date when the invoice was flagged as disputed by the client or internally.']
  dispute_reason varchar [note: 'Description of the reason for dispute if the invoice status is disputed. Captures client objections or billing discrepancies.']
  fb_revenue_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total revenue from food and beverage services provided during the event. Includes catering, banquet meals, and beverage packages.']
  invoice_date date [note: 'Date the invoice was officially issued to the event client. This is the principal business event timestamp for the invoice.']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique invoice number assigned to this event invoice. Used for client communication, payment tracking, and reconciliation.']
  invoice_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the event invoice. Tracks progression from draft through payment and any exceptions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|issued|sent|paid|partially_paid|overdue|disputed|cancelled|void — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the invoice record was last updated or modified.']
  miscellaneous_charges_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Revenue from other event-related charges not categorized elsewhere, such as decor, entertainment, special services, or incidentals.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or special instructions related to the invoice, such as billing adjustments, client requests, or internal comments.']
  outstanding_balance decimal(15,2) [note: 'Remaining unpaid balance on the invoice. Calculated as total_amount_due minus amount_paid.']
  payment_due_date date [note: 'Date by which payment is expected from the event client. Calculated based on credit terms agreed in the event contract.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the client is expected to or has made payment for this invoice.']
  payment_received_date date [note: 'Date when full or final payment was received for this invoice. Null if invoice remains unpaid or partially paid.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Description of the payment terms agreed with the client, such as net 30, net 60, due on receipt, or custom terms from the event contract.']
  resolution_date date [note: 'Date when a disputed invoice was resolved and moved back to an active billing status.']
  room_revenue_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total revenue from group room block pickups associated with this event. Represents accommodation charges for event attendees.']
  service_charge_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Service charge or gratuity amount applied to the invoice, typically a percentage of food and beverage revenue.']
  space_rental_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Revenue from rental of meeting rooms, ballrooms, and other function spaces used for the event.']
  subtotal_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Sum of all revenue line items before taxes and service charges are applied.']
  tax_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total tax amount applied to the invoice, including sales tax, VAT, GST, or other applicable taxes based on jurisdiction.']
  total_amount_due decimal(15,2) [note: 'Final total amount due on the invoice after all charges, taxes, and service charges are applied. This is the amount the client is expected to pay.']

  Note: 'Post-event invoice issued to the event client for all charges incurred during the event. Captures the invoice date, invoice number, billing period, itemized charges by category (room revenue, F&B revenue, space rental, AV, miscellaneous), applicable taxes and service charges, total amount due, payment due date, and invoice status (draft, issued, paid, disputed). Reconciles against the event_account credit terms and BEO actuals. This is the event-domain billing document — actual AR ledger posting, payment receipt, and collections are owned by the finance domain. Integrates with SAP S/4HANA AR for financial posting via cross-domain FK.'
}

Table event.event_revenue {
  event_revenue_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the event revenue record. Primary key for this transactional revenue fact.']
  banquet_event_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fnb.banquet_event_order. Business justification: F&B revenue reconciliation and audit require tracing event revenue postings back to the originating banquet event order. event_revenue has fnb_revenue_center_id but no direct link to banquet_event_ord']
  beo_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.beo. Business justification: event_revenue currently has beo_reference_number (STRING) as a denormalized reference. Revenue is often recognized based on BEO execution (actual services delivered per the BEO). This should be normal']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Event revenue must be attributed to originating marketing campaigns for ROI calculation and marketing spend justification. Essential for campaign performance reporting, budget allocation decisions, an']
  campaign_offer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign_offer. Business justification: Offer-level ROI reporting requires linking event revenue to the specific campaign offer that drove the booking (e.g., 20% group discount). event_revenue already links to campaign but not campaign_offe']
  event_booking_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent event booking for which this revenue was recognized. Links to the event booking master record.']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Event revenue recognition, RevPAR/TRevPAR contribution tracking, and budget variance reporting all require fiscal period assignment. A hospitality controller expects event revenue records to carry fis']
  fnb_revenue_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fnb.revenue_center. Business justification: Event F&B revenue must be allocated to proper F&B revenue centers for USALI-compliant financial reporting, departmental P&L analysis, cost center tracking, and performance management. Critical for hot']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.event_invoice. Business justification: event_revenue currently has invoice_number (STRING) as a denormalized reference. Revenue recognition is tied to invoicing in hospitality accounting. This should be normalized to a proper FK to event_i']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Event revenue recognition posts to general ledger via journal entries. Auditors trace event revenue to GL for SOX compliance, revenue recognition standard (ASC 606) validation, and financial statement']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property where the event took place and revenue was generated.']
  service_recovery_action_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to experience.service_recovery_action. Business justification: Service recovery actions (comps, rate adjustments, revenue waivers) directly impact event revenue records. Linking enables financial impact tracking of recovery actions on event P&L, GL reconciliation']
  tax_posting_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.tax_posting. Business justification: Event revenue generates tax liabilities (VAT, occupancy tax, service charges) that are posted to tax_posting. Finance controllers reconcile event tax amounts against tax_posting records for regulatory']
  actual_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The actual revenue amount recognized for this category on this date, in the property\'s base currency.']
  adjustment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The amount of any revenue adjustment applied (positive for additions, negative for reductions).']
  adjustment_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for any revenue adjustment or correction applied to this line (e.g., discount, refund, billing correction, service recovery).']
  attendee_count varchar [note: 'Number of attendees at the event, used to calculate per-person revenue metrics and analyze event profitability.']
  budgeted_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The budgeted or forecasted revenue amount for this category on this date, used for variance analysis.']
  commission_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Commission amount payable to third-party agents or intermediaries for this revenue (e.g., meeting planner commission, OTA commission).']
  commission_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Commission rate percentage applied to this revenue line, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 10.00 for 10%).']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'The cost center or department code responsible for this revenue (e.g., catering department, banquet operations).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this revenue record was first created in the system. Part of the audit trail.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the revenue amounts (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  event_type varchar [note: 'The type of MICE event that generated this revenue (meeting, conference, wedding, banquet, exhibition, incentive trip, etc.).']
  function_space_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the function space or meeting room where this event took place and revenue was generated.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'The general ledger account code to which this revenue was posted in the financial system.']
  group_adr decimal(18,2) [note: 'Average Daily Rate for group room nights associated with this event. Calculated as group room revenue divided by group room nights.']
  group_room_nights varchar [note: 'Number of group room nights associated with this event revenue, used to calculate group ADR (Average Daily Rate) contribution.']
  is_voided boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this revenue line has been voided or reversed. True if voided, False if active.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this revenue record was last modified. Part of the audit trail for tracking changes.']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or username of the person who last modified this revenue record. Part of the audit trail.']
  net_revenue_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net revenue after deducting taxes and service charges from the actual amount. This is the revenue recognized for GOP (Gross Operating Profit) calculation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments related to this revenue line, used for clarification, special instructions, or audit trail documentation.']
  payment_received_date date [note: 'The date payment was received for this revenue line. Null if payment is still pending.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current payment status of this revenue line. Tracks whether the revenue has been collected.']
  per_attendee decimal(18,2) [note: 'Average revenue generated per event attendee. Calculated as total event revenue divided by attendee count.']
  posting_date date [note: 'The date this revenue was posted to the general ledger in the financial system.']
  recognition_method varchar [note: 'The accounting method used to recognize this revenue (accrual basis, cash basis, or deferred revenue).']
  revenue_category varchar [note: 'The USALI-aligned revenue category for this line item. Categorizes event revenue into standard hospitality revenue streams.']
  revenue_date date [note: 'The business date on which this revenue was recognized per USALI revenue recognition standards. This is the principal business event timestamp for this transaction.']
  revenue_source varchar [note: 'The channel or source through which this event revenue was generated (direct booking, OTA, GDS, corporate account, group sales).']
  revpar_contribution decimal(18,2) [note: 'The contribution of this event\'s group room revenue to the property\'s overall RevPAR (Revenue Per Available Room) metric.']
  service_charge_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Service charge or gratuity amount included in this revenue line, typically for Food and Beverage (F&B) or banquet services.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this revenue data originated (Oracle OPERA PMS, SAP S/4HANA, Delphi, MICROS POS).']
  subcategory varchar [note: 'Detailed subcategory within the revenue category for granular revenue analysis (e.g., breakfast, lunch, dinner under food; beer, wine, spirits under beverage).']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tax amount collected on this revenue line (sales tax, VAT, service charges, etc.).']
  trevpar_contribution decimal(18,2) [note: 'The contribution of this event\'s total revenue (rooms plus F&B plus other) to the property\'s TRevPAR (Total Revenue Per Available Room) metric.']
  variance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The variance between actual and budgeted revenue (actual minus budgeted). Positive indicates revenue overperformance.']
  void_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided for voiding this revenue line (e.g., billing error, event cancellation, duplicate entry).']
  void_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this revenue line was voided. Null if the line has not been voided.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of actual revenue recognized from an event booking, broken down by revenue category per USALI standards (rooms, food, beverage, space rental, AV, other). Captures the revenue date, revenue category, actual amount, budgeted amount, variance, RevPAR contribution from group rooms, and posting reference to the general ledger. This is the event-domain revenue fact — the authoritative GL journal entries and period-close revenue recognition are owned by the finance domain. Enables event revenue performance tracking, group ADR contribution analysis, and TRevPAR impact assessment. Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS and SAP S/4HANA GL.'
}

  attendee_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the event attendee record. Primary key.']
  amenity_fulfillment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to experience.amenity_fulfillment. Business justification: VIP event attendees receive amenity fulfillments (welcome gifts, room drops, F&B setups). Linking enables tracking of what was delivered to which attendee, supporting VIP program reporting, cost attri']
  experience_special_request_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to experience.experience_special_request. Business justification: Event attendees submit special requests (dietary, accessibility, VIP amenities) that are fulfilled through experience_special_request. Linking enables fulfillment tracking, personalization delivery, a']
  guest_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the guest profile in the Property Management System (PMS). Links attendee to their master guest record in Oracle OPERA PMS for loyalty tracking and preference management.']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.invoice. Business justification: Individual attendees may have per-person registration fees, meal charges, or other billable items that are invoiced separately or rolled into a master invoice. Linking attendee.invoice_id -> event.inv']
  loyalty_member_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.member. Business justification: Individual event attendees are frequently loyalty members earning points for event participation and room nights. Essential for loyalty point accrual, tier qualification tracking, and personalized eve']
  reservation_booking_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the room reservation booking associated with this attendee, if the attendee is part of the group room block. Null for day attendees or virtual participants.']
  service_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to experience.service_case. Business justification: Individual attendees raise service cases during or after events (dietary failures, accessibility issues, room problems). Service recovery teams need attendee-level case linkage for personalized resolu']
  spa_appointment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to spa.appointment. Business justification: Event attendees book individual spa appointments during event stay. Critical for cross-sell revenue tracking, attendee experience management, and spa capacity planning around event dates. Real operati']
  spa_membership_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to spa.membership. Business justification: Event attendees convert to spa members post-event. Key conversion funnel metric for marketing ROI analysis. Tracks which events drive membership acquisition, informing future event-spa package design ']
  survey_response_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.survey_response. Business justification: Post-event satisfaction surveys are sent to attendees to measure event experience, NPS, and identify service recovery needs. Critical for event quality measurement, attendee satisfaction tracking, and']
  accessibility_requirements varchar [note: 'Description of any accessibility accommodations required by the attendee, such as wheelchair access, hearing assistance, visual aids, or service animal. Ensures ADA compliance.']
  attendance_mode varchar [note: 'Mode of participation for the attendee: in-person (physical attendance), virtual (remote online participation), or hybrid (combination of both). Critical for capacity planning and platform provisioning.']
  badge_number varchar [note: 'Unique badge identifier assigned to the attendee for event access control and session tracking. Printed on physical badge or used as digital credential.']
  cancellation_date date [note: 'Date when the attendee registration was cancelled. Null if registration is active. Used for refund policy application and attrition tracking.']
  check_in_status varchar [note: 'Current check-in status of the attendee at the event. Tracks whether attendee has arrived and collected their badge or credentials.']
  check_in_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the attendee checked in at the event registration desk or virtual platform. Null if not yet checked in.']
  confirmation_date date [note: 'Date when the attendee registration was confirmed, typically after payment or approval. Null if not yet confirmed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the attendee record was first created in the database. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  data_privacy_consent_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the attendee has provided explicit consent for data processing and storage in compliance with GDPR and CCPA. True if consent given, False otherwise.']
  dietary_restrictions varchar [note: 'Free-text description of attendee dietary requirements, allergies, or preferences for Food and Beverage (F&B) planning. Examples: vegetarian, vegan, gluten-free, kosher, halal, nut allergy.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email address for attendee communication, event confirmations, and virtual platform access credentials.']
  emergency_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the emergency contact person for the attendee. Used in case of medical or safety incidents during the event.']
  emergency_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the emergency contact person. Used for urgent communication in case of attendee emergency.']
  feedback_comments varchar [note: 'Free-text feedback comments provided by the attendee in post-event survey via Medallia. Used for service recovery and continuous improvement.']
  first_name varchar [note: 'First or given name of the event attendee as provided during registration.']
  job_title varchar [note: 'Professional title or role of the attendee within their organization. Used for badge printing and attendee segmentation.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'Last or family name of the event attendee as provided during registration.']
  marketing_opt_in_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the attendee has consented to receive marketing communications from the property or event organizer. True if opted in, False otherwise.']
  nps_score varchar [note: 'Net Promoter Score provided by the attendee in post-event survey, ranging from 0 (not likely to recommend) to 10 (extremely likely to recommend). Null if not provided.']
  organization_name varchar [note: 'Name of the company, institution, or organization the attendee represents. Used for badge printing and networking purposes.']
  payment_date date [note: 'Date when the registration fee payment was received. Null if payment is pending or waived.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current payment status of the registration fee. Tracks whether attendee has completed payment, is pending, or received a refund or waiver.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the attendee, used for event coordination and emergency contact.']
  registration_date date [note: 'Date when the attendee completed their event registration. Used for early-bird pricing determination and registration pace analysis.']
  registration_fee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total registration fee charged to the attendee in the event currency. May vary based on registration type, early-bird pricing, or group discounts.']
  registration_fee_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the registration fee amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  registration_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the attendee registration. Tracks progression from initial registration through confirmation, cancellation, or no-show.']
  registration_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the attendee registration was submitted. Used for audit trail and registration sequence tracking.']
  registration_type varchar [note: 'Category of attendee registration indicating their role at the event. Determines access levels, pricing tier, and badge type.']
  satisfaction_score decimal(3,1) [note: 'Post-event satisfaction score provided by the attendee via Medallia survey. Typically on a scale of 1-10 or 1-5. Null if survey not completed.']
  session_selections varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or JSON array of session identifiers that the attendee has pre-registered for. Used for capacity planning and personalized agenda management.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system from which this attendee record originated (e.g., Delphi by Amadeus, Salesforce CRM, Oracle OPERA PMS). Used for data lineage and integration troubleshooting.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the attendee record in the source system. Used for reconciliation and bi-directional synchronization with Delphi by Amadeus or other event management platforms.']
  special_requests varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing any additional special requests or notes from the attendee, such as seating preferences, networking interests, or other accommodations.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the attendee record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  virtual_platform_access_granted_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the attendee has been provisioned with access credentials to the virtual event platform. True if access granted, False otherwise.']
  virtual_platform_username varchar [note: 'Username or login identifier for the attendee on the virtual event platform. Used for hybrid and virtual attendance modes. Null for in-person-only attendees.']

  Note: 'Record of an individual registered attendee for a specific event booking, supporting both in-person and virtual/hybrid participation modes. Captures the attendee name, organization, title, registration date, registration type (delegate, speaker, VIP, staff, virtual-remote), attendance mode (in-person, virtual, hybrid), dietary restrictions, accessibility requirements, room reservation linkage, badge number, check-in status, session selections, and virtual platform access credentials where applicable. Enables attendance management, rooming list coordination with the group block, capacity compliance, and post-event satisfaction tracking via Medallia.'
}

Table event.beo_attendance {
  beo_attendance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the beo_attendance association']
  attendee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the registered attendee participating in this BEO function.']
  beo_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the Banquet Event Order (BEO) function the attendee is participating in.']
  attendance_confirmed_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this attendee\'s participation in this specific BEO function has been confirmed. Used to distinguish tentative registrations from confirmed headcounts for catering guarantees.']
  check_in_status varchar [note: 'Per-function check-in status for this attendee at this specific BEO function. Distinct from the booking-level check_in_status on attendee — an attendee may check in to some functions but not others.']
  check_in_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the attendee checked in at this specific BEO function. Used for safety compliance headcounts and post-event attendance reporting.']
  dietary_restrictions varchar [note: 'Documentation of attendee dietary restrictions, allergies, and special meal requirements (e.g., vegetarian, vegan, gluten-free, kosher, halal). Critical for food safety and guest satisfaction. [Moved from beo: The BEO\'s dietary_restrictions field is an aggregate summary of all attendee dietary needs for catering planning. The per-attendee dietary restriction already lives on the attendee product. The BEO-level field is a derived aggregate and should be computed from attendee records rather than stored redundantly on the BEO. If per-function dietary overrides are needed, they belong on the beo_attendance association.]']
  meal_count varchar [note: 'Number of meals to be prepared for this attendee at this BEO function. Feeds into the catering guaranteed count for food production planning. Defaults to 1 but may be 0 (dietary exclusion) or higher for hosted guests.']
  seat_assignment varchar [note: 'Table or seat identifier assigned to this attendee for this BEO function (e.g., Table 12, Seat B). Belongs to the attendee-function intersection, not to the attendee or BEO alone.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the Function Attendance event between an attendee and a specific Banquet Event Order (BEO) function. It captures the operational record of an individual attendee\'s participation in a specific event function, including per-function check-in status, meal selection, and seat assignment. Each record links one attendee to one BEO function with attributes that exist only in the context of this attendee-function participation — enabling accurate catering guarantee management, safety compliance headcounts, and post-event billing reconciliation.. Existence Justification: In MICE (Meetings, Incentives, Conferences, Exhibitions) hotel operations, an attendee participates in multiple BEO functions (e.g., welcome reception, breakout session, gala dinner) and each BEO function has multiple attendees. This is a genuine operational M:N: catering teams must know per-function headcounts for food production guarantees, and event operations teams track per-function check-in for safety compliance and post-event billing. The relationship is actively managed by event staff who create, update, and delete function attendance records throughout the event lifecycle.'
}
